title: VP of Product
description_text:
The Role
Clearstory is looking for a seasoned Vice President of Product to lead product execution and help shape the next phase of our product strategy as we scale a proven financial workflow platform purpose-built for construction.
We have strong product-market fit, growing industry demand, high retention and expansion and billions in change orders flowing through our platform today. This role is about taking something that already works and making it exceptional.
The VP of Product sits at the intersection of Product, Engineering, and the business. You will partner closely with the CEO and executive team, bringing deep customer insight, strong product judgment, and operational rigor to turn company direction into clear product bets and consistently shipped outcomes.
You will own the end-to-end product lifecycle from discovery, prioritization, delivery, and adoption while building and leading a high-performing product organization. Your work will directly shape the only business network purpose-built to manage out-of-contract work in construction, spanning change orders, T&M tickets, allowances, pricing, and approvals, moving billions of dollars through Clearstory every month, and determining how accurately and efficiently companies get paid.
This is a senior leadership role for someone who thrives in fast-paced, scale-up environments. Someone who is comfortable with ambiguity, values strong opinions and healthy debate, and knows how to execute decisively once a direction is set. We are looking for a builder, not someone searching for their first VP role, and not someone who prefers maintaining products at rest.
If you’re excited by complex financial workflows, real customer pain, and the opportunity to define a category, this role will be both demanding and deeply rewarding.
Key Outcomes You Will Own and DriveHelp Shape and Execute a Clear Product Direction
Partner with the CEO and executive team to refine, document, and operationalize Clearstory’s product strategy.
Translate customer, market, and business insights into clear product narratives, product briefs, and decision frameworks that drive alignment and tradeoffs.
Ensure tight alignment across Product, Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success on product vision, priorities, and sequencing—from discovery through release.
Execute with Speed, Quality, and Discipline
Own a rigorous, repeatable product development process that leverages AI-assisted workflows for PRDs, product briefs, discovery synthesis, and roadmap planning.
Drive roadmap development, prioritization, and delivery with clarity, predictability, and measurable outcomes.
Champion rapid prototyping, validation, and iteration using modern tooling to reduce cycle time from idea to customer value.
Partner closely with Engineering leadership to continuously improve discovery quality, execution velocity, and delivery confidence.
Build and Scale a High-Performing Product Organization
Hire, mentor, and develop strong product leaders and ICs who combine strategic thinking with operational excellence.
Establish and own clear product operating rhythms across discovery, planning, execution, launch readiness, and retrospectives.
Raise the bar for product rigor, clarity, and simplicity—ensuring decisions, tradeoffs, and priorities are well-documented and well-understood as the team scales.
Build a product team that is deeply AI-forward in how it works, not just what it builds.
Drive Measurable Business Impact
Ensure product execution directly supports retention, expansion, and competitive differentiation.
Own the product side of software releases, ensuring tight coordination with Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success on positioning, enablement, and rollout.
Equip go-to-market teams with clear product context, narratives, and artifacts to support strategic and competitive deals.
Use data, customer feedback, and strong judgment to continuously refine priorities, investments, and bets.
Must Haves
10+ years of total professional experience.
3–5+ years leading a Product organization.
Experience building fintech, financial workflow, billing, payments, accounting, or revenue‑critical B2B SaaS products.
Proven experience scaling products in fast‑growing SaaS environments.
Strong cross‑functional partnership skills with Engineering, Sales, and Customer Success.
Ability to operate effectively in ambiguity and make high‑quality tradeoffs.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
About You
You are a senior product leader who:
Enjoys close partnership with a CEO and executive team.
Brings strong opinions backed by customer insight and data, and collaborates well through disagreement.
Is execution‑oriented and accountable for outcomes, not just ideas.
Cares deeply about simplicity, trust, and quality in revenue‑critical systems.
Thrives in high‑growth environments with constant change.
You embody Clearstory’s core values:
Be Curious
Customer Obsession
Keep It Simple
Raise the Bar
The Company You’ll Join
Clearstory is a SaaS platform modernizing how construction companies communicate, approve, and track change orders and related cost workflows. We replace paper, spreadsheets, and email with simple, trusted financial workflows that help contractors get paid accurately and on time.
We are a Series B, 100% SaaS company with strong product‑market fit, growing six‑figure deals, and a large, underserved TAM. Our customers love us, our retention is strong, and we are building for long‑term impact.
The Opportunity
As a senior leader at Clearstory, you will have an outsized impact on the product, the team, and the company’s trajectory. You will help scale a product with proven demand while shaping how product work gets done as the company grows.
This is an opportunity to lead execution at a critical moment with meaningful ownership, real influence, and the chance to help define the future of financial workflows in construction.
Benefits
Competitive market‑rate salary for a Series B company
Early equity
Hybrid work schedule (3 days/week minimum in Walnut Creek office)
Subsidized healthcare, vision, and dental
Regular executive and board interaction
Clearstory is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing a positive interview experience for every candidate.

Faster approvals, better forecasting, and fewer late surprises.

title: Lead Software Engineer
description_text:
RouteSmart Technologies, a leader in vehicle route optimization software technology, is seeking a Lead Software Engineer to join our Product Development team. This position reports directly to the Director of Product Development and plays a critical role in both technical leadership and people management, while maintaining hands-on responsibility for code design, implementation, review, and testing.
The Lead Software Engineer will hold product ownership for one or more RouteSmart products and will work closely with internal stakeholders and external clients to ensure product objectives are met through efficient, secure, and scalable software solutions. Our products include web services and desktop applications featuring complex GIS and mapping functionality combined with intuitive, user-friendly design.
The starting salary for this position is anticipated to be in the range of $160,000 to $170,000 per year. At RouteSmart Technologies, Inc., we offer various base pay ranges depending on the work location within the US. Please note that base pay may differ based on your level of experience.
This position is ideally hybrid. However, we are open to remote. We are currently seeking only candidates who are domiciled in the following states: Maryland, New York, Pennsylvania DC Virginia Arizona California Colorado Florida Idaho Illinois Indiana Share this job OVERVIEW APPLICATION Pennsylvania, DC, Virginia, Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Minnesota, New Jersey, North Carolina, Oregon, South Carolina, Texas, Washington. 
Essential Duties & Responsibilities
Lead ownership of product development for one or more RouteSmart products
Plan and manage engineering resources for products within assigned scope
Motivate, mentor, and oversee Software Engineers, providing design oversight and peer code reviews
Serve as a primary technical point of contact for client interaction related to product design and development status
Lead direction and review of both UI and backend code architecture and implementation
Design, write, test, and maintain well-structured, efficient, and secure code following software development best practices
Research and evaluate emerging industry standards and technologies to improve application quality, performance, and security
Ensure desktop and web applications adhere to best practices for security, session management, and scalability
Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned
Provide off-hours support in rare circumstances, as needed
Proven experience guiding and mentoring other programmers in effective and efficient code design 
Expertise in developing mobile applications
5+ years experience leading a team of developers
Strong background in conducting code reviews to improve efficiency, maintainability, and long-term sustainability 
Demonstrated experience planning and delivering shared software platforms used across multiple products or teams 
Expertise in C# development using Visual Studio and other Microsoft development tools 
Strong working knowledge of relational databases (PostgreSQL, MS SQL, etc.) with experience writing efficient queries 
Working knowledge of GIS technologies and mapping services highly preferred 
Strong understanding of web application development, including security best practices 
Strong knowledge in real-time data applications 
Strong knowledge in Docker
Experience in UML modeling highly preferred 
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both verbal and written 
Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to work independently with minimal supervision 
Minimal travel required 
Education & Experience 
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Technology, or a related field required
10+ years of professional programming experience, including both client-side and server-side development
RouteSmart offers its employees numerous opportunities for professional development. Our goal is to provide our employees with the chance to learn and grow as the company grows. We recognize that our employees are our most valuable asset. The work they do is key to our ability to provide high quality service to our clients. We also recognize that our employees are unique individuals with their own lifestyle and interests. RouteSmart Technologies pays a significant share of the cost of benefits for our employees. We believe our investment makes good business sense by helping to create a positive and productive work environment. Among the benefits we offer are:
Medical/Dental/Vision/STD & LTD Plans
Employer paid Life Insurance Plan
Employee Training & Development
401(k) Retirement Savings Plan w/ Employer Match
Vacation/Sick/Holiday Leave
Tuition Reimbursement
Flex hours
Casual Dress
RouteSmart Technologies is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. Individuals seeking employment at RouteSmart Technologies are considered without regards to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or sexual orientation.
We do not accept resumes from headhunters, placement agencies, or other suppliers that have not signed a formal agreement with us.
All candidates must have legal authorization to permanently live and work in the United States.
This position does not qualify for sponsorship.

linkedin_org_description:
RouteSmart Technologies, a FedEx Company, is a global leader in route planning and optimization solutions, focusing on waste collection, public works, postal and local delivery, utilities and field service, and newspaper delivery. 

Leveraging more than  40 years of expertise in operations research and analytics, RouteSmart delivers advanced, efficient route planning tools such as RouteSmart Online, RouteSmart for ArcGIS Pro, RouteSmart Routing as a Service® (RaaS), RouteSourcing, and our in-cab device SmartSuite Mobile & Web. RouteSmart helps clients around the world enhance safety, reduce costs, and improve operational efficiencies. 

RouteSmart is committed to sustainability, providing solutions that optimize routes to reduce fuel consumption and carbon emissions. The company's continual investment in research and development ensures that its technology remains at the cutting edge, incorporating the latest in AI and machine learning to further enhance routing efficiency. The culture at RouteSmart is one of innovation, collaboration, and dedication to customer success. 
RouteSmart is based in Columbia, Maryland, but our clients span the globe with a heavy presence in the Americas, Europe, Asia, and Australia. Our community of clients operates in large cities as well as small towns and rural areas. By reducing operational costs, enhancing safety, and improving efficiency, RouteSmart helps companies not only save money but also increase their service quality and reliability. 

For more information, visit www.RouteSmart.com.

title: Lead Software Engineer
description_text:
RouteSmart Technologies, a leader in vehicle route optimization software technology, is seeking a Lead Software Engineer to join our Product Development team. This position reports directly to the Director of Product Development and plays a critical role in both technical leadership and people management, while maintaining hands-on responsibility for code design, implementation, review, and testing.
The Lead Software Engineer will hold product ownership for one or more RouteSmart products and will work closely with internal stakeholders and external clients to ensure product objectives are met through efficient, secure, and scalable software solutions. Our products include web services and desktop applications featuring complex GIS and mapping functionality combined with intuitive, user-friendly design.
The starting salary for this position is anticipated to be in the range of $160,000 to $170,000 per year. At RouteSmart Technologies, Inc., we offer various base pay ranges depending on the work location within the US. Please note that base pay may differ based on your level of experience.
This position is ideally hybrid. However, we are open to remote. We are currently seeking only candidates who are domiciled in the following states: Maryland, New York, Pennsylvania DC Virginia Arizona California Colorado Florida Idaho Illinois Indiana Share this job OVERVIEW APPLICATION Pennsylvania, DC, Virginia, Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Minnesota, New Jersey, North Carolina, Oregon, South Carolina, Texas, Washington. 
Essential Duties & Responsibilities
Lead ownership of product development for one or more RouteSmart products
Plan and manage engineering resources for products within assigned scope
Motivate, mentor, and oversee Software Engineers, providing design oversight and peer code reviews
Serve as a primary technical point of contact for client interaction related to product design and development status
Lead direction and review of both UI and backend code architecture and implementation
Design, write, test, and maintain well-structured, efficient, and secure code following software development best practices
Research and evaluate emerging industry standards and technologies to improve application quality, performance, and security
Ensure desktop and web applications adhere to best practices for security, session management, and scalability
Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned
Provide off-hours support in rare circumstances, as needed
Proven experience guiding and mentoring other programmers in effective and efficient code design 
Expertise in developing mobile applications
5+ years experience leading a team of developers
Strong background in conducting code reviews to improve efficiency, maintainability, and long-term sustainability 
Demonstrated experience planning and delivering shared software platforms used across multiple products or teams 
Expertise in C# development using Visual Studio and other Microsoft development tools 
Strong working knowledge of relational databases (PostgreSQL, MS SQL, etc.) with experience writing efficient queries 
Working knowledge of GIS technologies and mapping services highly preferred 
Strong understanding of web application development, including security best practices 
Strong knowledge in real-time data applications 
Strong knowledge in Docker
Experience in UML modeling highly preferred 
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both verbal and written 
Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to work independently with minimal supervision 
Minimal travel required 
Education & Experience 
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Technology, or a related field required
10+ years of professional programming experience, including both client-side and server-side development
RouteSmart offers its employees numerous opportunities for professional development. Our goal is to provide our employees with the chance to learn and grow as the company grows. We recognize that our employees are our most valuable asset. The work they do is key to our ability to provide high quality service to our clients. We also recognize that our employees are unique individuals with their own lifestyle and interests. RouteSmart Technologies pays a significant share of the cost of benefits for our employees. We believe our investment makes good business sense by helping to create a positive and productive work environment. Among the benefits we offer are:
Medical/Dental/Vision/STD & LTD Plans
Employer paid Life Insurance Plan
Employee Training & Development
401(k) Retirement Savings Plan w/ Employer Match
Vacation/Sick/Holiday Leave
Tuition Reimbursement
Flex hours
Casual Dress
RouteSmart Technologies is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. Individuals seeking employment at RouteSmart Technologies are considered without regards to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or sexual orientation.
We do not accept resumes from headhunters, placement agencies, or other suppliers that have not signed a formal agreement with us.
All candidates must have legal authorization to permanently live and work in the United States.
This position does not qualify for sponsorship.

title: Credentialing Specialist
description_text:
Under general supervision, the credentialing specialist is responsible for assuring the proper credentialing and privileging of physicians and mid-level providers. This position is responsible for the initial credentialing, recredentialing and ongoing monitoring of sanctions and exclusions process for practitioners. This position is also responsible for meeting daily/weekly production goals and maintaining high level of confidentiality for provider information. 
Responsibilities
· Guide provider through credentialing and re-credentialing process and ensure the completion of required documentation for IndieMD TX
· Works with provider to prepare and submit Medicare & Medicaid applications to enrollment office.
· Prepare all credentialing applications, develop, and provided documentation that are not supplied by the provider, address any additional information or corrections requested. 
· Verify and track the proper credentialing and re-credentialing of Physicians and mid-level providers within IndieMD TX.
· Receive credentialing application and enter data into credentialing system
· Work with Provider to maintain and ensure compliance for all licensures and appointments with state and governmental agencies and certifications for each provider, generate monthly report of expiring credentials.
· Ensure ongoing monitoring of performance between credentialing cycles, generate performance review documentation, share with department for completion, and notify external entities when a provider has been suspended, if necessary.
· Notify providers and departments of credentialing status and result, email notice to department and provider, ensure credentialing system is updated with final credentialing result. 
· Track and ensure the completion of required continuing medical education courses/workshops; provide documentation to outside entities according to requirements. 
· Perform all other duties as assigned.
· Three (3) years administrative experience, preferably in the hospital or clinical field, is required. 
· Ability to effectively serve the customer’s need strong attention to detail and ensuring accuracy.
· Ability to work independently under limited supervision
· Strong multitasking and organizational skills
· Exhibits accuracy and thoroughness; monitors own work for quality
· Strong organizational and time management skills
· Proficiency in relevant software such as HSTREAM, PECOS, Availity, Cigna, UHC, and other payer portals.
· Strong verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills
Why this job is AWESOME:
Join us and you will see…we don’t do things like everyone else. We are the newest and fastest growing in popularity for mobile healthcare servicing long-term care facilities. It’s no secret how we're accomplishing this… it’s our incredible team members and the winning culture we’re building!
If you are looking for an amazing practice to work for, grow, learn, and have a blast - this is it.
Great team! We spend a ton of time hiring and training the best people.
Great pay! We pay very well.
Great opportunities! We're growing and we like to promote from within. If you're a true rock star, the opportunities are limitless!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Credentialing Specialist is responsible for the credentialing and privileging of physicians and mid-level providers, including initial credentialing, recredentialing, and ongoing monitoring. They guide providers through the credentialing process, ensure compliance with licensures, and maintain confidentiality of provider information.

title: CSA Engineer
description_text:
Winthrop Technologies is a dedicated data centre delivery partner providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe.
Winthrop specialises in providing turnkey data centre solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning.
Winthrop has grown to become the leading and most trusted company in our sector and is the only dedicated Data Centre delivery partner in Europe.
We are currently constructing multiple turnkey data centre projects in 7 different European countries, equating to over 430 MW of IT load in flight.
We are pleased to announce that we are currently seeking a CSA Engineer to join our team. 
Checking works carried out and ensure quality control in maintained throughout 
Work part of the Quality team to ensure finished works meet standards required, managing quality of the works set against approved benchmarks, actively coordinate with the QA managers to ensure the correct documentation is submitted in a timely manner by the contractors.
Liaise with sub-contractor’s on tracking progress against schedule and Carry out sub-contractor meetings.
Manage site documentation.
Attend and actively participate in Site Meetings, Internal Project Reviews, Engineering Reviews, Planning, Co-ordination and lookahead meetings, as well as Handover Meetings. 
Review drawings with design team and effectively solve any issues. 
Actively monitor and snag contractors works ensuring works is installed as per the approved drawings and spec ensuring the highest quality is achieved to ensure zero defects, snag list to be raised on the approved platform 
Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or similar is essential
Ability to work on own initiative 
3+ years’ experience working on large scale projects 
Experience in facades and fit-out projects 
Previous data centre experience is advantageous 
Winthrop Technologies are an equal opportunities employer
ai_core_responsibilities: The CSA Engineer will check works carried out and ensure quality control is maintained throughout. They will manage site documentation and actively participate in various project meetings.
ai_requirements_summary: A Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or a similar field is essential, along with 3+ years of experience on large scale projects. Previous data centre experience and experience in facades and fit-out projects are advantageous.

title: Deputy Store Manager
description_text:
Join Our Team as Deputy Manager - ProCook at Our Brighton Store!
Are you ready to inspire a team and deliver exceptional customer experiences with the UK’s fastest-growing kitchenware brand? ProCook is looking for a dynamic Deputy Manager to join our new store set to open in April in Churchills Square in Brighton!
This is a permanent, full-time position (40 hours per week), working 5 days out of 7, Monday to Sunday, on a rota basis. As part of your onboarding, you’ll complete mandatory training including two days at our Head Office in Gloucester and four to eight weeks at one of our training stores. The timing and order of this training may vary.

Key Responsibilities:
Support the Store Manager in daily operations and team leadership.
Engage with customers to provide personalised product guidance and outstanding service.
Assist in training team members on products, sales techniques, and customer engagement.
Maintain high standards of store presentation, cleanliness, and inventory management.
Drive sales through innovative merchandising and customer service strategies.
What You Will Do Daily:
Start each day with a team briefing to set goals and tasks.
Monitor customer service standards and take action to enhance customer satisfaction.
Ensure compliance with company policies and operational procedures.
Conduct stock checks and manage inventory efficiently.
Assist the Store Manager in achieving sales targets and KPIs.
Who You Are:
Enthusiastic about providing top-notch customer service.
Experienced in retail management or supervisory roles.
A strong leader who can motivate and inspire a team.
Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written.
Organised and detail-oriented with a problem-solving mindset.
Comfortable using retail management systems and technology.
Our Commitment to You — And Your Commitment to ProCook
We’re proud of our strong values, supportive culture, and the benefits we offer our colleagues. At ProCook, we invest in your development and success and in return, we’re looking for individuals who will bring energy, reliability, and a genuine commitment to delivering exceptional service every day. If you care about quality, teamwork, and growing with a dynamic brand, you’ll fit right in.
Why You’ll Love Working at ProCook:
Salary is up to £29,000/year depending on experience.
A delightful welcome bag awaits you, filled with our fantastic products for you to explore.
Quarterly Bonus based on sales targets.
Be part of a fast-growing company.
Join a friendly, close-knit team.
Enhanced Maternity, Paternity and Adoption Leave.
Generous colleague discount - 40% + 5 friends and family discount cards of 30%.
2 x Annual paid volunteering days.
Employee Assistance Programme.
Friends and Family Referral Scheme
6.6 weeks per holiday year, including Bank Holidays + Earn more holidays as you grow with us.
Learning & Development opportunities.
Bike to Work scheme.
We Welcome Everyone: 

ProCook is an equal-opportunity employer. We believe diverse teams are the best teams, so we encourage applications from all backgrounds.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you through the application or interview process, please let us know. We’re committed to making our recruitment process as inclusive and accessible as possible.
About ProCook’s Sustainability Commitment:

At ProCook, sustainability is at the heart of what we do. 
As a certified B Corporation, we strive to balance profit with purpose, focusing on reducing waste, and ensuring ethical business practices. 
By joining our team, you will contribute to our efforts in creating a positive impact on the planet.

At ProCook, we’re more than just a kitchenware brand, we’re a people-first community driven by a love of great cooking and doing things the right way. title: Lead Stores Operator (Night Shift)
description_text:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is what happens when history, purpose and daring talent come together. Backed by TWG Global and GM, our team is uniquely positioned to disrupt Formula 1®, bringing a fresh perspective and an unrelenting drive for success. We have the energy of a start-up, with the ideas and originality of a business that always wants to lead, never wants to follow. 
We’re building everything from the ground up, from a high-performance car to an inclusive, values-driven culture. We show bold ambition. We combine leadership in innovation with excellence in execution. We are one team. We have the freedom to think differently, the opportunity to shape process and practice, an ego-free environment where people thrive on being challenged by those around them. A historic name behind us. Career-defining moments ahead. 
A New Chapter Begins.
Please Note: This is a Night Shift position working 4 days x 10 hours rotated between Monday – Thursday and Tuesday – Friday from 6pm – 4:30am.
Fueled by bold ambition:
Play your part in getting us on the grid.
Closing Date: 13th February 2026
We have an exciting opportunity for a Lead Stores Operator to join The Cadillac Formula 1® Team at the new Silverstone Facility. In this role, you will be responsible for managing inventory, processing stock movements, and ensuring that materials are available to support the team's operational needs. This role will also require you to drive a van as part of daily operations. At The Cadillac Formula 1® Team, we aspire to be the absolute best in every aspect of our team. Team members are encouraged to challenge established thinking and share their ideas to create an environment where everyone can contribute to our shared success.
· Receive, inspect, and store incoming materials and components
· Process stock movements and maintain accurate inventory records
· Assist in the preparation and dispatch of materials as required
· Ensure compliance with health and safety regulations
· Maintain a tidy and organized storage area
· Collaborate with colleagues and other departments to fulfil material requests
· Participate in regular stock audits and reconciliations
· Utilize ERP systems for tracking inventory and orders
· Report any discrepancies or issues with inventory to management promptly
· Delivery and collection of parts by vehicle to internal and external destinations
Driven by high performance:
What do you need to bring to the team?
· A full, clean UK driving license is required for this role
· Must be comfortable driving a van
· Previous experience in a stores or warehouse environment
· Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
· Ability to operate warehouse equipment (e.g., forklifts, pallet jacks)
· Basic computer skills, including experience with ERP systems
· Effective communication skills, both verbal and written
· Ability to work effectively in a team and adapt to a fast-paced environment
· Understanding of health and safety regulations associated with warehouse operations
· Flexible and proactive attitude towards work
· Positively contribute to an open and inclusive culture
A team like no other.
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team challenges conventions and redefines success through bold ambition, cutting-edge innovation, and an unwavering commitment to precision and excellence—on and off the track. This includes offering industry-leading pension, generous time off and, as part of a global brand, huge potential for career development. 

Please be aware that we will be reviewing applicants on a rolling basis and this job posting will close once a suitable candidate is identified. We encourage all interested individuals to submit their application as soon as possible. 
As an equal opportunities employer, we are committed to the equal treatment of all current and prospective employees and does not condone discrimination on the basis of age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, race or ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity or marriage and civil partnership. We aspire to have a diverse and inclusive workplace and strongly encourage suitably qualified applicants from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.
At The Cadillac Formula 1®, all Team Members are expected to actively support and uphold our policies and procedures, including those focused on Environmental responsibility, Sustainability initiatives, Inclusion and Health and Safety practices.
Please note that additional security checks may be required as part of the recruitment process. This may include a background check covering a minimum of the past five years and a criminal record check.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Lead Stores Operator will manage inventory, process stock movements, and ensure materials are available for operational needs. This includes receiving and inspecting materials, maintaining inventory records, and collaborating with colleagues.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a full, clean UK driving license and previous experience in a stores or warehouse environment. Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to operate warehouse equipment are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: The Original American F1® Team
linkedin_org_description:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is The American Original—bringing a bold, American-led vision to the future of Formula 1™. Set to join the grid in 2026, the team is built from the ground up and designed for the global stage—driven by innovation, shaped for culture and engineered to deliver long-term brand affinity.
 
With operational hubs in Fishers (Indiana), Silverstone (UK) & Cologne (GER), we're creating a platform that blends world-class racing performance with commercial and cultural relevance. 
 
Powered by the strategic and commercial leadership of TWG Motorsports and the technical excellence of General Motors, the team combines deep motorsport expertise, cross-platform capability and a legacy of American engineering. At its heart is Cadillac—a brand defined by bold ambition, design innovation and modern American luxury.
 
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team reflects the ambition, optimism and ingenuity that define the American spirit—balanced with global sensibility and deep respect for the sport’s legacy.

title: Mortgage Servicing System Support
description_text:
FICS is thrilled to continue expanding its customer base and is on the hunt for a dedicated individual to join our mortgage loan servicing software system support team. We are looking for a self-driven team member with at least three years of hands-on experience in residential loan servicing within the mortgage industry. A solid understanding of mortgage loan servicing is a must, particularly knowledge in escrow administration, ARM analysis, payment processing, and IRS reporting.
As a System Support team member at FICS, you will serve as the primary point of contact for providing high-quality support related to our mortgage loan servicing software. Your role is crucial in helping clients disentangle the mortgage process. Each interaction significantly impacts the efficiency of our customers’ operations and their success. In this fast-paced environment, you will interact with customer through phone calls and emails, conduct training sessions on FICS software, and facilitate product webinars. Moreover, you will be actively involved in collaboratively testing the FICS software for quality assurance.
What you bring: 
We are looking for candidates with 3-5 years of experience in the residential mortgage loan servicing sector.
Hands-on experience with reporting to investors, such as Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and/or Ginnie Mae is highly desirable.
A strong grasp of mortgage loan servicing is essential. Familiarity with escrow administration, ARM analysis, payment processing, investor reporting, and/or IRS reporting would be advantageous.
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills are essential.
We also value excellent interpersonal and presentation skills, as well as a genuine eagerness to learn.
Willingness to travel across the U.S., when needed.
At FICS, our customers are our top priority, and we've proudly established a reputation for delivering substantial competitive advantages to our nationwide clientele for over 40 years and still going strong.
Curious about what it's like to be part of the FICS family? Discover more at: www.FICS.com/about-us/life-at-fics. Check out some of the amazing benefits and perks we offer at www.FICS.com/about-us/employment.
Don’t miss out—apply today to become a member of the FICS Mortgage Servicer System Support team!
ai_core_responsibilities: As a System Support team member, you will provide high-quality support related to mortgage loan servicing software and help clients navigate the mortgage process. You will also conduct training sessions and facilitate product webinars.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3-5 years of experience in residential mortgage loan servicing and a solid understanding of mortgage loan servicing processes. Familiarity with investor reporting and outstanding communication skills are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Your Mortgage Software Partner Since 1983
linkedin_org_locations: 14285 Midway Road, Suite 200, Addison, TX 75001, US
linkedin_org_description:
FICS® is a leading mortgage loan origination software, residential servicing software, and commercial loan servicing software provider. FICS offers the most cost-effective, efficient, flexible software solutions on the market today. FICS® has delivered exceptional automation, performance, system support and value for more than four decades. 

Mortgage Servicer® automates mortgage servicing operations, including payment processing, investor reporting, custodial accounting, imaging, report writing and more. Commercial Servicer® automates and provides seamless data flow for virtually every facet of commercial loan servicing for diverse types of commercial loan products. Loan Producer® automates the loan origination process from point of sale to closing, funding and secondary marketing, a complete end-to-end loan origination system.

With FICS’ solutions, you'll save money through a low initial investment, nominal monthly support, and maintenance fee, paperless servicing or origination, real time processing, and enhanced productivity. FICS’ software systems are flexible, meeting the specific needs of small and large companies. When you call FICS, you'll speak with our receptionist who will direct you to the appropriate support person. You won't lose valuable time waiting for someone to return your call or answer your email. We strive to exceed our customers’ expectations.

Visit www.fics.com for more information about our exceptional software solutions.

title: Lead Data & Marketing Solutions Architect
description_text:
The Company
At Servant, we specialize in empowering leaders and organizations within faith communities through innovative digital transformation and change management solutions. We are dedicated to providing tailored strategies that align with our clients' values and missions, focusing on service, integrity, and excellence. Our team is passionate about driving impactful change by equipping our partners with the tools and technologies they need to thrive in a digital age.
Position Overview
At Servant, we empower leaders and organizations within faith communities through mission-driven digital transformation. We are seeking a Lead Data & Marketing Solutions Architect to serve as the senior technical solution owner and strategic partner for a high-impact nonprofit client.
This is a client-facing, strategy-forward role—not a traditional engineering management position. You will act as the Integration Orchestrator for a complex ecosystem of data, analytics, marketing, and fundraising platforms, ensuring systems work together seamlessly to support mission-critical outcomes. Partnering closely with our Client Account Manager, you will guide senior stakeholders through high-stakes architectural and data decisions with broad organizational impact.
You will lead a skilled team of Data Engineers and Analysts, overseeing day-to-day execution while maintaining ownership of the overall solution architecture. While you won’t write ETL pipelines day-to-day, you will have the authority to evaluate, challenge, and refine architectural decisions to ensure scalability, accuracy, and long-term value.
We are seeking a Data Product Leader, not a pure infrastructure engineer. Your core “superpower” is Analytics & Reporting Integrity—ensuring that data captured across digital experiences flows accurately into the warehouse and downstream systems, enabling trustworthy reporting, insight generation, and informed decision-making that directly supports the organization’s mission.
Key Responsibilities
Strategic Architecture & Value Engineering
Pragmatic Roadmapping: Partner with the Client Account Manager to map out technical scenarios, framing engineering decisions in terms of cost and business value (ROI).
Broker Technical Knowledge: Understand the client’s digital landscape and advise on high-impact changes, pushing vendors past “wheel-spinning” when issues arise.
Technical Authority: Serve as the primary technical decision-maker, translating complex business goals into executable technical roadmaps.
Data Product & Analytics Integrity (Hands-On Focus)
Tracking Strategy: Define and own tracking plans and conversion logic; implement standard JavaScript tracking SDKs to capture high-fidelity data at the source.
Reporting Trust: Ensure reporting accuracy by auditing pipelines combining donor, CRM, marketing, and mobile data; verify that Executive Dashboard numbers reflect reality.
Schema & Flow: Design logical data flows between web, mobile, and Snowflake warehouse, ensuring distinct systems share a common “data language.”
Ecosystem Orchestration & Vendor Management
Integration Architecture: Lead third-party platform integrations (Virtuous, FundraiseUp, Webflow, Rudderstack, Snowflake). Define clean integration points and ensure adherence to standards.
Marketing Technology Support: Provide technical recommendations for marketing initiatives, including UTM governance, A/B testing frameworks, and internationalization support for web and mobile.
Team Leadership
Mentorship: Provide architectural oversight to a team including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, and Analysts.
Stakeholder Bridge: Collaborate with Directors of IT, Data, and Marketing, leveraging emotional intelligence to navigate tension and drive consensus.
8–10+ years in technical leadership with experience in Solution Architecture, Data Engineering, or Technical Product Management.
Consulting experience preferred; ability to manage “up and out” to clients and “down and in” to delivery teams.
Alignment with Servant’s mission: a desire to work with faith-based nonprofits to enable digital transformation.
Mastery (Hands-On):
Analytics & Tracking: Tracking plans, conversion logic, GA4, Rudderstack, or similar CDPs.
Data Integrity: Auditing and querying data to verify reporting accuracy.
Fluency (Architectural Oversight):
Data Warehousing: Snowflake, dbt, ETL/ELT patterns; ability to review Data Engineer’s architecture and API integrations.
Marketing Platforms: CRM data schemas (HubSpot, Virtuous) and warehouse integration.
Generalist Knowledge:
Web & Mobile: Familiarity with modern stacks to manage QA and release readiness of integrated systems.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Lead Data & Marketing Solutions Architect will act as the senior technical solution owner, guiding stakeholders through architectural and data decisions. They will lead a team of Data Engineers and Analysts while ensuring the integrity of data and analytics across various platforms.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 8-10+ years of experience in technical leadership, particularly in Solution Architecture or Data Engineering. A strong alignment with the mission of faith-based nonprofits and experience in consulting is preferred.

title: Personal Injury Trial Attorney
description_text:
Please Note- This is for candidates licensed in CA and local to Irvine, CA. 
ALL APPLICANTS AND APPLICATIONS WILL REMAIN CONFIDENTIAL - APPLY WITH CONFIDENCE
At TORKLAW, we are dedicated to providing exceptional legal representation to individuals who have suffered injuries due to the negligence of others. Our team is composed of experienced and compassionate professionals who are committed to fighting for the rights and best interests of our clients. We are currently looking for a Litigation Attorney to join our dynamic team and contribute to our mission of delivering justice and fair compensation to those we serve.
As a Trial Attorney at TORKLAW, you will play a crucial role in managing a diverse caseload of personal injury claims. You will work closely with other attorneys to develop case strategies, conduct legal research, and lead case management teams in the effective representation of our clients. 
Key Responsibilities
The Trial attorney will be responsible from inception to settlement for the litigation cases . Your duties will include, but are by no means limited to:
Manage a heavy caseload and all phases of personal injury cases.
Conduct thorough case investigations to determine liability and damages.
Draft and file legal documents needed in litigation, discovery disputes, law and motion and opposing motions for summary judgment (MSJ opps)
Negotiate settlements with insurance companies and opposing counsel.
Provide exceptional client service, keeping clients informed about the progress of their cases.
First Chair Civil Jury Trial

About TORKLAW:
TORKLAW is an incredibly fast-moving, innovative law firm that is all about delivering an exceptional client experience and high-quality legal representation to each and every one of our clients. 
We’ve been entrusted with an awesome responsibility, and we respond with hard work, discipline, and laser focus. Along the way, we’ve earned recognition for our growth and the work we do including features in CNN, Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, Daily Journal & The Advocate.
If you would like to be a part of a downright badass team like this, then we can’t wait to hear from you! 
As a values-based firm. We believe in:
Radical Authenticity - being transparently who we are: with ourselves, with each other, and with our clients & partners.
Relentless Pursuit of the Win - achieving stellar results keeping a laser focus on performance and goals.
Growth Mindset – continuously learning, growing and developing as individuals, as a business, and as advocates for our clients.
Ownership – we take responsibility for our work and actions.
Results Driven - we focus on the outcome and disregard the level of effort required to achieve those results.
Respect for Each Other – supporting each other with kindness and respect, and enjoying the journey together.
Unwavering Integrity – standing up for what’s right with consistently sound ethics and courageous honesty.
Lawyer in Good Standing to practice law in state
7+ years of personal injury experience with a focus in Trial.
Min. 10+ Civil Jury Trials to Verdict
Excellent knowledge and understanding of state laws, regulations, and legal procedures pertaining to personal injury cases
Strong research and analytical skills with the ability to effectively interpret and apply legal precedents, statutes, and regulations
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to draft and review legal documents, contracts, and correspondence
Proven track record of successful case management and client representation, with a focus on achieving positive outcomes
Experience in conducting legal research, preparing legal briefs, and providing sound legal advice to clients
Ability to effectively collaborate and work as part of a team, including coordinating with paralegals, support staff, and other attorneys
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage multiple cases and deadlines simultaneously
Demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion in handling sensitive and confidential information
Bilingual (English/Spanish) a plus but not required
In addition to a competitive salary, this position will receive the following benefits:
12 paid holidays annually
10 days of paid vacation annually
6 days of sick leave annually
Medical insurance 
401(k) with 4% fully vested safe-harbor company match
Training and continuing education budget
Regular firm events (happy hours, team building, holiday party, etc)
Laptops are replaced every 3 years. After 3 years, your work laptop will become your personal laptop.
TORKLAW is a multi-state employer, as such, any salary range provided may not be applicable in all states. Any offer made to a successful candidate will be dependent on several factors that may include years of experience, education, location, etc.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Trial Attorney will manage a diverse caseload of personal injury claims from inception to settlement. Responsibilities include conducting case investigations, drafting legal documents, negotiating settlements, and providing exceptional client service.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be licensed to practice law in California and have at least 7 years of personal injury experience with a focus on trial work. A minimum of 10 civil jury trials to verdict is required, along with strong legal knowledge and communication skills.

linkedin_org_description:
TORKLAW is a national personal injury law firm dedicated to fighting for accident victims and their families. Our team of personal injury attorneys helps people who have been seriously injured in car accidents, motorcycle accidents, truck accidents, bicycle accidents, pedestrian accidents, slip and fall cases, workplace injuries, defective products, wrongful death claims, and more.

With a proven record of success, TORKLAW has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for clients across the United States. Our mission is simple: to deliver justice, accountability, and full compensation for those harmed by negligence.

At TORKLAW, every case is personal. We combine compassionate client care with relentless legal advocacy, guiding clients through each step of the legal process while fighting to maximize results.

If you or someone you love has been injured, our attorneys are here to help. Connect with us today to learn more about your rights and how TORKLAW can stand by your side in the pursuit of justice.

title: Product Designer - SaaS (Mid-Level)
description_text:
Product Designer - SaaS 
We seek a passionate and collaborative Mid-Level Product Designer to join our SaaS design team. Treatwell’s SaaS team focuses on the business tools salons used to manage their business through our iOS and Android apps.
In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to solve problems through design while thinking holistically across Treatwell’s user journeys and platforms. You’ll contribute to the entire design process, from research and ideation to prototyping and implementation. You’ll work collaboratively with the SaaS design lead, product managers, and engineers in an internationally distributed team. 
Most importantly, we seek someone who is a self-starter, organised, ambitious and enthusiastic about developing their skills further. 
You will be responsible for: 
Collaborating with other designers, user researchers, product managers, and engineers to plan, design, build and evolve our products.
Influencing strategy for a product area by articulating a coherent UX vision based on clear, actionable problem / opportunity statements.
Advocating for the user and crafting UX solutions that meet critical business objectives.
Working on a range of projects on different platforms.
Helping facilitate idea generation with stakeholder.
Designing with scalability in mind and actively contributing to our design system.
Creating simple, intuitive experiences in a space with complex rules and workflows.
Consistently creating clear and compelling materials and narratives explaining design rationale to team and stakeholders using customer, business or competitive insights.
Actively seeking diverse feedback on your work and anticipating feedback to address it proactively.
About you: 
Detail-oriented and passionate about the craft.
4+ years as a Product or UX Designer (or equivalent experience) with robust case studies that showcase deep interaction and problem-solving skills.
Experience creating and using design systems.
Experience working in a start-up or scale-up environment.
Strong communication (both written and verbal) skills.
Excellent understanding of design principles.
An ego-free attitude that welcomes candid feedback.
A willingness to fail and learn and to go beyond good enough.
Bonus if you can speak Dutch or German.
About Treatwell
Hi, we’re Treatwell. Nice to meet you.
We’re a team of 700 passionate and brilliantly talented people across Europe. We’re the industry’s smartest salon management software, all connected to Europe’s leading bookings platform, working with over 50,000 salon partners across 13 countries, processing over 8 million bookings every month. And….breathe.
But we’re not done yet. We’re on an exciting journey to digitally revolutionise the hair and beauty community.
If this excites you, we might just have the role for you.
We'll treat you with
A challenging role at one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe, with a fun company culture in the heart of Amsterdam.
28 vacation days, plus national holidays
A €40 voucher each month via our benefits platform Alleo – so you can treat yourself, too
Mental health support through our partnership with OpenUp
The possibility to use the Lease a Bike scheme after 7 months of employment
Participation in the pension plan with Bright Pension after second contract of employment
A delicious freshly prepared office lunch each day, available for a small contribution
One extra day off per year – the Treat Yourself Well Day – to recharge and take time for yourself, in your own way
Equal Ops
We are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to their condition.

ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 4+ years of experience in Product or UX Design with strong problem-solving skills and experience in creating design systems. Excellent communication skills and a willingness to learn and accept feedback are essential.

linkedin_org_description:
At Treatwell, we empower industry growth to bring more beauty and wellness to everyone, everywhere. As Europe’s No.1 beauty and wellness booking platform, we connect millions of people with salons and professionals across 13 countries. With the industry’s smartest salon management software at our core, we help beauty and wellness professionals grow their business while making treatments more accessible than ever.

Our vision is simple: to create a world where everyone can treat themselves well. That means giving salons the tools, visibility, and support to thrive, while making it effortless for customers to book the treatments they love. Anytime, anywhere.

Behind it all is a team of passionate, ambitious people united by our values: Treat everyone well, Think like a salon owner, Make every appointment count, and Play to win together. These values shape how we work, drive our growth, and keep us focused on building a company that truly makes a difference for our people, partners, and industry.

Ready to be part of the journey? Explore our careers page, your next chapter could start here.

title: 5th Shift Distillery Operator - (Friday, Saturday, Sunday 7:00PM - 7:00AM)
description_text:
Primary Job Responsibilities
1. Master all production operations to a level of independent comfort. Facilitate all distilling operations including but not limited to receiving grain, milling grain, cooking, mashing, distilling, operating boiler, transfer stillage and filling barrels. 
2. Follow all protocols for proper equipment use, maintenance and safety. Immediately notify the direct supervisor of observations, difficulties, errors or maintenance needs. Conduct initial equipment troubleshooting according to protocols. 
3. Share expertise with teammates and seek expertise and learning from teammates. Proactively ask questions and seek needed information.
4. Work with the team to ensure all Batch Sheets and other record keeping documents are accurate and reported in a timely manner. 
5. Support grain, mash and beer testing and chemistry record keeping as well as completion of other necessary Quality Control record keeping. 
6. Ensure all work areas are maintained with good housekeeping in accordance with OSHA and company safety regulations. Follow all cleaning protocols to ensure proper equipment maintenance, safety of employees and a hospitable environment for guests. 
7. Maintain working knowledge of automated and manual process controls and computer screens. 
8. Follow the production schedule to ensure that all elements of the distilling process are running smoothly per the assigned schedule. 
9. Comply with all necessary Standard Operating Procedures (SOP's), Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP’s), and Safety Protocols. Comply with all applicable federal, state and local laws. 
10. Support barrel team with tank proof, filling and rolling if needed. 
Other duties will be assigned at the discretion of the Master Distiller. Operators report directly to the Distillery Operator Lead on the shift with additional support provided by the Distillery Operations Director. 
Qualifications
Must be 21 or older to apply. High School diploma required. 
One year of relevant experience in one or more of the following areas: boiler maintenance, brewing, distilling, fermentation, food and beverage production or chemical manufacturing. 
Must be comfortable communicating and collaborating with a team. Must be comfortable with multitasking and taking initiative. Ideal candidates would be enthusiastic about problem solving and enjoy repetitive daily tasks. Working knowledge of Microsoft Windows, Excel and Word or Pages preferred.
Must be self motivated, comfortable working independently and able to follow instruction with attention to detail. Must demonstrate an unwavering positive attitude, with a strong commitment to honesty, ethical judgment, diligence and teamwork. 
All staff members are required to respect confidentiality and comply with all safety policies.
All candidates are subject to drug screening and background checks. 
Full-time position
Work Schedule - Friday, Saturday, & Sunday - 7:00PM - 7:00AM
Health insuranceVision insurance Life insuranceDental program optionPaid Time Off 
Health Insurance and PTO go into effect after the 90 day employment probation period. Life Insurance enrollment is in June.
Merchandise discounts take effect immediately.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Distillery Operator will master all production operations and facilitate distilling operations while ensuring safety and maintenance protocols are followed. They will also support record keeping and quality control processes.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be at least 21 years old and have a high school diploma, along with one year of relevant experience in related fields. Strong communication, teamwork, and problem-solving skills are essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Welcome to the family. Welcome to Willett.
linkedin_org_locations: 1869 LORETTO RD, Bardstown, Kentucky 40004, us
linkedin_org_description:
On St. Patrick’s Day in 1937, Thompson Willett, a third-generation whiskey maker, stored the first batch of Kentucky Bourbon Whiskey made at Willett Distilling Company. The distillery was built on the family’s farm a year earlier, set atop the highest point in Nelson County.
 
Fast forward nearly 90 years, and Willett has a choice Kentucky Whiskey to be shared among friends for celebrations intimate to epic. Willett Distillery still sits on the same land and is owned and operated by descendants of Thompson Willett.

Willett Distillery feels a lot like it did when we opened nearly 90 years ago – and we like it that way. We’re a cozy place set atop rolling hills and staffed by family and friends that we have known for decades. 
 
The Bardstown air that blows through the rickhouses and over the land is filled with a rich history, a vision for future generations – and a deep appreciation for the present, keeping Willett a family operation by choice.

title: WFH Sales Rep (Up to 78K + 13th Month)
description_text:
We are looking for a candidate with phone experience in sales, who is fluent in both spoken and written English. This is a remote job position with a call center feel, so if you are dependable with an excellent work ethic, and a distraction-free workspace, you can work from the comfort of your own home. 
This is a fixed-term employment contract from February to September. The role may be extended or converted to regular employment based on performance and business needs. You’ll undergo structured sales training and receive continuous coaching throughout the ramp-up period.
What You’ll Do
Manage inbound and outbound warm calls (approx. 200+ weekly).
Handle calls lasting 6–15 minutes to close new customers, explain LawnStarter’s services, and upsell recurring plans.
Meet and exceed sales metrics and quality standards.
Demonstrate exceptional communication skills with U.S. customers.
Maintain accurate CRM records and uphold data protection policies.
Work Schedule 
Operations run Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM–12:00 AM CST, and Saturday–Sunday, 8:00 AM–10:00 PM CST. 
You will render a minimum of forty (40) hours of work per week or as may be required by the operational schedule.
You may be assigned to a rotating schedule within the company’s hours of operation.
Shift adjustments may be made based on operational requirements; employees will receive 1–2 weeks’ advance notice when changes occur.
The position may operate under a Compressed Workweek (CWW) or rotating schedule as allowed under DOLE-compliant arrangements.
Standard hours may exceed eight (8) hours per day when placed under an approved CWW, and such hours will not be classified as overtime.
Overtime, holiday work, and rest-day work shall be compensated in accordance with Philippine Labor Code Articles 87–90.
What We’re Looking For
Proven ability to close sales and overcome objections
Strong customer service and empathy skills
Goal-oriented with strong adaptability
Coachable and receptive to feedback
Consistent achievement of performance targets
Qualifications
High school graduate; college preferred
Excellent written and spoken English
At least 2 years of telesales or BPO experience
Familiarity with CRM/VoIP tools (Google Suite preferred)
Desktop/laptop with camera (Core i5, 8GB RAM minimum), dual monitors, noise-canceling headset
25 Mbps connection with 10 Mbps backup
Quiet, dedicated workspace (PLDT/Converge preferred)

Base Pay: Php 41,360.00/month
Performance Incentives: Php 9,000–37,000/month
Total Potential Pay: Php 50,360–78,360/month
Statutory benefits(SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG, 13th month pay, taxes)
13th month pay and night differential as mandated by DOLE
Work from the comfort of your own home
ai_core_responsibilities: Manage inbound and outbound warm calls to close new customers and upsell services. Maintain accurate CRM records and meet sales metrics and quality standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least 2 years of telesales or BPO experience and be fluent in English. A high school diploma is required, with a preference for college education.

linkedin_org_description:
LawnStarter is a marketplace for outdoor home services. We offer the simplest and most effective solution for ordering and managing outdoor home services, with operations in more than 3,000 cities across the United States. With incredibly high ratings from consumers and providers, it is easy to see why we have industry-leading retention on both sides of the marketplace and are poised to build a multi-billion-dollar consumer brand.

We have successfully raised over $35M from some of the leading technology and growth investors.

title: WFH Sales Rep (Up to 78K + 13th Month)
description_text:
We are looking for a candidate with phone experience in sales, who is fluent in both spoken and written English. This is a remote job position with a call center feel, so if you are dependable with an excellent work ethic, and a distraction-free workspace, you can work from the comfort of your own home. 
This is a fixed-term employment contract from February to September. The role may be extended or converted to regular employment based on performance and business needs. You’ll undergo structured sales training and receive continuous coaching throughout the ramp-up period.
What You’ll Do
Manage inbound and outbound warm calls (approx. 200+ weekly).
Handle calls lasting 6–15 minutes to close new customers, explain LawnStarter’s services, and upsell recurring plans.
Meet and exceed sales metrics and quality standards.
Demonstrate exceptional communication skills with U.S. customers.
Maintain accurate CRM records and uphold data protection policies.
Work Schedule 
Operations run Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM–12:00 AM CST, and Saturday–Sunday, 8:00 AM–10:00 PM CST. 
You will render a minimum of forty (40) hours of work per week or as may be required by the operational schedule.
You may be assigned to a rotating schedule within the company’s hours of operation.
Shift adjustments may be made based on operational requirements; employees will receive 1–2 weeks’ advance notice when changes occur.
The position may operate under a Compressed Workweek (CWW) or rotating schedule as allowed under DOLE-compliant arrangements.
Standard hours may exceed eight (8) hours per day when placed under an approved CWW, and such hours will not be classified as overtime.
Overtime, holiday work, and rest-day work shall be compensated in accordance with Philippine Labor Code Articles 87–90.
What We’re Looking For
Proven ability to close sales and overcome objections
Strong customer service and empathy skills
Goal-oriented with strong adaptability
Coachable and receptive to feedback
Consistent achievement of performance targets
Qualifications
High school graduate; college preferred
Excellent written and spoken English
At least 2 years of telesales or BPO experience
Familiarity with CRM/VoIP tools (Google Suite preferred)
Desktop/laptop with camera (Core i5, 8GB RAM minimum), dual monitors, noise-canceling headset
25 Mbps connection with 10 Mbps backup
Quiet, dedicated workspace (PLDT/Converge preferred)

Base Pay: Php 41,360.00/month
Performance Incentives: Php 9,000–37,000/month
Total Potential Pay: Php 50,360–78,360/month
Statutory benefits(SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG, 13th month pay, taxes)
13th month pay and night differential as mandated by DOLE
Work from the comfort of your own home
ai_core_responsibilities: Manage inbound and outbound warm calls to close new customers and upsell services. Maintain accurate CRM records and meet sales metrics and quality standards.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have at least 2 years of telesales or BPO experience and be fluent in English. A high school diploma is required, with a preference for college education.

title: Marketing Specialist
description_text:
FICS, Inc. is seeking a creative and results-driven Marketing Specialist to join our dynamic team. Working with the Marketing Director, as a Marketing Specialist, you will play a pivotal role in developing and executing marketing strategies that promote our innovative loan origination and servicing software products and engage our target audience. 
You will be responsible for creating compelling content for various marketing materials, coordinating digital marketing campaigns, and analyzing market trends to identify new opportunities. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with a passion for marketing, a knack for storytelling, and is extremely organized. If you are ready to make a significant impact in our organization and create marketing initiatives that resonate, we want to hear from you!
What You Bring:
Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Advertising, Communications, and/or a related field.
Minimum of four (4) years of experience in marketing, including developing and implementing marketing strategies, as well as graphic design experience, preferably for a mortgage company, financial institution, or in the software industry.
Significant work experience as a marketing specialist or similar role.
Design samples and marketing campaign examples upon request.
Intermediate to Advanced Proficiency in Adobe CC Applications (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign, and Acrobat).
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office, including Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint and ability to learn other software used by FICS in a Windows environment
Physical ability to unpack, assemble, disassemble, and pack convention booths.
Strong analytical skills to measure and report on the effectiveness of marketing campaigns.
Detail-oriented with excellent organizational and project management skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment, as well as independently.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to create engaging content.
Experience with email marketing is preferred but not required.
A creative mindset with an eagerness to learn and innovate.
At FICS, our customers are our top priority, and we've proudly established a reputation for delivering substantial competitive advantages to our nationwide clientele for over 40 years and still going strong.
Curious about what it's like to be part of the FICS family? Discover more at: www.FICS.com/about-us/life-at-fics. Check out some of the amazing benefits and perks we offer at www.FICS.com/about-us/employment.
Don’t miss out—apply today to become a member of the FICS Marketing team!
ai_core_responsibilities: The Marketing Specialist will develop and execute marketing strategies to promote software products and engage the target audience. Responsibilities include creating content, coordinating campaigns, and analyzing market trends.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have a bachelor's degree in a related field and a minimum of four years of marketing experience. Proficiency in Adobe CC applications and strong analytical skills are also required.

FICS® is a leading mortgage loan origination software, residential servicing software, and commercial loan servicing software provider. FICS offers the most cost-effective, efficient, flexible software solutions on the market today. Mortgage Servicer® automates mortgage servicing operations, including payment processing, investor reporting, custodial accounting, imaging, report writing and more. Commercial Servicer® automates and provides seamless data flow for virtually every facet of commercial loan servicing for diverse types of commercial loan products. Loan Producer® automates the loan origination process from point of sale to closing, funding and secondary marketing, a complete end-to-end loan origination system.

With FICS’ solutions, you'll save money through a low initial investment, nominal monthly support, and maintenance fee, paperless servicing or origination, real time processing, and enhanced productivity. FICS’ software systems are flexible, meeting the specific needs of small and large companies. When you call FICS, you'll speak with our receptionist who will direct you to the appropriate support person. You won't lose valuable time waiting for someone to return your call or answer your email. We strive to exceed our customers’ expectations.

Visit www.fics.com for more information about our exceptional software solutions.

title: Operations Associate, Proxy Voting
description_text:
Proxymity does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, marital status, or based on an individual’s status in any group or class protected by applicable federal, state or local law. Proxymity encourages application from minorities, women, the disabled, protected veterans and all other qualified applicants.
About Proxymity
Proxymity is a market leading digital investor communications platform focused on providing regulatory compliant products focused initially on Proxy Voting and Shareholder Disclosure services. Founded in London and spun out of Citi, from the very beginning our mission focused on benefitting the whole capital markets ecosystem, rather than just one part or one player within it. This ethos is endorsed by investment from global consortium of some the industry’s most influential financial institutions. 
Proxymity’s service offering is now a Global one, matching the needs of our custodian and broker community respectively. This is reflected by a fast-growing client base who have long desired an improved service by a trusted vendor. As a result, we are a fast-growth company and have already gone from 10 to 200+ employees in the last four years with a global footprint in London, New York, Tel-Aviv, Malaysia and Melbourne. 
What we’re looking for: 
Smart, energetic and inquisitive individuals to join our core Operations team in Manchester. You will perform a critical role in delivering and supporting first class operational execution and client support through the Proxymity platform and ancillary systems and processes. 
You will become a key contributor throughout the growth phases of Proxymity, supporting various pivotal activities and will have the opportunity to expand and develop your remit and role as the needs of the business continue to grow. 
Core Responsibilities: 
The core responsibilities for this role include, but are not limited to; 
Complete Proxy Voting processes and control activities in line with procedures and policies 
Ensure Proxy Voting assigned queries are managed in accordance with agreed KPIs 
Understand and track current and future workloads 
Execute daily tasks with a client focused approach 
Ask questions to challenge the status quo 
Identify opportunities for process, platform and control environment improvements 
Participate in improvement projects and operational testing as necessary to support the change agenda 
Review, understand, and operate within Standard Operating Procedures and be responsible for maintaining their integrity through continuous review and validation 
Proactively seek opportunities for self-improvement 
Understand the goals and objectives of the team, ensuring personal contributions and work execution align 
Assist with the training of new employees to the team 
Create a culture of inclusion and belonging, leading by example 
Escalate issues and / or concerns that impact the business, your role or personal wellbeing in a timely manner 
Be an ambassador for the team, Operations and Proxymity, always 
Strong attention to detail 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills 
Team orientated with the ability to build positive relationships and work collaboratively with internal and external partners, peers and clients 
Ability to work self-sufficiently with strong organisational skills 
Ability to execute in a high pressure, fast paced environment 
Flexibility to manage changing priorities and respond to time constraints 
Fast learning to understand complex business processes and take initiative to acquire necessary knowledge 
Seeks and acts upon feedback with a focus on personal development 
Desirables: 
Understanding of the Securities Services industry and Asset Servicing products, or similar 
Working experience within an operations environment 
Experience of working within a start-up / scale-up organisation 
Experience of operational workflow management tools and processes 
Fluency in another language 
Practical experience of working with remote teams 
Knowledge of programming / coding languages and applications 
Working knowledge of Microsoft applications 
Desire to develop technical skills to build effective solutions for the business 
Headspace subscription
Birthday off in addition to annual leave
Access to Absorb Learning
Improved family-friendly policies
Hybrid working
Choose your tech
2 duvet days a year
1 volunteer day a year
4-week sabbatical after 4 years at Proxymity
Workation- Our Workation policy means you can work anywhere in the world for up to 45 days per calendar year
A dog friendly office with snacks and free drinks
Companywide social events x2 per year
Regular team social events
A company wins programme
Companywide panels on variety of subjects like diversity and inclusion, mental heath, wellbeing etc.
To assist us with evolving our DEI efforts and ensuring our process is as inclusive as possible, we will be sending out a brief diversity and inclusion survey for you to complete if you choose to apply for this position. This will be completely anonymous and is in no way tied to your application. If you could please take a few minutes out of your day to complete this that would be much appreciated.
