linkedin_org_description:
About Proxymity

Adopted by 94% of FTSE 100 companies, Proxymity is a leading digital investor communications platform that connects issuers, intermediaries, and investors directly in real time. Our technology replaces manual, fragmented and paper-based processes with secure, authenticated digital connectivity across the shareholder communications chain, delivering faster processing, greater accuracy and end-to-end transparency. 

Our Solutions 

Proxymity Vote Connect
A digital proxy voting platform serving intermediary banks, custodians and brokers. Vote Connect enables real-time distribution of meeting announcements, vote instructions, and vote confirmations through a direct, authenticated network.

Proxymity Vote Connect North America
A purpose-built digital proxy voting solution for the US and Canadian markets, enabling intermediaries and investors to vote at general meetings with greater speed, accuracy, and transparency.

Proxymity Shareholder Insights
A shareholder reporting suite for issuers and issuer agents, delivering enhanced visibility into shareholder ownership, voting behaviour and engagement.

Proxymity Shareholder Disclosure
A fully automated shareholder disclosure solution for intermediaries, custodians and banks, enabling secure, accurate and compliant processing of disclosure requests across multiple jurisdictions.

Our Story

Founded by Dean Little and Jonathan Smalley, Proxymity was developed within Citi’s ICG Securities Services business. Today, we are backed by a consortium of the world’s largest financial institutions managing over $200 trillion in assets under custody, including BNY, BNP Paribas, BetaNXT, Citi, Computershare, Deutsche Bank, Deutsche Börse, HSBC, JP Morgan, MUFG and State Street.

Recent Awards

“Innovation in Digital Proxy Voting” & “Outstanding Technology Provider” - Global Custodian Magazine

Global Locations

London | Manchester | New York | Melbourne | Kuala Lumpur | Tel Aviv

title: Delivery Driver (Independent Contractor - Multiple Route Types Available
description_text:
NOW Courier is actively accepting applications for new route openings across Indiana and surrounding states.
Drive with purpose. Deliver with NOW Courier.
As an Independent Contractor Driver, you’ll partner with NOW Courier to deliver essential goods throughout Indiana and neighboring regions. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routed schedule or flexible on-demand work, we’re always seeking dependable, professional drivers to join our growing network.
Why Partner with NOW Courier:
✅ Freedom & Flexibility: Choose the routes that fit your schedule — full-time, part-time, or supplemental work.
✅ Reliable Earnings: Transparent route pay — no guessing games.
✅ Supportive Team: Our contractor relations and operations teams are here to help — not micromanage.
✅ Local Opportunity: Partner with an Indiana-based company that values relationships and reliability.
Maximize Your Earnings & Benefits:
💰 Maximize Your Earnings: Drive more, earn more – your earnings scale with your efforts.
🏦 Weekly Pay: Enjoy the convenience of weekly deposits directly into your bank account.
🚚 Join a Leader: Be part of NOW Courier, a recognized leader in the industry. We outpace the competition with our swift responses and efficiency, operating from six strategic locations with a team of 225 dedicated drivers serving Indiana and its neighboring states.
Available Opportunities Include:
We continuously recruit for:
Routed Drivers — fixed schedules with repeat customers. 
On-Demand Drivers — same-day flexible deliveries. 
Sprinter Van Drivers — high-volume and specialty routes (van required). 
📍 Common hiring areas:
Indianapolis • Merrillville • Fort Wayne • South Bend • Evansville • Scottsburg
Even if we don’t currently have an opening in your area, we encourage you to apply. Your application ensures you’ll be among the first contacted when new routes become available.
Must be 21+ years old with a valid driver’s license. 
Must have a reliable vehicle and smartphone. 
Must carry active auto insurance and be willing to carry commercial auto insurance. 
Must be able to lift up to 50 lbs. 
Authorized to work in the U.S.
You must successfully pass a comprehensive contract screening process before beginning with NOW Courier, which includes
Our Values:
Empathy. Drive. Agility. Reliability.
These are the values that guide what we do, why we deliver, and who we decide to contract with. We want people who are accepting of others, look for creative solutions around obstacles, remain adaptable and flexible to meet the moment, and exemplify commitment, discipline, and a solid work ethic.
By submitting your resume, you indicate your interest in partnering with NOW Courier and allow us to consider you for future opportunities that match your location.
We appreciate your interest in joining our team at NOW Courier!

title: Retail Compliance Officer
description_text:
At ProCook we have an exciting opportunity for a new role as a Retail Complience Officer. This is an exciting role for someone who has an eye for detail and an interest in risk management. As we grow as a business, we are looking to build out our risk and compliance function, and compliance assessments to ensure our services are delivered to the highest possible standard across stores and the warehouse to keep customers coming back to ProCook. 
The candidate must be happy to travel across the country conducting compliance assessments regularly (occasionally with overnight stays). Prior experience in an internal audit or compliance role within retail is desirable.
Support with the enhancement of store and warehouse audits (prior experience would be ideal). 
Conduct store audits, document findings, assign and follow up on actions. 
Conduct warehouse audits, document findings, assign and follow up on actions. 
Partner effectively with Retail and Warehouse leadership to improve our compliance and evidence where required. 
Key areas of focus in audits will be Health & Safety, customer service, stock management, merchandising and labelling, cash management, front of house & back of house checks, and data privacy. 
Additional Areas of Opportunity: 
Support delivery of periodic thematic reviews focusing on key areas of the business with direction from the Board. 
Support the business in the management of risk events – identifying the root cause, understanding the corrective actions, liaising with the business to agree preventative actions and tracking the event to closure
This role is for you if you have the below skills:
Active listening, observation and attention to detail are key for this role. As an independent compliance function, and a regulated business, we must conduct our audits thoroughly and objectively. 
Analytical and innovative problem-solving skills are needed here. We partner effectively to ensure any actions assigned are SMART (specific, measurable, achievable, realistic and time-based) and practical to continue to grow at scale. 
Experience of compliance / internal audit within retail is a preference here to provide valuable insights. 
We pride ourselves on our sustainability practices and ethical business model and so should you. 
We foster a culture of teamwork and collaboration across the business. In this role, you’ll be partnering with the Heads of Retail and the Warehouse to help to ensure our customer service is the best in the market. This scope would be broader for thematic reviews and risk events. 
We support the business, as a second line function, in our compliance and operational efficiency. 
As a growing business, we must adapt our recommendations – we focus on sustainable solutions, which are cost-effective and efficient.
This role requires attention to detail and commitment to excellence.
What’s in it for You?
💰Salary is between £34,000 - £38,000 depending on experience and skills
🏆 Opportunity to work with a passionate team
🎁 Generous colleague discount - 40% off to spend in store or online + 30% for friends and family
🌍 2 x Annual paid volunteering days
📞 Employee Assistance Programme
👥 Friends and Family Referral Scheme
🏖️33 Days Annual Leave (including bank holidays) 
💰 Discretionary bonus scheme of up to 15%
🅱️ B Corp certified company committed to ethical practices and sustainability
🥇 A company recognised as a Great Place to Work for 4 consecutive years
🩼 Disability Confident Leader
We Welcome Everyone

At ProCook we are committed to diversity and inclusion, and we welcome applications from people of all abilities.

If you require any reasonable adjustments to support you through the application or interview process, please let us know. We’re committed to making our recruitment process as inclusive and accessible as possible.
About ProCook’s Sustainability Commitment:

At ProCook, sustainability is at the heart of what we do. 
As a certified B Corporation, we strive to balance profit with purpose, focusing on reducing waste, and ensuring ethical business practices. 
By joining our team, you will contribute to our efforts in creating a positive impact on the planet.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Retail Compliance Officer will conduct regular compliance assessments in stores and the warehouse, documenting findings, assigning actions, and partnering with leadership to improve compliance standards across key areas like Health & Safety and stock management. This role also involves supporting periodic thematic reviews and managing risk events by identifying root causes and tracking corrective actions to closure.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess strong active listening, observation, and attention to detail skills, along with analytical and innovative problem-solving abilities to ensure thorough and objective audits. Prior experience in an internal audit or compliance role within the retail sector is desirable, as is the ability to travel frequently across the country, sometimes requiring overnight stays.

title: C004570 Dep. Service Delivery Manager- Pen Test. Svcs (NS) - THU 19 Feb RELAUNCH
description_text:
Deadline Date: Thursday 19 February 2026
Requirement Title: Deputy Service Delivery Manager – Penetration Testing Services
Location: Mons, Belgium
Full time on-site: Yes
Not to Exceed: 97 Euro
Total Scope of the request (hours): 1254
Required Start Date: 23 MARCH 2026
End Contract Date: 31 DEC 2026
Required Security Clearance: NATO Secret
Specific Working Conditions: Normal Office Conditions. 10 days travel is foreseen for this position within Belgium. Post is required to be on site in Mons full time.
Previously submitted candidates deemed non-compliant for the following reasons:
- Network technician, lack of proven experience (before oct 24) in the penetration testing and offensive security
- test engineer, no professional experience in penetration testing or adversary emulation, poor experience in SDM/PM in cyber security
- candidate interviewed, limited knowledge in penetration testing and SDM/PM roles, not enough to fulfil the position
- System administrator/security officer without extensive background in penetration testing or adversary emulation
- crafted CV, current role is Incident responder, not penetration tester
- SOC manager/Incident response, no significant experience in penetration testing or adversary emulation
- no penetration testing experience
- VA manager experience but no penetration testing experience or adversary emulation experience. Not enough to fulfill the position.
- Cloud / AI engineer, lack of proven experience in penetration testing and SDM/PM functions
Duties and Role 
Support the SDM in delivering high-quality penetration testing services.
Accurately evaluate scope and level of effort (LoE) for penetration testing tasks.
Provide technical oversight of vulnerabilities identified during testing.
Act as a technical liaison between penetration testers, service management, and stakeholders.
Contribute to service quality, consistency, and continuous improvement.
Assist in planning and scheduling penetration testing engagements across NATO and NCIA environments.
Coordinate resources and ensure adherence to agreed timelines.
Coordinate resources and ensure adherence to agreed timelines.
Monitor engagement progress and manage risks and dependencies.
Conduct technical scoping discussions with stakeholders.
Assess complexity, attack surface, and constraints of target systems.
Produce justified LoE estimates aligned with NCSC PTAE methodologies.
Support engagement kick-offs, debriefings, and technical discussions.
Translate technical findings into risk-focused language for decision-makers
Support service reporting, KPIs, and dashboards.
Contribute to methodology updates and lessons learned.
NATO Secret security clearance
3+ years in project management or service delivery management in cyber security.
Extensive technical background in penetration testing and offensive security.
Demonstrated understanding of vulnerabilities across network, application, and cloud environments.
Ability to estimate effort and scope complex technical assessments.
Strong understanding of the penetration testing service lifecycle.
Proven ability to scope engagements including objectives, in/out of scope, assumptions, constraints, RoE, and deliverables
Solid knowledge of common penetration testing standards and requirements (e.g., OWASP, PTES, NIST/ISO-aligned practices)
ITIL experience, with a strong focus on Change Management
Proven customer-facing experience including requirements gathering, stakeholder management, and expectation setting.
Ability to map needs to the right test types (web/API, network, cloud, mobile, wireless, red teaming vs. penetration testing)
Excellent English (written and spoken); able to produce and review customer-ready documentation
ai_core_responsibilities: The Deputy Service Delivery Manager will support the SDM in delivering high-quality penetration testing services, including accurately evaluating scope and level of effort for testing tasks. This role involves providing technical oversight of identified vulnerabilities and acting as a technical liaison between testers, service management, and stakeholders.

A NATO Secret security clearance is mandatory, along with proven ability to scope complex engagements and strong knowledge of common penetration testing standards.

title: Maintenance Engineer - Hilton Garden Inn, Fort Myers, FL
description_text:
Join us at the Hilton Garden Inn Fort Myers, FL, a Lafrance Hospitality property. We are excited to add a new member to our team.
We are currently looking for a Part Time Maintenance Engineer.
We want you to join our team at the Hilton Garden Inn as a part of our Hotel Maintenance Team! At our hotel you'll have a great work environment surrounded by friendly coworkers and have opportunities for cross training, career growth and mentorship. As a Hilton Team member you'll receive amazing discounts on hotel rooms for both you & your family to enjoy. In addition we run Company wide incentives both year round & seasonal for all staff.
Take advantage of our remarkable DailyPay feature, which allows you to receive your earnings on any day that you prefer. Furthermore, if you successfully refer someone to join our remarkable team, you have the opportunity to earn up to an astounding $500.
The Hotel Maintenance Engineer is responsible for the maintenance of the hotel’s building and grounds and the operation of its equipment and mechanical electrical systems.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Reviews maintenance problems, complaints and work orders to prioritize and schedule work assignments.
Trouble-shoots, diagnoses and repairs malfunctioning electrical/mechanical systems and equipment to his/her level of ability.
Inspects property to identify potential and current needs.
Plans and executes on-going appropriate maintenance programs.
Solicits bids from contractors, evaluates their proposals and, once employed, ensures work is completed to specifications.
Coordinates with local health, safety, fire and building inspectors to ensure compliance with all applicable codes and regulations.
Performs preventive maintenance assignments on a scheduled basis.
Services the hotel’s pool including adjusting chemicals and cleaning filters.
Maintains the building’s exterior if not serviced by a contractor (e.g., lawn care, painting.)
Pay: $17-$19 Per Hour
Schedule: Nights and weekend availability is required 
Job Type: Part Time 
Keywords: maintenance, hotel maintenance, hotel, handyman, maintenance engineer
Knowledge of HVAC, troubleshooting and servicing up to his/her ability.
Skill in maintaining and repairing security hardware.
Thorough knowledge of the occupation hazards and corresponding safety precautions necessary for the safe performance of assigned duties.
Pool testing certification, if appropriate.
Nights & Weekend availability
DailyPay - Get Paid Any Day!
Brand Wide Room Discounts
Company Wide Room Discount Program
Annual Review Program
Extensive Room for Growth
Paid Training
ai_core_responsibilities: The Hotel Maintenance Engineer manages the maintenance of the hotel's building, grounds, and mechanical/electrical systems by prioritizing and scheduling work orders based on reviews of maintenance problems and complaints. This role involves troubleshooting, diagnosing, and repairing malfunctions, inspecting the property for needs, and planning ongoing maintenance programs.

Availability for nights and weekends is required, and pool testing certification is necessary if appropriate for the role.

linkedin_org_description:
Lafrance Hospitality was established in the 1950’s and has since grown into a full service hospitality company with a current portfolio of fourteen hotels, five restaurants/function facilities and one catering division. Our current hotels include Hilton, Marriott, IHG and Choice properties. With our team of esteemed professionals, depth of experience, proven strategies and commitment to providing an outstanding guest experience, Lafrance Hospitality provides a complete range of management services. Our small size affords our team the opportunity to focus on each hotel’s individual markets and needs, and apply our core principles; hiring, training and retaining the best team members, exceeding guest expectations, and maintaining streamlined, efficient operations.

Lafrance Hospitality's corporate office consists of dedicated team members in all aspects of development and operations. Lafrance Hospitality has the ability to provide consulting and management services in all aspects of operations; finance, sales and marketing, development, daily operations and human resources. In addition, we provide distressed asset management and receivership services to ensure distressed assets run in a cost effective manner during the critical transition phase. Our hands on approach in daily operations have contributed to the success of each of our properties.

Our vision is to be the PREMIER Hospitality Company by employing the VERY BEST PEOPLE, trained and empowered, to deliver a QUALITY product with SENSATIONAL service to every guest, every time.

title: CSA Package Manager
description_text:
Winthrop Technologies is a dedicated data centre delivery partner providing turnkey solutions to our clients, headquartered in Dublin and delivering throughout Europe.
Winthrop specialises in providing turnkey data centre solutions, providing services from design, through to construction across the full range of civil, structural, architectural, mechanical and electrical services as well as commissioning.
Winthrop has grown to become the leading and most trusted company in our sector and is the only dedicated Data Centre delivery partner in Europe.
We are currently constructing multiple turnkey data centre projects in 7 different European countries, equating to over 430 MW of IT load in flight.
We are pleased to announce that we are currently seeking a CSA Manager to join our team. This role will look after the roofing and cladding work packages.
Manage the co-ordination of the Civil and Structural work package. This particular role will look after the roofing and cladding package.
Management of Site QA in conjunction with the project team.
Management of Project Schedule in conjunction with Planner, Project Managers, Sub-Contractors and consulting Engineer/Architects etc.
Management of all day to day site operations
Management of sub-contractor activities including tracking progress against schedule and manpower levels. Carrying out weekly meetings with sub-contractor.
Ensure all Sub-contractor documentation including safety documentation is maintained up to date and filed for future reference.
Attendance at Site Meetings, Internal Project Reviews, Engineering Reviews, Planning and Co-ordination Meetings and Handover Meetings
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or similar essential 
At least 7 years of CSA industry experience.
Strong background in managing roofing and/or cladding packages is essential.
Ability to maintain an overview of entire projects while continuing to attend to detailed technicalities
Experience in scheduling, programming, and sequencing.
Experience in effectively managing teams is essential. 
Experience in the delivery of large-scale MEP project
Winthrop Technologies ltd are an equal opportunities employer
ai_core_responsibilities: This role is responsible for managing the coordination of the Civil and Structural work packages, with a specific focus on overseeing the roofing and cladding work packages. Key duties include managing day-to-day site operations, tracking subcontractor progress against the schedule, and ensuring all required safety and project documentation is maintained.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or a similar field, along with a minimum of 7 years of experience in the CSA industry. Essential requirements include a strong background in managing roofing and/or cladding packages and proven experience in effectively managing teams.

title: Sales Promotor MediaMarkt Hisense
description_text:
Zoek je een leuke bijverdienste in het weekend? Word jij enthousiast van elektronica zoals smart tv’s of laser tv’s en is verkopen helemaal jouw ding? Als Hisense shop-in-shop verkoper doe je sales ervaring op bij een internationaal bedrijf.
Wat doet een verkoper voor Hisense? Als verkoper in een MediaMarkt filiaal ben jij onderdeel van het CPM Sales Team voor Hisense. Je bent het aanspreekpunt voor de consument wanneer zij vragen hebben of opzoek zijn naar advies. Je brengt de behoefte in kaart en overtuigt hen van de voordelen die de producten van Hisense hen kunnen bieden. Je zorgt dat de klanten met een goed gevoel ( én een aankoop) huiswaarts keren.
Jouw profiel:
Perfect NL talig
Commercieel ingesteld en enthousiast
Je stapt spontaan op mensen af en houdt van targets te behalen
Interesse in elektronica
Je hebt reeds ervaring in sales of promotie
Beschikbaar in de weekends (vrijdag en zaterdag) is een must
Wij bieden jou:
Niet enkel een job waar je zelfvertrouwen en sales skills enkel maar kunnen groeien, maar ook:
Werken voor een internationaal merk
Dagcontracten of contract onbepaalde duur
Marktconform loon 
Volledige reiskostenvergoeding of kilometervergoeding
Sales trainingen
Doorgroeimogelijkheden 
Ben jij dé Sales Promotor die wij zoeken?
Solliciteer direct, dan nemen wij spoedig contact met je op. 
Vragen over de vacature? 
Neem contact op met Charlotte!
charlotte.vandeputte@cpm-int.com
ai_core_responsibilities: As a Hisense shop-in-shop seller in a MediaMarkt store, the role involves being the primary contact for consumers seeking advice on electronics like smart TVs or laser TVs. The main goal is to assess customer needs and convince them of the benefits of Hisense products, ensuring customers leave satisfied with a purchase.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate must be perfectly fluent in Dutch, possess a commercial mindset, and be enthusiastic about approaching people spontaneously to achieve sales targets. Prior experience in sales or promotion is required, along with a genuine interest in electronics and mandatory availability on weekends (Friday and Saturday).
linkedin_org_slogan: We inspire people to TRY, BUY & LOVE your brands.

CPM is een internationaal sales outsourcing en customer experience bureau. Wij inspireren mensen om uw merk te PROBEREN, KOPEN & ervan te HOUDEN, waar, wanneer en hoe ze ook beïnvloed kunnen worden.

Wij werken met merken en bedrijven die elke kans willen identificeren en aangrijpen om het rendement te maximaliseren en de risico's efficiënt te minimaliseren. We werken samen met iconische merken in alle belangrijke sectoren, van eten en drinken tot luxe, technologie en auto's.  

Onze geweldige mensen maken contact met klanten via fysieke, virtuele en digitale touchpoints in meerdere markten en kanalen. Met behulp van strategische planning, datawetenschap en technologie selecteren en implementeren we de beste middelen op de meest flexibele manier om de resultaten voor u te maximaliseren.

Onze geïntegreerde oplossingen omvatten: Sales, Merchandising, Customer Experience, Sampling, Experience Marketing, Shopper Marketing, Auditing en Data Science.

Maar we zouden het niet kunnen zonder onze getalenteerde mensen.  Zij beschikken over een breed scala aan vaardigheden, ervaringen en achtergronden en werken als een hecht team dat om elkaar geeft, nieuwsgierig is en samen resultaten boekt.   

Er is een betere manier. Neem contact met ons op om te zien hoe wij u kunnen inspireren

title: NOW Courier: Delivery Driver (Routed)
description_text:
Seize the Freedom of the Open Road!
Are you a dependable go-getter with a passion for customer service and the thrill of being your own boss? NOW Courier, a recognized leader in logistics, is looking for enthusiastic and professional Contract Delivery Drivers to join our successful fleet.
This role covers various open routes, breakdowns, and overflow, meaning we need flexible drivers ready to take on diverse assignments across the region. We offer the freedom to steer your own schedule while delivering top-notch service that keeps our customers smiling. New to the driving game? We provide expert guidance to help you succeed!
Your Thrilling Mission:
Clockwork Precision: Pick up and deliver cargo to our valued customers on time, every time.
Smart Navigation: Use GPS and mapping software to chart and follow the most streamlined routes.
Clear Communication: Maintain crystal-clear communication with customers and dispatchers, providing real-time updates and quickly addressing any issues.
Safety First: Adhere to all traffic laws, secure cargo properly, and use appropriate safety gear.
Customer Service Excellence: Deliver a wave of customer service—handling questions, resolving complaints, and ensuring complete customer satisfaction.
Problem Solver: Showcase your knack for quick thinking when faced with unexpected hurdles like traffic snags or incorrect addresses.
Opportunity & Earnings Details: 
Potential Commission: Earn up to $800 to $1,200 per week (depending on the routes you cover).
Availability: Routes run Monday-Friday with Saturday opportunities, when available. Start times range from 6:00 AM to 9:00 AM and may fluctuate based on the route, but all deliveries must be finalized by 5:00 PM.
Route Details: Expect between 30 to 100 daily stops and weekly mileage that varies up to 1,750 miles.
Coverage Area: Mostly operating in Indiana, with routes potentially involving considerable inter-state travel.
Preferred Vehicle: Cargo Van or Sprinter are preferred.
To maintain our industry-leading fleet of professional drivers, you must meet the following:
Age: Be at least 21 years of age.
Vehicle: Own a vehicle that can pass a multi-point inspection and is suitable for delivery.
Smartphone: Possess a smartphone capable of running our driver app.
Licensing & Insurance: Hold a valid driver's license and commercial insurance coverage with a minimum of $300,000 combined single limit liability.
Pre-Employment Screening: You must successfully pass a comprehensive pre-employment process before beginning work, which includes a:
Driving History Review (past 36 months)
Criminal Background Check
Substance Abuse (Drug) Screening
Why Drive with NOW Courier?
Maximize Your Earnings: Your earnings potential scales with your efforts. Drive more, earn more!
Abundance of Opportunities: Regular and additional assignments to help you maximize your income.
Weekly Pay: Enjoy the convenience of weekly direct deposits into your bank account.
Comprehensive Benefits Access: Access to medical, dental, and vision insurance options for you and your loved ones.*
Essential Savings: Retain more of your earnings with discounted rates on a range of essential services, including automotive, legal, financial, and others.*
Join a Recognized Leader: Be a part of a company that outpaces the competition, operating from seven strategic locations and serving Indiana and its neighboring states.
* Benefits and essential service discounts are offered through an external partner. Utilizing these services does not alter your independent contractor status in any way.
ai_core_responsibilities: Delivery drivers are responsible for picking up and delivering cargo to customers on time while maintaining clear communication with dispatchers. They must also adhere to safety regulations and provide excellent customer service.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be at least 21 years old, own a suitable vehicle, and possess a valid driver's license with commercial insurance. A smartphone capable of running the driver app is also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: It is the why that drives us
linkedin_org_specialties: Exclusive Use Drivers for On-Demand & Routes
On-Demand Services with Convenient Online Ordering
Reliable Route Expertise across Indiana & the Midwest
Route analysis,planning & design to address growth & changes
Vet & Train Additional Drivers for Growth

linkedin_org_description: NOW Courier stands out as the leading provider of last-mile and transportation services in the Midwest, exceeding the capabilities of traditional carriers like FedEx, UPS, and USPS in control, insight, and service quality. Our specialized distribution network ensures swift and efficient parcel delivery across the Midwest, supported by skilled drivers dedicated to precise, timely, and conscientious delivery. We offer not only the creation of a customized transportation program for our customers but also its ongoing refinement to keep pace with their changing business needs. Our customers, spanning from healthcare providers to family-owned businesses, trust us to help them keep their most important promises.

title: Model Design Engineer
description_text:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is what happens when history, purpose and daring talent come together. Backed by TWG Global and GM, our team is uniquely positioned to disrupt Formula 1®, bringing a fresh perspective and an unrelenting drive for success. We have the energy of a start-up, with the ideas and originality of a business that always wants to lead, never wants to follow.
We’re building everything from the ground up, from a high-performance car to an inclusive, values-driven culture. We show bold ambition. We combine leadership in innovation with excellence in execution. We are one team. We have the freedom to think differently, the opportunity to shape process and practice, an ego-free environment where people thrive on being challenged by those around them. A historic name behind us. Career-defining moments ahead.

A New Chapter Begins.
Fueled by bold ambition
Play your part in getting us on the grid.
Closing Date: 20th February 2026
As a Model Design Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in delivering the physical components that feed our aerodynamic development—the very foundation of on-track performance. Working at the heart of our Silverstone facility, you’ll be part of a highly skilled, ambitious group of engineers focused on technical excellence and relentless innovation. Your work will directly influence car performance in the wind tunnel, translating design into data, and ultimately into lap time.
You’ll be trusted with end-to-end ownership of complex design projects, and encouraged to challenge convention, push boundaries, and propose smarter, faster, more efficient ways of working. With a culture that values initiative and creativity, your ideas will not only be heard—they’ll be expected.
This is your chance to make a lasting mark on the future of our team, in a high-performance environment where every part counts and every detail matters.
Deliver high-quality, well-validated parts for wind tunnel testing in line with aerodynamic targets
Support on complete design projects, including CAD creation, project management, quality-checking, and documentation
Continuously improve the functionality, quality, and robustness of model parts to optimize wind tunnel test efficiency
Drive the development and implementation of improved design workflows and standards
Collaborate closely with Aerodynamicists and Surface Design Engineers to ensure alignment with technical specifications
Ensure adherence to standard design practices and help refine them as needed
Driven by high performance
What do you need to bring to the team?
Degree in Engineering, Design, Motorsport, or a related discipline or equivalent
Strong proficiency in 3D CAD software (preferably 3DExperience/CATIA) and experience in using FEA for structural validation of parts
Strong understanding of parametric modeling, part robustness, and model layout principles
Strong knowledge of materials and manufacturing processes, including composites and advanced digital manufacturing techniques
Strong understanding of GD&T and its application and proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills
Enthusiastic contributor to a collaborative, inclusive, and open culture and highly detail-oriented with a commitment to delivering work right the first time
Willing to travel and work flexible hours during race season
A team like no other.
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team challenges conventions and redefines success through bold ambition, cutting-edge innovation, and an unwavering commitment to precision and excellence—on and off the track. This includes offering industry-leading pension, generous time off and, as part of a global brand, huge potential for career development. 
As an equal opportunities employer, we are committed to the equal treatment of all current and prospective employees and does not condone discrimination on the basis of age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, race or ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity or marriage and civil partnership. We aspire to have a diverse and inclusive workplace and strongly encourage suitably qualified applicants from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.
At The Cadillac Formula 1® Team, all Team Members are expected to actively support and uphold our policies and procedures, including those focused on Environmental responsibility, Sustainability initiatives, Inclusion and Health and Safety practices.
Please note that additional security checks may be required as part of the recruitment process. This may include a background check covering a minimum of the past five years and a criminal record check.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Model Design Engineer will be responsible for delivering high-quality, validated physical components for wind tunnel testing to meet aerodynamic targets, while supporting complete design projects including CAD creation and documentation. They must continuously improve model parts and drive the development of improved design workflows in collaboration with Aerodynamicists and Surface Design Engineers.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a degree in Engineering, Design, Motorsport, or a related discipline, along with strong proficiency in 3D CAD software (preferably 3DExperience/CATIA) and experience using FEA for structural validation. Essential knowledge includes parametric modeling, materials, manufacturing processes like composites, and strong understanding of GD&T.
linkedin_org_slogan: The Original American F1® Team
linkedin_org_description:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is The American Original—bringing a bold, American-led vision to the future of Formula 1™. Set to join the grid in 2026, the team is built from the ground up and designed for the global stage—driven by innovation, shaped for culture and engineered to deliver long-term brand affinity.
 
With operational hubs in Fishers (Indiana), Silverstone (UK) & Cologne (GER), we're creating a platform that blends world-class racing performance with commercial and cultural relevance. 
 
Powered by the strategic and commercial leadership of TWG Motorsports and the technical excellence of General Motors, the team combines deep motorsport expertise, cross-platform capability and a legacy of American engineering. At its heart is Cadillac—a brand defined by bold ambition, design innovation and modern American luxury.
 
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team reflects the ambition, optimism and ingenuity that define the American spirit—balanced with global sensibility and deep respect for the sport’s legacy.

title: Senior QA Engineer - Desktop and Distributed Systems
description_text:
Our Client is a US based security company looking for a Senior QA Engineer who thrives in complex, highly technical environments. 
You will own end-to-end manual and automated testing, ensuring the distributed services and endpoint applications (built in .NET, C++, and React) are secure, reliable, and compatible. This is not a button-clicking QA role — they need someone who can reverse-engineer bugs, write integration test harnesses, validate stateful client/server behavior, and think like an attacker or power user.
What You Will Do: 
Define, own, and execute comprehensive test strategies across backend services, web frontends, and desktop apps on both Windows and macOS.
Validate distributed system behaviors, including state synchronization, persistence, fault tolerance, and security guarantees.
Create detailed, well-researched test plans and cases based on low-level system behavior, API contracts, and desktop workflows.
Design and implement automation where appropriate (API/integration/UI level).
Partner closely with development teams to catch issues early through ATDD or early exploratory testing.
Contribute to reproducibility efforts for complex bugs (across platforms), including writing minimal repro apps or network mocks.
Drive quality in high-scale systems composed of dozens of backend services and desktop agents.
Maintain test environments and make sure they are evolving with requirements as they change.
5+ years of QA experience, with significant time spent testing backend distributed systems, desktop apps, and full stack web apps.
Strong hands-on experience testing Windows and macOS desktop applications, with a working knowledge of OS-level concepts like file systems, permissions, services, and sandboxing.
Experience designing and running manual and automated tests for REST APIs, message queues, and background services.
Understanding of .NET, C#, Win32 APIs, Swift, Objective-C, and modern web frameworks (like React) from a testing/debugging perspective.
Familiarity with network protocols, security testing, or reverse engineering behavior at the application level.
Experience using tools such as Charles/Fiddler, Wireshark, Postman, browser dev tools, or custom test harnesses.
Comfortable with modern issue tracking, CI/CD, test management, and observability tools.
Salary: $6,000- $7,000 USD /month + Holidays
Unlimited PTO
ai_core_responsibilities: Define and execute comprehensive test strategies across various platforms, ensuring the quality of distributed services and endpoint applications. Collaborate with development teams to identify and resolve issues early in the development process.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have over 5 years of QA experience, particularly with backend distributed systems and desktop applications. Strong knowledge of testing methodologies and familiarity with various programming languages and tools is essential.
linkedin_org_description: Sur Company is a company based out of Colombia.

title: Lead Hand - RROX
description_text:
As a locally owned and operated business, RROX Aggregates Ltd. is proud to be an affiliate of Park Paving Ltd. and a top-notch producer of high-quality aggregates for the Edmonton area. Our aggregate products are crafted and shipped via rail from our reserves in north-central Alberta straight to the Edmonton market. We're excited to announce that our RROX division is seeking a friendly and dedicated LEAD HAND to join our team!
Responsibilities include:
Perform general labor work: shoveling, cleaning of equipment, oversee and assist with setting up conveyors, crushing equipment, washing equipment, and screening equipment, and other labor activities associated with the production of high quality washed aggregates, and/or crushed materials.
Operate heavy equipment and processing plants (once deemed competent, or in training under competent supervisor) associated with the production of high quality washed aggregates, and/or crushed materials:
Heavy Equipment: Excavator, skid steer, loader, railcar mover (with proper certification), conveyors
Processing Plants: Crushers, Wash Plants, Soil Cement Plants
Ensure “pre-trips” and all Daily Equipment Checklists are completed daily and any equipment concerns are addressed to your foreman.
Complete all daily (and any additional) maintenance on equipment required for continued safe functioning. This includes, but is not limited to, greasing pivot pins and fittings, and oil and filter changes.
Complete all maintenance logs, and daily equipment journals.
Complete daily inspection of all railcars for deficiencies and defects and report to the foreman.
Comply with all Traffic Laws & Regulations.
Efficiently move material: dumping, placing, loading and unloading of material to and from trucks and railcars, feeding processing plants, and yard clean-up.
Achieve efficient and correct output of material by proper calibration of all processing plants and equipment.
Crew Development:
Set a positive example for the crew—encourage positive attitudes and crew harmony, demonstrate a willingness to learn, take direction and give direction.
Aid in the training and development of other crew members as required by your foreman (or other supervisor).
Ensure the crew is completing assigned daily tasks safely, thoroughly and efficiently.
Over time, you will be assigned more responsibilities and accountabilities in order to develop your leadership skills as well as receive training in pursuit of providing qualified supervision at all RROX sites.
Job Requirements:
Ability to kneel, stoop, crouch, balance, climb, or crawl.
Must have the ability to exert heavy physical effort, handling average weight objects up to 50 pounds safely.
Must be at work on time, ready to work long hours/overtime, and consecutive days, as scheduled or required.
Must be able to perform all job duties in a wide range of (adverse) weather conditions.
Valid and clean Class 5 (or higher) Driver’s License to operate company vehicles and equipment
Must be willing to continuously learn and develop knowledge on all site equipment and processes to improve efficiency and ensure continued operations under any circumstances.
Ability to work with other departments across the company as required.
Benefits:
Extended Health Care
Dental Care
Vision Care
Employee Assistance Program
RRSP Matching
Profit Sharing
Pay Scale: $34 / hour
Successful candidates will thrive in a physically demanding, highly productive, and high-pressure environment. They will also possess a valid driver’s license and have a strong drive to contribute to the team's success by working with operators and other crew members to perform a wide range of duties. Successful candidates will also be able to maintain a safety-minded and high accountability work culture while working with their direct supervisors, and other employees while maintaining an positive and respectful work environment.
*** Successful candidates must have a driving record free of major infractions, and must pass a medical/fitness exam, as conditions of employment. ***
ANY MISREPRESENTATION OF YOUR QUALIFICATIONS DURING THE APPLICATION PROCESS WILL RESULT IN THE IMMEDIATE DISQUALIFICATION OF YOUR CANDIDACY.
#PPLHP
ai_core_responsibilities: The Lead Hand will perform general labor work and operate heavy equipment associated with the production of high-quality aggregates. They will also oversee crew development and ensure safety and efficiency in daily operations.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be physically capable of handling heavy objects and working in adverse weather conditions. A valid Class 5 driver's license and a willingness to learn about site equipment and processes are also required.

linkedin_org_description:
Park Paving Ltd. is one of Edmonton's largest locally owned and operated road construction contractors specializing in all aspects asphalt and concrete construction.  Our company's services include new road construction, existing road rehab (road resurfacing, and full-depth reclamation), as well as the material supply of high-quality aggregates, soil-cement, and asphalt, to a wide range of specifications.  We're constantly growing and always have room for new clients, and we welcome any inquiries for construction services or material supply.

At Park Paving our care and commitment extend beyond providing the highest quality product, and we pride ourselves on providing a stable and opportunity-rich environment to all of our employees within the construction industry.

title: Impiegato/a Amministrativo/a Contabile
locations_alt_raw: Genoa, Metropolitan City of Genoa, Italy
description_text:
Ciao #FruitAdventurers!
Benvenuti in Cosiarma Spa, azienda che fa parte del Gruppo Orsero, leader nel Mediterraneo per l’importazione e distribuzione di prodotti ortofrutticoli freschi di qualità eccellente. Il nostro mondo è vivace, sfidante e ricco di connessioni internazionali. Il Gruppo Orsero opera in tutta l’Europa mediterranea generando un fatturato di 1,5 miliardi di euro.
Posizione
Siamo alla ricerca di un Impiegato/a Amministrativo/a Contabile per la nostra sede di Genova.
Nello specifico, la risorsa selezionata si occuperà di:
- Gestione contabilità clienti, fornitori e banche (principalmente fatturazione attiva e registrazioni fatture passive);
- Registrazioni di contabilità generale.
Requisiti
- Diploma di Ragioneria o cultura equivalente;
- Nozioni di contabilità e di partita doppia;
- Buona padronanza del pacchetto office, in particolare Excel;
- Gradita la conoscenza della lingua inglese.
Cosa ti offriamo:
- Inserimento entry level, contratto a tempo determinato iniziale con finalità di stabilizzazione.
- Orario di lavoro: full time 40 ore settimanali da lunedì a venerdì con orario 9:00 – 13:00 e 14:00 – 18:00.
Sedi di lavoro: 
Via Operai, 20 – 16149 Genova (GE)

Vuoi scoprire qualcosa in più su di noi? Visita il nostro sito!
Avviciniamo il mondo, ogni giorno. 🌍💚
#Orseropeople
Il Gruppo Orsero promuove le pari opportunità e accoglie con favore le candidature di tutte le persone, nel rispetto del D.lgs. 198/2006. Le selezioni sono aperte a candidati e candidate di ogni età, genere, orientamento sessuale, origine, disabilità e convinzione personale.
ai_core_responsibilities: The selected resource will manage client, supplier, and bank accounting, primarily handling active invoicing and recording passive invoices. Responsibilities also include general accounting entries.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a Diploma in Accounting or equivalent education, along with knowledge of accounting principles and double-entry bookkeeping. Good proficiency in the Microsoft Office suite, especially Excel, is required, and knowledge of English is appreciated.
linkedin_org_slogan: Importazione e distribuzione di prodotti ortofrutticoli freschi.
linkedin_org_specialties: importazione e distribuzione di prodotti ortofrutticoli freschi
linkedin_org_locations: Via Vezza d'Oglio, 7, Milano, 20139, IT
linkedin_org_description:
Da 60 anni distribuiamo ogni giorno nei Mercati all’Ingrosso di tutta Italia e alla Grande Distribuzione frutta e verdura fresca da ogni parte del mondo.
Stabiliamo con i nostri fornitori e clienti rapporti di partnership e da sempre lavoriamo per crescere insieme a loro.
Grazie a 7 magazzini di stoccaggio, maturazione e confezionamento offriamo un servizio tempestivo e capillare unico in Italia.
Dal qualche anno ci occupiamo anche di frutta tagliata pronta al consumo e frutta da bere.

title: Salesperson #JD Sports Ιωάννινα
locations_derived: Ioannina, Epirus and Western Macedonia, Greece
description_text:
Established in 1981 with a single store in the North West of England, the JD Group is a leading global omnichannel retailer of Sports, Fashion and Outdoor brands. The Group now has approximately 3,400 stores across 32 territories, with a strong presence in the UK, Europe, North America and Asia Pacific. In JD Group, we believe in extending our entrepreneurial and competitive spirit beyond financial performance to making the world around us a better place. We always strive to do the right thing for our colleagues, our customers, and our communities. We currently have more than 60,000 colleagues within our business. Our aim is to attract, develop and engage committed and talented people so that they choose to grow with the Group throughout their careers.
We are looking for a talented and passionate Store Manager for our JD Sports Store in Ioannina!
In this role, you will assist the Store Manager in the day to day running of the store. Taking ownership of a number of duties while making sure that the stores KPIs are achieved. Furthermore, in the Manager’s absence, you will be the first point of contact, therefore you will motivate the workforce to drive sales and offer exceptional customer service while exceeding targets and maintaining the standards of the business.
Responsibilities:
CUSTOMER
Maintain an exceptional service experience in store, ensuring high level customers’ satisfaction
Deal with inquiries and complaints and resolving clients issues / requests
Foster a Client centric mindset by developing and maintaining solid Client relationships
Implement, in partnership with other company functions, clienteling initiatives and events and securing Client loyalty
TEAM
Train, mentor, motivate, offer constructive feedback to the team and encourage progression within the company
Assist in regular training meetings within the store and ensure all personnel completes the inhouse online Academy, making sure the team is able to maintain the high standards required
Support team members (e.g., leading by example or coaching) to ensure a great guest experience
Promote an entrepreneurial and team spirit approach, using effective communication, ensuring efficient organization and professional growth
Ensure a hazard-free, safe and healthy working environment in compliance with regulations
Ensure the team is always informed about product, policies and procedures
Attract and recruit the best talents available on the market through networking and scouting
RETAIL
Manage day-to-day operational activities of the store and ensure compliance with policies and procedures
Monitor and control store productivity levels with an aim to achieve and exceed targeted KPIs
Analyse reports in order to make key sales decisions
Implement personnel and resource planning strategies to optimize staff schedules
Work extensively in partnership with the Marketing, VM, Commercial dept. in order to build strong customer retention
VISUAL MERCHANDISING
Attract, engage and motivate customers into making purchases in store by using the latest visual techniques.
Making sure that all new product and the advertised product is all visible and in the correct locations.
Ensure mannequins, displays, and windows are updated in accordance with Visual Merchandising guidelines.
University Degree or equivalent qualification
A minimum of 2-3 years’ experience of managing a standalone retail store (or Department Store floor)
Excellent use of English language and computer skills
Excellent verbal, written and interpersonal skills
Numerical and analytical knowledge to monitor KPIs
Sound working knowledge of Microsoft Office
Leadership skills and business orientation
Excellent communicator, with a positive, open and progressive, energetic, motivating, personal style
Customer service orientation with in-depth knowledge of basic business management processes
Passion for sports and fashion
Willingness to learn and tackle new challenges
Competitive compensation package & performance bonus
Career development opportunities & education programs
Health & wellness programs to keep you active
One day off, part of our "parents’ day off" program
Generous employee discount in all our brands
Up to €3,000 discount at UCLan, for both undergraduate and postgraduate studies, supporting your academic and professional development
Extras, discounts and perks-We offer a variety of 100+ cool extras! Link here

Responsibilities cover customer interaction, team leadership, retail operations management, and visual merchandising execution.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a University Degree or equivalent qualification along with a minimum of 2-3 years of experience managing a standalone retail store or department store floor. Essential requirements include excellent English language skills, strong computer literacy, numerical/analytical knowledge for KPI monitoring, and proven leadership and business orientation.
linkedin_org_slogan: Retailer in Sporting & Athleisure goods
linkedin_org_locations: 433 Mesogeion, Agía Paraskeví, Attiki 153 42, GR
linkedin_org_description:
Η ιστορία της Cosmos Sport ξεκινάει περίπου 40 χρόνια πριν, στη Χερσόνησο του Ηρακλείου Κρήτης, όταν το πρώτο ομώνυμο κατάστημα άνοιξε το 1982, εστιάζοντας στην ικανοποίηση του πελάτη, στο προϊόν και στην αθλητική κουλτούρα. Μέσα στα επόμενα χρόνια, κατέγραψε μια σταθερή αναπτυξιακή πορεία, επεκτείνοντας τις δραστηριότητές της σε μεγάλα αστικά κέντρα, αλλά και στην Κύπρο. Από το 2021, είναι μέλος του βρετανικού κολοσσού JD Sports Fashion Plc., και πλέον αριθμεί 6 εμπορικές επωνυμίες (www.cosmossport.gr, www.jdsports.gr www.sneaker10.gr, www.sportsfactory.gr, www.slamdunk.gr, www.rundome.gr) 90+ φυσικά καταστήματα, 9 πρωτοπόρα ηλεκτρονικά καταστήματα, και πάνω από 1.500 εργαζομένους σε Ελλάδα και Κύπρο! 

Όραμά μας είναι να μας αναγνωρίζουν ως την καλύτερη εμπειρία Sports & Athleisure αγορών, σε κάθε σημείο επαφής μας με τον πελάτη. Καθημερινά μας κινητοποιούν ο ενθουσιασμός των πελατών μας, η καινοτόμος δράση και το πάθος να κάνουμε τη διαφορά ως ομάδα.

Στη Cosmos ξεκινάμε την κάθε μας μέρα με μία νέα πρόκληση, επιδιώκοντας να γινόμαστε όλο και καλύτεροι - για εμάς, για τους ανθρώπους και τους πελάτες μας, για την κοινωνία και το περιβάλλον, για τον κόσμο μας. Με σεβασμό στη διαφορετικότητα & τη συμπερίληψη, τα προϊόντα, οι υπηρεσίες και οι δράσεις μας έχουν κοινό στόχο την αλλαγή και την εξέλιξη προς ένα μέλλον βιώσιμο, ανοιχτό για όλες και όλους, γεμάτο ευκαιρίες.

Σκοπός μας είναι να δίνουμε τον τόνο που θα ενθαρρύνει έναν πιο δραστήριο τρόπο ζωής και να ενδυναμώσουμε όσα άτομα τολμούν να υπερβούν τα όριά τους και διεκδικούν να εκφραστούν με το δικό τους μοναδικό τρόπο, προσφέροντας μεγάλη ποικιλία ρούχων, παπουτσιών, αξεσουάρ και εξοπλισμού από Κορυφαία Sport & Athleisure brands σε όλο τον κόσμο.

Μαζί, κάνουμε καθημερινά τον Κόσμο μας καλύτερο.

title: CentralReach Support Specialist (ABA Therapy) - EST Hrs (Remote)
description_text:
ISTA Personnel Solutions is a dynamic and fast-growing BPO company. We are not a recruitment agency - we operate as a dedicated extension of our clients’ teams, delivering high-quality service with precision, efficiency, and a personal touch.
Join our team as a CentralReach Support Specialist (ABA Therapy), supporting Behavior Technicians (BTs) and quality coordination teams delivering ABA therapy to children with autism across the U.S. This role plays a key part in onboarding, training, and maintaining compliance within the CentralReach system, while supporting the broader quality coordination team.
PLEASE NOTE:
Working Hours: Monday – Friday | 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST (4:00 PM – 01:00 AM South African time – subject to daylight savings). 
Public Holidays: This role requires working on both South African and U.S. public holidays (compensation for SA public holidays in accordance with the BCEA). 
Internet Requirements: A fixed fibre line with a minimum speed of 25 Mbps (upload & download) and wired Ethernet capability is mandatory. Applicants without a fixed fibre line cannot be considered.
Power Backup: Reliable backup required to manage load shedding or outages. Applicants without a power backup cannot be considered.
Work Environment: Fully remote.
Key Responsibilities:
Training & Onboarding
Assist in developing CentralReach training materials, including PowerPoint presentations and Loom video tutorials 
Deliver training sessions to new and existing BTs and provide CR-related training for Quality Coordinators (e.g., adding data, converting timesheets, signing amended notes) 
Take ownership of the NoteGuard platform, including ongoing training and issue resolution 
CentralReach & Session Management
Monitor session note conversion status and follow up with BTs regarding overdue, incorrect, or incomplete sessions 
Guide BTs on how to backdate and correct session data, including meeting with them to amend converted notes as required 
Send session conversion reminders via text, email (CC’ing Giti), and voicemail 
Scheduling & Coordination
Schedule appointments with BTs assigned to new or existing cases 
Update and manage ClickUp cards by BT status (e.g., scheduled, unresponsive, needs assistance, cancelled cases) 
Liaise with managers to connect therapists with assigned teams and escalate BT-related issues by creating and managing Para tickets based on urgency and priority 
Reporting & Administration
Conduct daily check-ins with Giti and refer payroll-related BT queries when appropriate 
Edit converted timesheets when session times require correction 
Compile and submit detailed end-of-day reports outlining daily activities, follow-ups, and outstanding issues
Industry / Technical Experience
Mandatory: Hands-on experience with CentralReach and supporting ABA therapy or Behaviour Technicians 
Experience in U.S. healthcare administration or a similar operational role is advantageous 
Experience using ClickUp or similar task/project management tools 
Comfortable creating training documentation and Loom video walkthroughs 
Soft Skills
Excellent verbal and written English communication 
Highly organized, detail-oriented, able to manage multiple follow-ups simultaneously 
Confident trainer with the ability to explain systems clearly and patiently 
Proactive, reliable, and comfortable working independently in a remote environment 
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to escalate issues appropriately 
Technical Skills
Strong computer literacy, including MS Office and Outlook 
Solid understanding of CentralReach data entry, session conversion, scheduling, and reporting
If you are not contacted within 14 working days, please consider your application unsuccessful.
ai_core_responsibilities: The CentralReach Support Specialist will assist in onboarding and training Behavior Technicians while ensuring compliance within the CentralReach system. They will also monitor session note conversion and manage scheduling and coordination tasks.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have hands-on experience with CentralReach and supporting ABA therapy or Behavior Technicians. Strong communication skills and the ability to create training documentation are also essential.

title: CentralReach Support Specialist (ABA Therapy) - EST Hrs (Remote)
description_text:
ISTA Personnel Solutions is a dynamic and fast-growing BPO company. We are not a recruitment agency - we operate as a dedicated extension of our clients’ teams, delivering high-quality service with precision, efficiency, and a personal touch.
Join our team as a CentralReach Support Specialist (ABA Therapy), supporting Behavior Technicians (BTs) and quality coordination teams delivering ABA therapy to children with autism across the U.S. This role plays a key part in onboarding, training, and maintaining compliance within the CentralReach system, while supporting the broader quality coordination team.
PLEASE NOTE:
Working Hours: Monday – Friday | 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST (4:00 PM – 01:00 AM South African time – subject to daylight savings). 
Public Holidays: This role requires working on both South African and U.S. public holidays (compensation for SA public holidays in accordance with the BCEA). 
Internet Requirements: A fixed fibre line with a minimum speed of 25 Mbps (upload & download) and wired Ethernet capability is mandatory. Applicants without a fixed fibre line cannot be considered.
Power Backup: Reliable backup required to manage load shedding or outages. Applicants without a power backup cannot be considered.
Work Environment: Fully remote.
Key Responsibilities:
Training & Onboarding
Assist in developing CentralReach training materials, including PowerPoint presentations and Loom video tutorials 
Deliver training sessions to new and existing BTs and provide CR-related training for Quality Coordinators (e.g., adding data, converting timesheets, signing amended notes) 
Take ownership of the NoteGuard platform, including ongoing training and issue resolution 
CentralReach & Session Management
Monitor session note conversion status and follow up with BTs regarding overdue, incorrect, or incomplete sessions 
Guide BTs on how to backdate and correct session data, including meeting with them to amend converted notes as required 
Send session conversion reminders via text, email (CC’ing Giti), and voicemail 
Scheduling & Coordination
Schedule appointments with BTs assigned to new or existing cases 
Update and manage ClickUp cards by BT status (e.g., scheduled, unresponsive, needs assistance, cancelled cases) 
Liaise with managers to connect therapists with assigned teams and escalate BT-related issues by creating and managing Para tickets based on urgency and priority 
Reporting & Administration
Conduct daily check-ins with Giti and refer payroll-related BT queries when appropriate 
Edit converted timesheets when session times require correction 
Compile and submit detailed end-of-day reports outlining daily activities, follow-ups, and outstanding issues
Industry / Technical Experience
Mandatory: Hands-on experience with CentralReach and supporting ABA therapy or Behaviour Technicians 
Experience in U.S. healthcare administration or a similar operational role is advantageous 
Experience using ClickUp or similar task/project management tools 
Comfortable creating training documentation and Loom video walkthroughs 
Soft Skills
Excellent verbal and written English communication 
Highly organized, detail-oriented, able to manage multiple follow-ups simultaneously 
Confident trainer with the ability to explain systems clearly and patiently 
Proactive, reliable, and comfortable working independently in a remote environment 
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to escalate issues appropriately 
Technical Skills
Strong computer literacy, including MS Office and Outlook 
Solid understanding of CentralReach data entry, session conversion, scheduling, and reporting
If you are not contacted within 14 working days, please consider your application unsuccessful.

title: Lead DevOps Engineer
description_text:
About ITRex 
THE PLACE
ITRex - AI pioneers who build systems that actually work in the real world, not just in demos. We're 200+ people spread across the US and Europe, creating solutions for companies like P&G and Shutterstock. We keep it simple, build it right, and focus on what works.
THE PEOPLE
We're the kind of people who don't ignore messages in Slack, who jump in to help when you're stuck on a problem, and who offer solutions instead of blame when things go sideways. We believe in openness, accountability, and having each other's backs. No office politics, no hidden agendas - just people who care about doing good work together and supporting each other to get there.
About the job
We are seeking a skilled Lead DevOps Engineer, who will implement and maintain end-to-end CI/CD pipelines using GitHub, with a strong focus on test-driven deployment, automated release management, and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) practices.
The ideal candidate will containerize services using Docker, manage build, compilation, and publishing workflows for JavaScript, TypeScript, and C++ packages, and design robust multi-language build pipelines leveraging CMake or similar build tools.
The role also includes automating build, tagging, and publication processes across web, desktop, and mobile platforms to ensure consistent and traceable releases. Deep expertise in Linux system administration, networking, and IaC tools is essential to deliver scalable, secure, and highly available deployments.
Your Responsibilities
Lead the design, architecture, and management of CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions (and similar tools), ensuring fast, reliable, and reproducible software delivery.
Implement and enforce test-driven deployment systems, integrating automated testing, validation, and monitoring to maintain code quality and accelerate feedback cycles.
Containerize applications and microservices with Docker, optimize image builds, and manage deployment pipelines for distributed environments.
Oversee the build, packaging, and publishing lifecycle for JavaScript, TypeScript, and C++ packages, including versioning, semantic tagging, and NPM or internal registry publication.
Develop and maintain cross-platform build pipelines using CMake or equivalent tools, ensuring consistent compilation and release workflows across web, desktop, and mobile.
Automate end-to-end release processes, including tagging, building, signing, and distributing mobile, web, and desktop applications.
Define and manage Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to provision and maintain reliable, scalable, and secure infrastructure environments.
Collaborate closely with development, QA, and operations teams to troubleshoot deployment issues, optimize performance, and improve release reliability.
Continuously improve observability and feedback loops, leveraging monitoring and alerting systems to maintain operational excellence.
5+ years of hands-on experience in a DevOps, CI/CD, or Release Engineering role
Strong knowledge of AWS cloud, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), shell scripting
Knowledge of Docker, including image creation, registry management, and basic orchestration patterns
Proven experience in designing, architecting, and maintaining CI/CD pipelines, preferably using GitHub Actions (or similar tools)
Experience with cross-platform build and release automation (web, desktop, and mobile)
Experience in automating tagging, versioning, signing and artifact distribution processes
Experience managing Release workflows across web, desktop, and mobile, for cloud and on-premise systems
Ability to design robust multi-language build pipelines leveraging CMake or similar build tools
Hands-on experience with compilation and publishing workflows for JavaScript, TypeScript and C++ packages
Experience in integration of automated testing, validation, and monitoring
Nice to have
Deep understanding of C++ build systems, especially CMake
Coding experience, especially in C++, JavaScript, and TypeScript
Experience building and supporting AI/ML pipelines (training and deploying models) using PyTorch or TensorFlow
Strong Linux system administration and networking skills
Experience setting up monitoring and alerting systems
Familiarity with tools like Prometheus, Grafana, or ELK for CI/CD processes tracking
Background in secure environments like fintech, blockchain, or distributed systems
Knowledge of other clouds like GCP or Azure
English level: B2 or higher
What We Offer 
Why people stay
First, the foundation:
Remote flexibility: Work where and how you work best - we trust you to deliver
Fair compensation: Competitive salary + benefits that matter (medical, learning)
Then, the growth:
Ownership opportunities: See a problem worth solving? Own it. We back smart risks over bureaucratic safety
AI enhancement: We leverage AI to make you faster and stronger - complementing your abilities, not replacing them
Learning investment: English classes, professional development, well-being support
Career progression: Real paths up, not just sideways shuffling
Finally, the people:
Responsive teammates: No ignored Slacks, no "not my problem" attitudes
Supportive culture: When you're stuck, people help. When things break, we fix them together
Human connections: Regular meetups, tech talks, and actual relationships beyond work
Curious? We are too. Let's talk
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead the design and management of CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, ensuring reliable software delivery. Collaborate with development, QA, and operations teams to troubleshoot deployment issues and improve release reliability.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate should have 5+ years of experience in a DevOps role with strong knowledge of AWS and Infrastructure as Code. Experience with Docker, CI/CD pipeline design, and cross-platform build automation is essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: We turn AI ambition into working systems — Generative AI, data, and everything in between
linkedin_org_locations: Aliso Viejo Pkwy, Aliso Viejo, California 92656, US
Warsaw, PL
linkedin_org_description:
ITRex is an AI consulting and implementation company that builds systems meant to work in the real world, at scale, and under pressure.

Generative AI is a core focus, but our expertise spans the full AI and data stack — from classical machine learning and computer vision to autonomous agents and data infrastructure. Our solutions aren’t just prototypes. They're built to integrate, scale, and deliver. Leaders come to us to explore what’s possible — and then make it real.

Gen AI Solution Development & Deployment
– LLM integration and deployment (GPT, Claude, Gemini, LLaMA, Mistral)
– Production-grade RAG with proprietary data
– AI copilots for knowledge, support, operations
– SLMs for edge, low-latency, and private environments
– Autonomous agents for multi-step workflows
– Prompt engineering for efficiency and control

AI-Ready Infrastructure & Governance
– Vector DBs and pipelines (Pinecone, FAISS, Weaviate)
– Unstructured data processing and ingestion
– LLMOps for monitoring and observability
– Built-in compliance: HIPAA, SOC2, EU AI Act

Strategic AI Enablement
– Readiness assessments and use case roadmaps
– PoCs with a path to production
– Risk control: retrieval fallback, prompt audits, trust layers

We support clients in healthcare, biotech, pharma, retail, logistics, and manufacturing — where AI must be scalable, accurate, and compliant. With 500+ solutions delivered since 2009, clients like P&G, Shutterstock, WorkFusion, Dollar Shave Club, Turvo, and Noom trust us to deliver AI that works.

Bring us your challenge. Let’s make it real

title: Business and Science Graduate Scheme - UK Wide Travel
description_text:
Rentokil Graduate Pest Control Technician - Travelling Role
Join Our Team and Make a Difference!
Enjoy problem-solving and helping customers? Become a Pest Control Technician with Rentokil in England and Wales. Full training provided, no experience necessary. Apply now!
We are currently seeking a Pest Control Technician to join our dedicated team of travelling Technicians, covering England and Wales. If you enjoy managing your own schedule and solving unique customer problems in person, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!
Why Join Rentokil?
Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £26,733 per annum plus an inconvenience allowance of £5000 per annum due to travelling and staying away from home. 
Expected OTE: £32,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available.
Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards.
Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary.
Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers’ pest control needs.
Graduate Pest Control Technician Role:
Our Graduate Pest Control Technicians joining our specialist hit squad supporting key branches across the UK, working on service improvement. You will be working within different branches across the UK, therefore, a commitment to working and staying away from home during your working week is required. You'll provide effective solutions for residential and commercial pest control, including rodent control, insect control, and bird control, ensuring excellent customer service while resolving pest problems
Weekly Travel Pattern: Expect to travel to your hotel on Sunday, work in the assigned location Monday to Friday, and travel home on Friday, aiming to be back by 2-3 pm for end-of-week admin.
Nationwide Scope: While a lot of work is in London and the South East, locations could be anywhere in the UK, often requiring long-distance drives on Sundays and Fridays.
You'll start as a Graduate Pest Control Technician, gaining invaluable frontline experience over 6-12 months. You'll receive world-class, industry-recognised training leading to a globally respected Pest Control Qualification – a valuable asset for your entire career! Key responsibilities include:
Inspecting premises and identifying risks.
Tackling pest problems effectively.
Offering expert advice on hygiene and prevention.
Completing accurate reports & providing exceptional customer service.
Beyond the Field: Your Tailored Career Pathway & Progression
This travelling role offers unparalleled networking opportunities and typically leads to progression. After mastering your skills, the next 12-36 months unlock exciting opportunities to shape your own future within Rentokil Initial. Our structured development programme provides progression opportunities into diverse roles such as:
Sales Surveyor
Field Biologist
Service Team Leader
Key Account Manager
Rentokil offers you the platform to follow your own career path while constantly learning on the job and developing your career.
A minimum 2.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality, or Tourism.
A full UK driving licence.
A desire to work hard and have fun while meeting and interacting with customers daily.
Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs.
You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for.
Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes.
Access to a company vehicle and fuel card.
Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business.
Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme.
Enrolment in our company pension scheme.
Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards.
Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers.
Leads Commission scheme available - although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads.
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends.
Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service.
Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial.
A Company Putting “People First”

Rentokil is the world’s leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world’s leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world’s leading provider of plants and scenting.
As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.

title: Home Health Occupational Therapist
description_text:
Parx Home Health Care is looking for an Occupational Therapist to join our team. 
The home health therapist will be responsible for providing high quality and coordinated care to patients in their homes and communities. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in home health care, an understanding of state and federal regulations, and a passion for helping patients live their best lives.
Responsibilities
Collaborate with the interdisciplinary team to develop comprehensive care plans for each client, tailored to their specific needs and goals
Evaluate and treat clients with physical and cognitive limitations to help them improve their daily living skills, range of motion, strength, and balance
Utilize adaptive equipment and recommend modifications to the client's environment to enhance their independence and safety
Educate clients and their caregivers on ways to manage their condition and prevent future injuries
Maintain accurate and timely documentation of client assessments, progress notes, and care plans
Stay current with new developments and research in occupational therapy
Adhere to professional, ethical, and legal standards of practice
Adhere to all applicable laws, regulations, and ethical standards of nursing practice
Active license to practice Occupational Therapy in the state of Florida
Minimum of 1 year of experience working as an Occupational Therapist
Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of an interdisciplinary team
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills
Ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks effectively
Passionate about providing high-quality, patient-centered care
ai_core_responsibilities: The Occupational Therapist will collaborate with the interdisciplinary team to create comprehensive, tailored care plans and evaluate/treat clients with physical and cognitive limitations to enhance daily living skills, range of motion, strength, and balance. Responsibilities also include utilizing adaptive equipment, educating clients/caregivers, and maintaining accurate documentation.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess an active license to practice Occupational Therapy in Florida and have a minimum of one year of experience as an Occupational Therapist. The role requires the ability to work both independently and collaboratively, along with excellent communication and critical thinking skills.
