title: Composite Trimmer (Weekend Shift)
description_text:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is what happens when history, purpose and daring talent come together. Backed by TWG Global and GM, our team is uniquely positioned to disrupt Formula 1®, bringing a fresh perspective and an unrelenting drive for success. We have the energy of a start-up, with the ideas and originality of a business that always wants to lead, never wants to follow. 
We’re building everything from the ground up, from a high-performance car to an inclusive, values-driven culture. We show bold ambition. We combine leadership in innovation with excellence in execution. We are one team. We have the freedom to think differently, the opportunity to shape process and practice, an ego-free environment where people thrive on being challenged by those around them. A historic name behind us. Career-defining moments ahead. 
A New Chapter Begins.
Please Note: This is a Weekend Shift position working 3 days x 12 hours rotated between Friday – Sunday and Saturday – Monday from 6am – 6:30pm
Fueled by bold ambition:
Play your part in getting us on the grid.
Closing Date: 13th February 2026
As a Composite Trimmer at The Cadillac Formula 1® Team, you will play a crucial role in the finishing processes of composite components. You will be responsible for trimming, sanding, and preparing components for assembly, ensuring that all work is completed to the highest quality standards. This position requires attention to detail and a commitment to quality workmanship.
· Trim and finish composite components according to engineering specifications and quality standards
· Utilize hand and power tools effectively and safely during the trimming process
· Inspect finished components for defects, ensuring compliance with quality requirements
· Collaborate with assembly and production teams to ensure timely workflow
· Maintain a clean and organized workspace, adhering to safety protocols
· Assist in identifying areas for improvement in the trimming process
· Participate in training and development to enhance technical skills related to composite trimming and assembly
Driven by high performance:
What do you need to bring to the team?
· Experience in composite trimming/fitting, preferably within the F1®/motorsport or aerospace sectors
· Ability to read and Interpret engineering drawings
· A methodical and thorough approach to with excellent attention to detail with a can-do attitude
· Proficiency in using hand and power tools associated with composite trimming processes
· Strong attention to detail and commitment to producing high-quality work
· Ability to work effectively in a team and communicate clearly
· Experience in a fast-paced and dynamic environment with a willingness to be flexible during busy periods
· Experience of assembling Class A components while following QA documentation and company processes
· Experience of in line inspection/scanning would be desirable
· Strong problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to tasks
· Willingness to learn and adapt in a continually evolving industry
· Adherence to safety and quality standards in all aspects of work
A team like no other.
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team challenges conventions and redefines success through bold ambition, cutting-edge innovation, and an unwavering commitment to precision and excellence—on and off the track. This includes offering industry-leading pension, generous time off and, as part of a global brand, huge potential for career development. 

Please be aware that we will be reviewing applicants on a rolling basis and this job posting will close once a suitable candidate is identified. We encourage all interested individuals to submit their application as soon as possible. 
As an equal opportunities employer, we are committed to the equal treatment of all current and prospective employees and does not condone discrimination on the basis of age, disability, sex, sexual orientation, pregnancy or maternity, race or ethnicity, religion or belief, gender identity or marriage and civil partnership. We aspire to have a diverse and inclusive workplace and strongly encourage suitably qualified applicants from a wide range of backgrounds to apply.
At The Cadillac Formula 1®, all Team Members are expected to actively support and uphold our policies and procedures, including those focused on Environmental responsibility, Sustainability initiatives, Inclusion and Health and Safety practices.
Please note that additional security checks may be required as part of the recruitment process. This may include a background check covering a minimum of the past five years and a criminal record check.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Composite Trimmer will be responsible for trimming, sanding, and preparing composite components for assembly, ensuring all work meets the highest quality standards according to engineering specifications. This role requires utilizing hand and power tools safely and inspecting finished components for defects while collaborating with production teams.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess experience in composite trimming/fitting, preferably in F1/motorsport or aerospace, and demonstrate the ability to read and interpret engineering drawings. Essential requirements include proficiency with trimming tools, strong attention to detail, and the ability to work effectively in a team within a fast-paced environment.
linkedin_org_slogan: The Original American F1® Team
linkedin_org_description:
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team is The American Original—bringing a bold, American-led vision to the future of Formula 1™. Set to join the grid in 2026, the team is built from the ground up and designed for the global stage—driven by innovation, shaped for culture and engineered to deliver long-term brand affinity.
 
With operational hubs in Fishers (Indiana), Silverstone (UK) & Cologne (GER), we're creating a platform that blends world-class racing performance with commercial and cultural relevance. 
 
Powered by the strategic and commercial leadership of TWG Motorsports and the technical excellence of General Motors, the team combines deep motorsport expertise, cross-platform capability and a legacy of American engineering. At its heart is Cadillac—a brand defined by bold ambition, design innovation and modern American luxury.
 
The Cadillac Formula 1® Team reflects the ambition, optimism and ingenuity that define the American spirit—balanced with global sensibility and deep respect for the sport’s legacy.

title: Business and Science Graduate Scheme - UK Wide Travel
description_text:
Rentokil Graduate Pest Control Technician - Travelling Role
Join Our Team and Make a Difference!
Enjoy problem-solving and helping customers? Become a Pest Control Technician with Rentokil in England and Wales. Full training provided, no experience necessary. Apply now!
We are currently seeking a Pest Control Technician to join our dedicated team of travelling Technicians, covering England and Wales. If you enjoy managing your own schedule and solving unique customer problems in person, this could be the perfect opportunity for you!
Why Join Rentokil?
Competitive Salary Package: Start with a basic salary of £26,733 per annum plus an inconvenience allowance of £5000 per annum due to travelling and staying away from home. 
Expected OTE: £32,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available.
Benefits: Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform, and RI Rewards.
Work-Life Balance: Full-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (40 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary.
Industry-Leading Training: Receive top-notch training to support our customers’ pest control needs.
Graduate Pest Control Technician Role:
Our Graduate Pest Control Technicians joining our specialist hit squad supporting key branches across the UK, working on service improvement. You will be working within different branches across the UK, therefore, a commitment to working and staying away from home during your working week is required. You'll provide effective solutions for residential and commercial pest control, including rodent control, insect control, and bird control, ensuring excellent customer service while resolving pest problems
Weekly Travel Pattern: Expect to travel to your hotel on Sunday, work in the assigned location Monday to Friday, and travel home on Friday, aiming to be back by 2-3 pm for end-of-week admin.
Nationwide Scope: While a lot of work is in London and the South East, locations could be anywhere in the UK, often requiring long-distance drives on Sundays and Fridays.
You'll start as a Graduate Pest Control Technician, gaining invaluable frontline experience over 6-12 months. You'll receive world-class, industry-recognised training leading to a globally respected Pest Control Qualification – a valuable asset for your entire career! Key responsibilities include:
Inspecting premises and identifying risks.
Tackling pest problems effectively.
Offering expert advice on hygiene and prevention.
Completing accurate reports & providing exceptional customer service.
Beyond the Field: Your Tailored Career Pathway & Progression
This travelling role offers unparalleled networking opportunities and typically leads to progression. After mastering your skills, the next 12-36 months unlock exciting opportunities to shape your own future within Rentokil Initial. Our structured development programme provides progression opportunities into diverse roles such as:
Sales Surveyor
Field Biologist
Service Team Leader
Key Account Manager
Rentokil offers you the platform to follow your own career path while constantly learning on the job and developing your career.
A minimum 2.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality, or Tourism.
A full UK driving licence.
A desire to work hard and have fun while meeting and interacting with customers daily.
Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs.
You may be required to pass a DBS check depending on the role you have applied for.
Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes.
Access to a company vehicle and fuel card.
Salary grading system - linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business.
Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme.
Enrolment in our company pension scheme.
Exciting discounts and cashback offers from over 3,000 retailers with RI Rewards.
Overtime opportunities - potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers.
Leads Commission scheme available - although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads.
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) - free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends.
Long service recognition - includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service.
Refer a Friend - earn up to £1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial.
A Company Putting “People First”
Rentokil Initial (FTSE100) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world’s leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world’s leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world’s leading provider of plants and scenting.
As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family.
Our Social Links
Website
LinkedIn
Facebook
Instagram
Rentokil Initial are an equal opportunities employer and are committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data view our careers privacy policy here
ai_core_responsibilities: Technicians will inspect premises, identify risks, tackle pest problems effectively across residential and commercial settings, and offer expert advice on hygiene and prevention. Key duties also involve completing accurate reports and providing exceptional customer service while resolving pest issues.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a minimum 2.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality, or Tourism, along with a full UK driving license. A strong desire to work hard, interact daily with customers, and flexibility to support business needs are also essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Global leaders in Pest Control, Hygiene, Plants & Scenting services
linkedin_org_locations: Compass House, Manor Royal, Crawley, Sussex RH10 9PY, GB
linkedin_org_description:
Rentokil Initial plc employs c.68,500 people across 89 countries - offering the experience and expertise of a multi-national organisation, whilst delivering services with the agility and characteristics of a local business. 

As world leaders in Pest Control and Hygiene & Well-being services, we deliver services that protect people and enhance lives, to commercial and private customers worldwide.

Rentokil Initial plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange (FTSE 100).

title: Habitat Delivery Site Manager
description_text:
About us
At Environment Bank, we’re working together to help restore natural habitats across England. With thousands of acres of new habitat creation already underway, our projects are delivering biodiversity gains and helping vital ecosystems to recover and flourish. 
Our diverse team includes ecologists, land managers, habitat specialists, geospatial analysts, planning advisors, account managers, digital creatives, legal experts, and more. 
From our site managers to our sales and marketing teams, everyone at Environment Bank is a champion for our values, working towards our goal of reversing biodiversity loss. 
Environment Bank is a market-leading organisation in a sector that’s experiencing rapid growth. We’ve already built a highly skilled team of experts and we’re actively looking to expand our team in the coming months. 
We’re incredibly proud of all we achieved so far and we’re still growing. If you'd like to be part of a driven organisation that's making a real difference, find your next role with the team at Environment Bank.
About the role
As a site manager in the Habitat creation team, you will be responsible for delivering habitat creation and enhancement projects safely, on-time and to budget. You would work closely with our team of Land Managers, Ecologists and Applied- Ecologists to deliver habitat creation works across your region. Works would include creation and enhancement of a range of habitats including, woodland and scrub, species rich grassland, wetland, and associated infrastructure works.
Responsibilities
The Site Manager is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of assigned habitat sites or delivery packages, including:
Day-to-day planning and execution of delivery activities, tracking progress via site level delivery plans, reporting against agreed milestones and targets
Accurate planning and tracking of budgets against agreed spend profiles and updating forecasts for approval to variance
Appointment of contractors and suppliers in line with EB policies and requirements, agreeing contract terms
Contractor and supplier coordination on site
Management of on-site progress, quality and issue resolution, escalating risks, issues and changes where impacts exceed agreed tolerances
Accurate reporting of delivery progress, risks and issues
Reviewing Construction Phase Plan or equivalent (including Incident Response Plan), reviewing delivery methodology and providing input to the project risk register. 
Managing contractor KPIs 
Ensure delivery meets ecological, regulatory, contractual and quality requirements
Support health, safety and environmental compliance across all site activities
Address defects, non-conformances and delivery issues promptly and effectively
Support value-for-money delivery without compromising ecological or delivery outcomes
Maintain high quality relationships with landowners, their contractors, and other stakeholders.
EDI & Belonging
At the heart of Environment Bank are the people, who make it all possible. We employ individuals, who share the same passion to live our Environment Bank mission and values. 
At Environment Bank we believe that fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace makes us more innovative, dynamic, and competitive. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, ethnicities, cultures, and experiences. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone is valued and respected, free from discrimination based on race, religion, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, gender identity or expression, or any other legally protected status.
Essential Skills and Experience
Practical delivery experience in land-based, construction, infrastructure or habitat delivery environments
Experience managing contractors and on-site works
Experience of working with delivery plans, schedules and delivery constraints
Experience of working directly with farmers and/or landowners, in a professional capacity.
Strong interpersonal skills with both internal and external stakeholders.
Self-motivated and excellent time management skills. 
Ability to work as part of team. 
Strong organisation and planning capability
Clear communication and reporting discipline
Practical problem-solving in live delivery environments
Ability to balance quality, time and cost under site-level pressures
Full UK clean driving license and willingness to travel for regional site visits and events
Desirable Skills and Experience
Experience in habitat creation, land management or environmental delivery
Familiarity with regulated delivery environments or assurance-led programmes
Awareness of APM-aligned project controls (plans, risks and reporting)
A Health and Safety qualification e.g. NEBOSH/IOSH.
As an organisation, we believe that work should be fulfilling, inspiring, and balanced, so we’re creating a space where our team can thrive, both personally and professionally – providing flexible working arrangements, mental wellbeing support, and more. 
We offer... 
🎁 Performance-based annual bonus up to 10% of salary 
💸 Pension scheme with employer-matched contributions at 6% of salary 
🏖️ 30 days of annual leave, plus paid bank holidays, birthday, and wellbeing day 
➕ Option to purchase 5 additional days of annual leave after probation
🏡 Hybrid flexible working options 
💼 Regional and departmental team co-working days 
🌞 Expenses-paid whole company meet twice a year 
🎗️ Team fundraising and charity events 
🎓 Ongoing career development with training and mentoring 
🩺 Medical cash back scheme to cover everyday healthcare costs 
‍🧠 Employee assistance mental wellbeing support 
❤️‍🩹️ Enhanced sickness pay allowance
ai_benefits: Option to purchase additional annual leave
ai_core_responsibilities: The Site Manager is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of assigned habitat sites or delivery packages, ensuring works are delivered safely, on-time, and within budget. This includes day-to-day planning, execution, budget tracking, contractor management, and reporting on progress, risks, and quality against ecological and contractual requirements.
ai_requirements_summary: Essential requirements include practical delivery experience in land-based, construction, infrastructure, or habitat delivery environments, along with proven experience managing contractors and on-site works using delivery plans and schedules. Candidates must also possess strong interpersonal skills, self-motivation, planning capability, and a full UK clean driving license.
linkedin_org_slogan: The biodiversity experts
linkedin_org_locations: Baird Lane, The Catalyst, York, England YO10 5GA, GB
linkedin_org_description:
England’s leading biodiversity gains provider, Environment Bank has established the broadest network of BNG Habitat Banks - giving developers access to Biodiversity Units nationwide.

Our biodiversity experts work in partnership with local landowners to transform parcels of low-biodiversity, low-productive farmland into diverse spaces for nature to thrive.

Our Habitat Banks support sustainable agriculture and provide a secure, long-term income for farmers. We create new meadows, scrub, woodland, ponds, rivers, and other valuable habitats to suit the local landscape.

We also create high-impact nature projects across hundreds of acres. Businesses can support our mission to restore lost biodiversity and heal damaged ecosystems by purchasing Nature Shares in our projects - helping them to become nature-positive.

linkedin_org_description:
England’s leading biodiversity gains provider, Environment Bank has established the broadest network of BNG Habitat Banks - giving developers access to Biodiversity Units nationwide.

Our biodiversity experts work in partnership with local landowners to transform parcels of low-biodiversity, low-productive farmland into diverse spaces for nature to thrive.

Our Habitat Banks support sustainable agriculture and provide a secure, long-term income for farmers. We create new meadows, scrub, woodland, ponds, rivers, and other valuable habitats to suit the local landscape.

We also create high-impact nature projects across hundreds of acres. Businesses can support our mission to restore lost biodiversity and heal damaged ecosystems by purchasing Nature Shares in our projects - helping them to become nature-positive.

title: Habitat Delivery Site Manager
description_text:
About us
At Environment Bank, we’re working together to help restore natural habitats across England. With thousands of acres of new habitat creation already underway, our projects are delivering biodiversity gains and helping vital ecosystems to recover and flourish. 
Our diverse team includes ecologists, land managers, habitat specialists, geospatial analysts, planning advisors, account managers, digital creatives, legal experts, and more. 
From our site managers to our sales and marketing teams, everyone at Environment Bank is a champion for our values, working towards our goal of reversing biodiversity loss. 
Environment Bank is a market-leading organisation in a sector that’s experiencing rapid growth. We’ve already built a highly skilled team of experts and we’re actively looking to expand our team in the coming months. 
We’re incredibly proud of all we achieved so far and we’re still growing. If you'd like to be part of a driven organisation that's making a real difference, find your next role with the team at Environment Bank.
About the role
As a site manager in the Habitat creation team, you will be responsible for delivering habitat creation and enhancement projects safely, on-time and to budget. You would work closely with our team of Land Managers, Ecologists and Applied- Ecologists to deliver habitat creation works across your region. Works would include creation and enhancement of a range of habitats including, woodland and scrub, species rich grassland, wetland, and associated infrastructure works.
Responsibilities
The Site Manager is responsible for the end-to-end delivery of assigned habitat sites or delivery packages, including:
Day-to-day planning and execution of delivery activities, tracking progress via site level delivery plans, reporting against agreed milestones and targets
Accurate planning and tracking of budgets against agreed spend profiles and updating forecasts for approval to variance
Appointment of contractors and suppliers in line with EB policies and requirements, agreeing contract terms
Contractor and supplier coordination on site
Management of on-site progress, quality and issue resolution, escalating risks, issues and changes where impacts exceed agreed tolerances
Accurate reporting of delivery progress, risks and issues
Reviewing Construction Phase Plan or equivalent (including Incident Response Plan), reviewing delivery methodology and providing input to the project risk register. 
Managing contractor KPIs 
Ensure delivery meets ecological, regulatory, contractual and quality requirements
Support health, safety and environmental compliance across all site activities
Address defects, non-conformances and delivery issues promptly and effectively
Support value-for-money delivery without compromising ecological or delivery outcomes
Maintain high quality relationships with landowners, their contractors, and other stakeholders.
EDI & Belonging
At the heart of Environment Bank are the people, who make it all possible. We employ individuals, who share the same passion to live our Environment Bank mission and values. 
At Environment Bank we believe that fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace makes us more innovative, dynamic, and competitive. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, ethnicities, cultures, and experiences. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone is valued and respected, free from discrimination based on race, religion, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, gender identity or expression, or any other legally protected status.
Essential Skills and Experience
Practical delivery experience in land-based, construction, infrastructure or habitat delivery environments
Experience managing contractors and on-site works
Experience of working with delivery plans, schedules and delivery constraints
Experience of working directly with farmers and/or landowners, in a professional capacity.
Strong interpersonal skills with both internal and external stakeholders.
Self-motivated and excellent time management skills. 
Ability to work as part of team. 
Strong organisation and planning capability
Clear communication and reporting discipline
Practical problem-solving in live delivery environments
Ability to balance quality, time and cost under site-level pressures
Full UK clean driving license and willingness to travel for regional site visits and events
Desirable Skills and Experience
Experience in habitat creation, land management or environmental delivery
Familiarity with regulated delivery environments or assurance-led programmes
Awareness of APM-aligned project controls (plans, risks and reporting)
A Health and Safety qualification e.g. NEBOSH/IOSH.
As an organisation, we believe that work should be fulfilling, inspiring, and balanced, so we’re creating a space where our team can thrive, both personally and professionally – providing flexible working arrangements, mental wellbeing support, and more. 
We offer... 
🎁 Performance-based annual bonus up to 10% of salary 
💸 Pension scheme with employer-matched contributions at 6% of salary 
🏖️ 30 days of annual leave, plus paid bank holidays, birthday, and wellbeing day 
➕ Option to purchase 5 additional days of annual leave after probation
🏡 Hybrid flexible working options 
💼 Regional and departmental team co-working days 
🌞 Expenses-paid whole company meet twice a year 
🎗️ Team fundraising and charity events 
🎓 Ongoing career development with training and mentoring 
🩺 Medical cash back scheme to cover everyday healthcare costs 
‍🧠 Employee assistance mental wellbeing support 
❤️‍🩹️ Enhanced sickness pay allowance

title: Commodity Operations Analyst
description_text:
About Javelin
Javelin Global Commodities is a leading global commodities marketing, trading, logistics services and investment firm with head offices in London and wider global offices in Singapore, China, New York, Dallas, San Diego, St Louis, Switzerland, India, Australia, Canada, Dubai and Poland. Javelin was founded in 2015 and now operates across six continents with respect to more than 20 different commodities across thermal coal, metallurgical coal, iron ore, steel scrap, metals, hydrocarbons, freight, softs, and renewables.
Established in 2015, Javelin employs over 200 people and is a unique place to grow your career. We take pride in our values, global reach, meritocratic culture and strong work ethic. Our success over the years has been built on our guiding principles of customer service, creativity, transparency, integrity, and responsibility. 
Whilst our footprint grows, we still foster a small-company atmosphere. We have a flat company structure and encourage collaboration across all seniority levels, teams and locations to generate the best ideas. Javelin has a dedicated strategy to support local and meaningful charities through volunteer days, donation matching and other fundraising activities. We are committed to the wellbeing of our colleagues and organise various social evenings, sports clubs and lunchtime activities. Discover more at http://www.javelincommodities.com/. 
The Role
The Operations team at Javelin manages the reconciliation, confirmation, documentation, and settlement of commodity products across the full trade lifecycle. Working closely with Trading, Logistics, Commercial, and Trade Finance teams, Operations ensure that risk is captured accurately and that trades are supported efficiently from execution through settlement.
As the business continues to grow, we are seeking a motivated individual to join the Singapore Operations team, with a primary focus on trade documentation and operational controls, and exposure to trade finance and Letters of Credit. This is a dynamic and hands-on role that will complement the responsibilities of the wider Operations group and support the team in achieving its objectives.
Key Responsibilities
Perform T+1 trade checkouts to ensure accuracy of executed trades
Draft, review, and manage physical and financial commodity contracts 
Liaise closely with Trading and Logistics teams to resolve operational queries and discrepancies
Reconcile OTC and exchange-traded positions
Maintain and update confirmation and reporting databases within internal systems
Support trade lifecycle processes, ensuring timely and accurate settlement
Assist with trade documentation related to import/export transactions, including Letters of Credit where applicable
Liaise with Trade Finance, Commercial, and Operations teams on transaction-related requirements
Monitor and update daily operational reporting
Document operational processes and procedures to support controls and scalability
Assist with operational projects and continuous improvement initiatives
Skills and Experience Required:
We are looking for a self-motivated, proactive candidate who ideally comes with some previous experience in a Commodity Operations role or internship. 
Bachelors degree in a relevant subject preferred
A strong work ethic is required to help drive the team forward and push for technology-based solutions.
Demonstrable organizational skills
Clear communication skills, both written and verbal
Ability to manage and prioritise workload and judge priority and urgency of requests and issues
Excellent attention to detail 
Benefits:
Annual discretionary bonus scheme
Private medical insurance
Life insurance
Income protection
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves performing T+1 trade checkouts, drafting and managing commodity contracts, and liaising with Trading and Logistics teams to resolve operational discrepancies. Responsibilities also include supporting trade lifecycle processes, assisting with import/export documentation, and monitoring operational reporting.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate is a self-motivated and proactive individual, preferably with prior experience or an internship in Commodity Operations. A Bachelor's degree in a relevant subject is preferred, alongside a strong work ethic, excellent organizational skills, clear communication, and meticulous attention to detail.

Javelin Global Commodities is a global commodities marketing, trading, logistics, financing, and advisory company focused on bulk, steel, energy, green carbon, renewable fuels and soft commodities.
Javelin has unparalleled risk management expertise, extensive global network and broad market insight. We are forward thinking, dynamic and place the needs of our clients first. Our team at Javelin is unequivocally 'Moving Commodities Forward'.

Javelin was founded in 2015 by a team dedicated to deliver new, innovative, customer-led solutions to buy and sell commodities. 

Headquartered in London with offices in New York, San Diego, St. Louis, Puerto Rico, Bangalore, Zug, Singapore and Melbourne, we are uniquely positioned to provide superior solutions to our clients globally. 
Javelin operates across 100 countries and six continents with respect to more than 20 different commodities, where we lead the market in supplying commodities to the energy, steel and industrial sectors.

title: Board Certified Physician Reviewers - Interventional Radiology - Remote
description_text:
Dane Street wants you to join our dynamic team of expert reviewers! In this role, you will have the opportunity to utilize your medical expertise to conduct thorough reviews of clinical cases.
This telework opportunity allows you to customize your schedule as a 1099 independent contractor.
Your main task will be to evaluate medical records, provide clinical summaries, engage in peer communications, and answer specific questions posed by our clients. 
We are on the lookout for talented professionals in the following area:
Interventional Radiology 
Be a part of a team that values your skills and dedication to improving patient care. Your expertise is vital to helping us deliver high-quality healthcare assessments.
Board Certified in Interventional Radiology
5+ years of clinical practice experience
Attends all required orientation and training
Maintains proper credentialing, state licenses, and any certifications or requirements necessary to perform the job
Independent consultant role allows for schedule flexibility and predictable work hours. You choose services and case types, dictate volume, and conduct this work based on your scheduled availability.
No doctor/patient relationship is established, and no treatment is provided. These are advisory-only opinions.
Dane Street offers a streamlined case flow and a user-friendly work portal. We facilitate all communication, organize and sort all medical records, support all administrative processes, prep cases extensively, and ensure the quality and timely delivery of all determinations/reports.
A chronological listing of medical records as well as all treatment guidelines used to make a determination are provided in an easy, click-to-view format.
Dane Street offers initial training as well as an ongoing point of contact should you have any questions about completing reviews.
ai_core_responsibilities: The main task involves utilizing medical expertise to conduct thorough reviews of clinical cases, which includes evaluating medical records, providing clinical summaries, engaging in peer communications, and answering specific client questions.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be Board Certified in Interventional Radiology and possess a minimum of 5 years of clinical practice experience. They are also required to maintain proper credentialing, state licenses, and attend all necessary orientation and training sessions.

Dane Street is the industry's fastest growing national IME and Peer Review provider with a panel of board-certified, active-practice physicians in all 50 states. Services are provided to the Workers Compensation, Pharmacy, Disability, Group Health and Auto/Liability lines of business.

Dane Street's Review and Evaluation services provide improved report quality, faster turnaround time and higher adjuster/nurse satisfaction and productivity.

title: Patient Care Coordinator
description_text:
Thanks for checking out our Patient Care Coordinator job post. We are an award-winning, one-on-one, manual therapy-based physical therapy clinic located in Fort Worth, Texas.
Because we’re growing, we are looking for a full-time Patient Care Coordinator to join our team. In this role you’ll be working to follow-up on the leads coming into our office, help with marketing strategy/efforts (via Facebook, YouTube, Google Adwords, Website, Keap, calls, text message), schedule appointments, meet with doctors, field questions, follow-up on inquiries, calling past patients, set up networking opportunities with other businesses, send out information packages, and assist in maintaining the integrity of the company’s database.
You must be a team player and good at multitasking 
This position is multi-faceted and you must be willing to learn new tasks as they arise
You must be detailed oriented
You enjoy being around people, and can hold meaningful conversations with clientsyou may not know for longer than 20 min
You're thoughtful and thorough - you can answer people's questions in a way that increases the likelihood of them becoming a client
You are alert and aware - you're good at recalling names, faces, and personal details - so that our clients always feel welcomed and remembered
You know what it means to provide a warm, welcoming, and world-class greeting and experience for clients
You will be working with insurance and talking to doctors
You have excellent organization and planning skills - you know how to manage schedules and re-prioritize your time so that you can focus on key priorities - without the need for micro-management
You follow through on commitments. You live up to verbal and written agreements without constant reminders
You have the ability to quickly and proficiently understand and absorb new information
You're pro-active and resourceful. You bring new ideas to the company, do things without being told, and can quickly shift and course-correct when things don't go according to plan - in a positive manner and without focusing on the negative
You're coachable, love to learn, and have a fail-forward attitude. You take constructive criticism well and enjoy getting regular feedback that will optimize your skills and performance.
1 Year Medical Office Experience
Paid Time Off
401(k) matching
Health insurance
ai_core_responsibilities: The Patient Care Coordinator will follow up on leads, assist with marketing efforts, schedule appointments, and maintain the company's database. They will also interact with clients and ensure a welcoming experience.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have at least one year of medical office experience and be detail-oriented with strong organizational skills. They must be proactive, coachable, and able to handle multiple tasks effectively.

title: Project Manager- PAYG Ticketing - 18 month FTC -
description_text:
Closing date for applications: 
20th February 2026
What is the purpose of this job?
We believe that the art of project management is the balance of delivering a shared strategic vision with co-ordinating projects across a project team. 
As a Project Manager your role is to deliver high-profile (Strategic in the wider industry and high value) projects and other initiatives across the whole lifecycle within scope, time and budget, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives. You will actively manage and nurture stakeholder relationships whilst following the RDG Project Delivery Framework whilst encouraging others to do the same.

This Project Manager role will be measured by the successful delivery of industry-leading rail projects, including those focused on Pay as You Go Ticketing, accessibility, fares, retail, and back-office systems.

The ideal candidate will have great leadership, communication and organisational skills, as well as the ability to manage cross functional teams successfully.
What can I expect to do in this job? 
Working for the Senior Project Manager and other internal or external stakeholders, you can expect to be involved in the following:
Leading projects – representing them with stakeholders, resolving blockers, whilst coordinating a strong and capable cross-functional team,
With the support of your team, establish, maintain and manage project plans, tracking and monitoring progress and issues and taking necessary actions and decisions.
Develop and maintain productive and professional external and internal senior stakeholder relationships to ensure that programmes are delivering to industry needs and realising the required benefits.
Manage risks, issues and change. Initiate corrective action against identified risks and issues to achieve programme objectives. Manage change considering business benefit and impact on costs and time
Set up the appropriate governance for the Programme in line with RDG’s existing governance structure. Responsible for providing reports and papers for that governance and stakeholders
Line & matrix manage and support project delivery teams in their day-to-day delivery to achieve the performance needed to successfully deliver the objectives of programmes
Adhere to RDG Project Delivery Framework standards and procedures and identify and implement improvements to these standards and procedures.
What experience, skills and knowledge do I need?
We are looking for someone with experience of working as a project manager, demonstrating a strong aptitude for the role and expert programme and project management knowledge.
Some of the sort of specific skills and knowledge we are looking for include:
Broad vocational experience ideally within Transport, Travel, Retail, Utilities, Professional Services
Full membership of the Association for Project Management is desirable.
Experience working with either or both Agile and Prince 2 methodologies, qualified in programme management (e.g. Managing Successful Programmes (MSP)
Delivery of projects within the agreed constraints of time, budget and high-level scope. This includes demonstrated experience in prioritising activities (including where necessary supplier priorities) and ensuring appropriate actions are regarding managing issues and risks and approving changes.
Experience in projects with a proven track record of delivering results in a complex environment, with multiple external stakeholders.
Leading a project with significant budget (£m) responsibility
Ability to make decisions relating to project priorities, change management (e.g. scope, time, contractual), financial and commercial changes.
Experienced in developing project plans for and assuring delivery of priority projects.
Identifying and documenting risks, issues, dependencies, assumptions and decisions and the associated management, with the appropriate action owner, to remediate.
Demonstrated leadership skills, leading and getting the best out of project teams and individuals.
Excellent communication skills, liaising with all senior executives, stakeholders, Industry boards and project team members, through the provision of high-quality multi-media reports, presentations, demonstrations and focus groups to influence decisions, make recommendations and provide support on business management.
The ability to think strategically in your approach to delivery of the project aims.
Why Work for RDG?
We offer a highly competitive package, including:
75% off rail travel for personal and family use, plus international rail discounts.
30 Days annual leave (plus buy/sell options and additional leave for key life events)
Season ticket loan for commuting costs.
Enhanced family leave – 30 weeks full pay for maternity, adoption, surrogacy, and shared parental leave.
Pension scheme – up to 11.58% employer contribution.
Private medical insurance (Vitality PPP) including mental health and specialist care.
Discounted gym membership and access to wellbeing programmes.
Career Development & Progression
You’ll be part of a team driving innovation at a national scale, with the opportunity to influence long-term ticketing strategy. This role provides real ownership, the chance to work with leading-edge technologies and partners, and visibility at senior levels across the rail industry. You’ll also gain wide exposure to transport and government stakeholders, opening up exciting development and progression opportunities within RDG.
Apply Now & Shape the Future of UK Rail
If you are interested in joining RDG as a Project Manager and shaping the future of digital ticketing, we’d love to hear from you!
Click Apply now to start your application and be part of a team that is leading digital innovation in the rail industry.
Please note that we are unable to consider applications from candidates who require visa sponsorship to work in the UK.

Responsibilities include developing project plans, managing risks and issues, and adhering to the RDG Project Delivery Framework.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in project management, ideally within the transport or retail sectors, and familiarity with Agile and Prince 2 methodologies. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills are essential for managing cross-functional teams and delivering results.

title: Planning Technician (PFT) (CUPE 10.26)
description_text:
Scope of Position:
To coordinate development application processes and improvements in order to help meet Planning Act legislative requirements along with providing frontline services to planning and development stakeholders and public, and to provide mapping services for planning applications and planning projects. A portion of the role may include planner and project support, coordination of development analytics and providing planning advice on land use planning matters having regard to the Planning Act, and other related Legislation, Regulations, Policy and planning principles. 
Position Description:
Coordinate the steps and timelines involved in the processing of development applications, such as land use amendments, site plans and draft plans of subdivision and condominium. This includes but not limited to:
The intake of development applications via the County’s software system and other methods
Circulation of applications and technical studies to planners, departments, agencies and uploading via internal systems
Public and stakeholder notifications and issuance of decisions
Coordinating the pre-consultation and site plan review committee agendas and documenting the “record” of requirements and conditions
Prepare and create Planning report mapping for consents, minor variance, zoning amendments, subdivisions, condominiums, Official Plan amendments, and Ontario Land Tribunal hearings. 
Undertake large projects such as creation of mapping for new Zoning By-Laws, Official Plans or other large project specific mapping.
Support in the preparation of staff reports, collection of agency and department comments, and other technical support in relation to development applications.
Assist with long-range and policy initiatives, which may include public engagement.
Coordinate reviews of key development application processes and planning services to ensure Norfolk is at the forefront of new techniques to engage the community and deliver results for stakeholders.
Responsible for responding to general inquiries related to land use planning matters. Coordinating with the department, others and Service Norfolk to find process efficiencies. 
Maintain and foster corporate goals by ensuring public access to and participation in the planning process, including utilization of engagement tools for planning initiatives such as the county’s online platform
Maintains, verifies, and updates the department’s development application tracking system (Cityview). 
Coordinate and provide analytics used for planning, growth or business plan purposes. This could include development applications, residential monitoring, coordination of development with engineering & infrastructure services, annual reporting and other data and information summaries.
Provide back-up assistance to the Committee of Adjustment Secretary Treasurer functions or meeting if needed.
Responsible for coordinating the maintenance and posting of the County’s Official Plan, Zoning By-law and other planning instruments and amendments thereto.
May be required for occasional site inspections in relation to planning applications.
To undertake special projects and other duties as assigned.
Knowledge and Experience:
Minimum four-year post-secondary degree in Land Use Planning preferred or related field such as Geography or Urban Studies.
Over one year, up to and including two years related experience.
GIS understanding through education or experience is required.
Skills and Abilities:
Strong organizational and administrative skills with demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively.
Effective oral and written communication skills.
Strong interpersonal and customer service skills; able to liaise with internal and external stakeholders with excellent judgement and decision-making regarding the sharing of information.
Understanding of relevant Planning and development process related legislation and policy.
Excellent computer skills, including solid knowledge of ArcGIS Pro, Microsoft Office, Adobe, file management. Additional knowledge, familiarity or ability to learn department specific software (CityView or comparable), Geographic Information System software (ArcGIS) and/or imaging/graphic design programs a benefit.
Ability to operate corporate equipment such as: scanner, printers, plotter, copier, digital camera, etc. 
A valid Ontario G driver’s license and access to a reliable vehicle including the ability to provide a drivers abstract when requested during the recruitment process.
Must be aware of safe work practices as they relate to job responsibilities and work environment, and have the basic understanding of the Occupational Health and Safety Act.

linkedin_org_slogan: Working together with our community
linkedin_org_locations: 50 Colborne St. S., Simcoe, Ontario N3Y 4H3, CA
185 Robinson St, Suite 200, Simcoe, ON N3Y 5L6, CA
linkedin_org_description:
Norfolk County is a single-tier municipal government located in Southern Ontario, Canada. Our population of 63,000 residents along Ontario's South Coast of Lake Erie enjoys an excellent quality of life. 

Norfolk County farmers grow more asparagus, tart cherries, ginseng, peppers, pumpkins, and other vegetables than anywhere else in the nation, according to Statistics Canada’s 2016 Census of Agriculture. Norfolk County is also Ontario’s leading grower of asparagus, cabbage, tart cherries, ginseng, peppers, pumpkins, rye,  Award-winning wineries, microbreweries and farm markets highlight Norfolk County’s status as Ontario’s Garden, a diverse agriculture powerhouse. 

We are best known for our warm sandy beaches in Port Dover, Turkey Point and Long Point. Country back roads and beautiful landscapes provide excellent touring and motorcycle routes. Port Dover offers live theatre, boat cruises, and romantic walks at sunset on the pier by the lighthouse. Try Lake Erie perch and other local food delights from Norfolk County at our restaurants. Outdoor adventures include zip-line canopy tours, star-gazing, wilderness suites and camping. There is hunting and fishing in the waters and marshes of Long Point Bay, plus cycling and hiking on our trails. 

Key festivals include the annual Tour de Norfolk cycling event (July), Norfolk Studio Tour (September), Norfolk County Fair & Horse Show (October), Simcoe Panorama (December).

Our municipal corporation's mission is "Working Together with our Community to Provide Quality Service"​. The Norfolk County Vision: "Our efforts will enhance the quality of life for all those who live, work and play in our community.  Through cooperation and dedication of staff and council we will respond to the current needs of our community and plan for the challenges of the future."​

title: Supplier Quality Engineer
description_text:
Conduct and maintain effectiveness of quality activities related to supplier quality including but not limited to:
A. Incoming inspection of critical supplied components. 
B. Participates in the approval process particularly conducting first article testing and reporting of supplied components (with support from MRB).
C. Validation testing of revised or re-designed components arising from supplier quality issues (with support from manufacturing and/or engineering).
D. Other testing that may be required pertaining to supplied components including replicating failure modes, preparation of samples to be submitted to a 3rd party laboratory, comparison testing with good versus bad material, etc.
E. Spearheading inspection of existing inventory at site as need arises (example: when a supplier claim arises with support from MRB)
F. Root cause analysis and problem resolution for any supplier-related issues including creation of corrective and preventive action report (Note: when working with suppliers, always include GSQM and/or SQM in the correspondence).
G. Creation of alerts or work visual instructions pertaining to supplier quality issues (i.e. product identification, markings, design changes, etc.)
H. Escalate any major supplier quality issues immediately to supervisor/s.
I. Conduct periodic quality audits to local suppliers (may be required to also audit overseas as needed).
J. Identifying areas for improvement pertaining to supplier quality and propose to management.
K. Participate in other quality activities that may impact supplier quality.
L. Act as local liaison between vendors and the factory on supplier quality.
Develop and/or maintain reports on:
M. Monitoring supplier performance as provided by GSQM.
N. Certificates of Analyses or Certificate of Conformance from suppliers.
O. Non-conformances (internal and field) for top thirty (30) suppliers or as designated by GSQM.
P. Technical documentation from suppliers (First Article Inspections, 3rd party inspection reports, Safety Data Sheets, Technical Data Sheets, etc.)
· Bachelor’s degree in engineering with 5+ years of experience in supplier quality
· Fluent in written and spoken English and Spanish
· Ability to work with minimum supervision.
· Proficient in MS Office and other quality-related software
Preferred Knowledge & Training:
· Knowledge of and/or experience in (preferred) in various manufacturing processes including but not limited to injection molding, extrusion, mechanical and/or electronic assemblies, etc.
· Knowledge and good understanding of QC concepts and methodologies including but not limited to 8D, ISO 9000 principles, Gage R&R, APQP.
Benefits exceeding the law
ai_core_responsibilities: The Supplier Quality Engineer is responsible for conducting quality activities related to supplier quality, including inspections, testing, and audits. They will also perform root cause analysis and problem resolution for supplier-related issues.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree in engineering and at least 5 years of experience in supplier quality. Fluency in English and Spanish, along with proficiency in MS Office and quality-related software, is required.

title: Senior Project Manager - Customer Experience & Continuity (CCT)
description_text:
Helprise is an organization that combines global reach with local talent. We work for companies that value quality, partnership, and responsibility, creating teams that really work. By precisely matching skills and business needs, we connect specialists with projects where their knowledge has a real impact. By joining us, you become part of an environment where matching is not a coincidence, but a decision.
We’re looking for a Senior Project Manager to drive the success of our Customer Continuity Team (CCT) regionally. In this role, you’ll ensure smooth, reliable operations during critical system cutovers, safeguarding the experience of customers and patients while coordinating cross-functional teams across multiple regions.
You Will:
Lead regional readiness programs, governance routines, and milestone tracking for multiday system cutovers. 
Coordinate cross-functional teams across regions, ensuring risks are identified early and mitigated. 
Support customer and patient communications to ensure clarity during transition periods. 
Maintain key documentation, dashboards, and risk registers as the single source of truth. 
Provide structured updates, issue management, and escalation support during cutover windows.
Proven experience managing large, cross-functional or multi-regional programs. 
Strong organizational skills and ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments. 
Comfort working in fast-paced, ambiguous situations. 
Familiarity with customer-facing operations, supply chain, service, or cutover programs (preferred). 
A structured, disciplined approach to governance, risk, and readiness planning. 
Excellent collaboration skills across teams and regions.
Work flexibility
We give you the opportunity to adjust your work to your lifestyle. We offer flexible hours as well as the possibility of remote or hybrid work, depending on the role and your preferences.
Various forms of cooperation
At Helprise, you can choose the model that best suits your needs. We offer both employment contracts and flexible B2B cooperation options.
Basic benefits package
We care about the health and well-being of our employees - we provide private medical care, a MultiSport card, and life insurance.
Development on your terms
We provide an individual development budget that you can use for your professional growth. As a Lumina Learning partner, we also give you access to unique tools and the support of certified practitioners, so you can better understand your potential and strengthen your talents.
Integration and shared experiences
We regularly enjoy new experiences together and build relationships in an inspiring atmosphere. From cooking sessions, through yoga weekends in nature, to the thrill of escape rooms.
ai_core_responsibilities: Lead regional readiness programs and coordinate cross-functional teams during critical system cutovers. Ensure smooth operations and effective communication with customers and patients during transition periods.
ai_requirements_summary: Proven experience managing large, cross-functional programs is required, along with strong organizational skills and the ability to navigate complex stakeholder environments. Familiarity with customer-facing operations and a structured approach to governance and risk planning are preferred.

title: Member Guide
description_text:
Firefly Health is building a revolutionary new type of comprehensive health "care and coverage,” powered by a relationship-driven care team, a trusted virtual and in-person clinical network, and our proprietary technology platform.
Founded by experienced clinicians and technology leaders, Firefly Health is on a mission to deliver clinical and financial health through joyful, always there care. We are flipping the script on what it means to be a health plan and actually providing a true health benefit to members.
We are intensely focused on optimizing the physical + mental + financial wellbeing of those who want (and deserve) something better than the status quo. If you are ready to roll up your sleeves and take on our audacious mission, we would love to hear from you.
The Role
The Member Guide Associate serves as Firefly Health's frontline, playing a critical role in supporting members through their healthcare journey and ensuring the smooth resolution of varying types of inquiries. This position requires an individual with strong attention to detail, a curious and action-oriented mindset, and the ability to take ownership of tasks from start to finish.
You will be responsible for helping members understand Firefly's unique approach to healthcare, ensuring they feel supported in understanding their benefits, getting them to the right internal team and answers, and also investigating and resolving billing and claims inquiries all within established service level agreements (SLAs). This role demands a true subject matter expert who can pivot seamlessly between explaining plan designs to a member and digging into technical claims adjudication issues with providers.
You will:
Member & Provider Support
Serve as the first point of contact for member and provider inquiries, delivering support with professionalism and empathy.
Educate members on their benefits—including deductibles, copays, coinsurance, Firefly Care Keys, and plan rules—through clear verbal and written communication.
Use Summary Plan Documents (SPDs) and internal knowledge base resources (Guru) to help members understand and maximize their Firefly health plan benefits.
Claims Inquiry Management:
Capture, document, and triage claims inquiries from members and providers within SLA.
Research claim details in internal and TPA systems to address technical questions and identify root causes.
Compare and analyze claims data to resolve disputes and coordinate solutions across internal teams.
Product & Technology Operations
Leverage trends in inbound inquiries to identify root causes and recommend process improvements to reduce volume and enhance workflows.
Provide feedback to enhance internal systems, tools, and claims workflows.
Customer Experience Excellence
Deliver empathetic, thorough support—often during stressful moments in a member’s healthcare journey.
Proactively follow up on open issues to ensure complete resolution and member satisfaction.
Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure seamless handling of issues across teams.
You’d be a good fit if:
You have a minimum of 2 years of experience in health plans, benefits, claims management, or customer service—ideally in a startup or growth-stage environment.
You can work weekend shifts
You have strong fluency in health insurance terminology (accumulators, OOP max, deductible, PPO/HMO networks, etc.) and can explain these clearly.
You are highly proficient with Google Workspace, CRM/ticketing systems, claims portals, Slack, IVR/telephony tools, and internal knowledge bases.
You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to convey complex information clearly.
You’re a structured and meticulous problem-solver with strong attention to detail.
You take initiative, follow through, and thrive in a fast-moving, ambiguous environment.
You care deeply about delivering an exceptional member and provider experience with patience and compassion.
It’d be nice if:
You’ve worked with third-party administrators (TPAs) or in claims adjudication workflows.
You have experience using Looker or similar analytics tools to dig into raw data.
You have prior exposure to early-stage or rapidly scaling healthcare organizations.
You enjoy optimizing processes, creating documentation, or contributing to operational improvements.
The salary range for this position is $25 per hour to $28 per hour; as part of a total benefits package which includes health insurance, 401k and bonus. In accordance with state applicable laws, Firefly Health is required to provide a reasonable estimate of the compensation range for this role. Individual pay decisions are ultimately based on a number of factors, including but not limited to qualifications for the role, experience level, skillset, and internal alignment.
Our office is in Watertown, Massachusetts, but we’ve developed a robust remote working structure to give us more geographical flexibility while hiring for many positions. This role can be done largely remotely, there are several times a year when staff come together onsite for planning and team building. 
Firefly is an equal opportunity employer. We value diverse backgrounds and perspectives. We're committed to building and sustaining an inclusive workplace culture where individuals are treated with dignity and respect. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. Firefly is an E-Verify employer.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Member Guide Associate acts as the frontline support, assisting members through their healthcare journey by explaining benefits, resolving inquiries, and investigating billing and claims issues within established service level agreements. This involves serving as the first point of contact for members and providers, educating them on plan details, and managing claims resolution.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a minimum of two years of experience in health plans, benefits, claims management, or customer service, ideally in a growth-stage environment, and must be able to work weekend shifts. Strong fluency in health insurance terminology and proficiency with various digital tools like Google Workspace and CRM systems are essential for this structured problem-solver role.
linkedin_org_slogan: Integrating care, coverage, and navigation to create an exceptional healthcare experience.
linkedin_org_locations: 260 Arsenal Pl, Watertown, Massachusetts 02472, US
linkedin_org_description:
By eliminating barriers between primary care, specialty care, and plan coverage, Firefly creates a frictionless, virtual-first experience unrivaled in the healthcare industry. 


-- SCAM ALERT -- 
Firefly Health has been alerted to a recruiting scam using our brand name. 
Scammers are using the names and photos of our management team to scam individuals who believe they are applying to open roles at Firefly Health.  These scams can be very convincing.  
The fraudsters often request recipients to provide personal information and to make payments as part of their fake recruiting process. They may also offer fraudulent employment opportunities to applicants and often ask for sensitive personal and financial information, including requiring candidates to arrange travel for face-to-face interviews with a certain travel agency. Firefly will never ask an applicant to send us money. We will also never offer an applicant a job without a verbal interview.  
To report a recruiting scam, visit the FTC website at https://www.consumer.ftc.gov/articles/0243-job-scams

title: Βοηθός Λογιστή - Διαχειριστής Εργοταξίου (Μουσείο Εναλίων Αρχαιοτήτων)

description_text:

Εκδίδει τα Δελτία Αποστολής 
Διασφαλίζει την ορθή αρχειοθέτηση εγγράφων

Συγκεντρώνει και ελέγχει τα δικαιολογητικά πρόσληψης προσωπικού
Καταγράφει τις ημέρες – ώρες εργασίας του προσωπικού και ενημερώνει σχετικά τα κεντρικά γραφεία
Διαχειρίζεται το ΠΣ ΕΡΓΑΝΗ και υποβάλλει τα σχετικά έντυπα δήλωσης απασχόλησης προσωπικού
Ελέγχει τα αποθέματα αναλωσίμων (π.χ. γραφική ύλη) και προβαίνει σε αντίστοιχες παραγγελίες και διανομή τους
Παρακολουθεί την επάρκεια των εγγράφων του στόλου των οχημάτων και του μηχανολογικού εξοπλισμού του εργοταξίου (απαιτήσεις νομοθεσίας, πιστοποιητικά κ.α.)
Διαχειρίζεται εισερχόμενες κλήσεις και μηνύματα, υπενθυμίσεις κ.λπ.
Παραλαμβάνει, αποστέλλει και τηρεί την εισερχόμενη – εξερχόμενη αλληλογραφία
Προετοιμάζει και συντάσσει αναφορές, εσωτερικά σημειώματα και επιστολές
Διασφαλίζει την εφαρμογή των εσωτερικών διαδικασιών του λογιστηρίου και τηρεί τα τιθέμενα χρονοδιαγράμματα των εργασιών
Απαιτούμενα Προσόντα:
Πτυχίο ΤΕΙ / ΑΕΙ Σχολών Διοίκησης Επιχειρήσεων, Λογιστικής ή Οικονομικών

title: Human Resources Coordinator
description_text:
Our purpose is to create A Better Life for Those at Home. We help provide people with medical or support needs assistance in their own homes. What makes us special is our life to the fullest approach to person-centred care. Working at The Care Company you will be part of a close-knit passionate team that truly makes a difference in our community.
We are looking to hire a Human Resources Coordinator to join our team Full-Time and help manage our employees. In this role, you will be responsible for recruiting and onboarding applicants, supporting scheduling needs, complete administrative tasks, assist with employee orientation, relations and retention, and help out with other day-to-day business activities.
Responsibilities: 
Recruit, hire, and onboard Personal Support Workers, Registered Practical Nurses, and Registered Nurses
Respond to daily emails and phone calls, file employee documents, and work with the scheduling team to manage a staff of 200 employees
Support the scheduling needs of complex care clients, including talking with families and other members of the healthcare team 
Update job descriptions and post job vacancies on external job sites
Administer pre-employment assessments and new employee orientations
Support with conducting intakes for new, prospective clients
Monitor employee certification requirements and follow up with employees as needed
Conduct performance evaluations for field staff
Maintain ongoing employee support and satisfaction
Collaborate with team members to provide excellent customer service
Support scheduling with filling gaps
This position is estimated to start ASAP
3-5 years of healthcare experience
Preference for someone with recruiting experience 
Strong computer skills, proficient in Microsoft Office
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
Organization, time management, and multi-tasking skills
Customer service and leadership
Adaptable and flexible attitude 
COVID-19 considerations: COVID Vaccination Receipts required subject to an exemption pursuant to the Ontario Human Rights Code
As a hybrid role, you will need to have the ability to commute to Toronto, ON M4G 1W6
Valid Criminal Record Check 
Valid WHMIS certification 
Full-Time, Monday-Friday 9am-5pm 
Work with a close-knit team
Self-directed daily schedule 
Health and dental benefits 
Group Retirement Savings Plan (RSP)
Make a difference for our clients and their families
Salary starting at $23/hr
ai_core_responsibilities: The Human Resources Coordinator will be responsible for recruiting and onboarding staff, managing employee relations, and supporting scheduling needs. Additionally, the role involves conducting performance evaluations and maintaining employee satisfaction.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3-5 years of healthcare experience, with a preference for those with recruiting experience. Strong computer skills, particularly in Microsoft Office, and exceptional communication abilities are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: A Better Life For Those At Home.
linkedin_org_locations: 864 Millwood Rd, Toronto, Ontario M4G 1W6, CA
linkedin_org_description:
A Better Life for Those at Home!
We believe that better health care comes to you. The Care Company provides in home Personal Support and Nursing services to people of all ages. We help seniors stay living in their own homes. We provide support for children and young adults facing health challenges. We provide on demand Nursing support for anyone who needs it post operation.
What really makes us special is our Make Each day Count philosophy where we help people reconnect with the hobbies and activities they might have abandoned due to health and mobility challenges.

title: Chief Compliance Officer
description_text:
About Bits:
Bits is an innovative fintech startup dedicated to enhancing financial inclusion and helping individuals build credit through our unique credit products. As a Y Combinator-backed company, we are rapidly growing and striving to transform the way consumers interact with credit. Our flagship product, the Bits Credit Card, alongside our cutting-edge AI offerings, has positioned us as a leader in the fintech space.
About the Role:
We are looking for an experienced Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) to lead our compliance initiatives and oversee regulatory affairs at Bits. In this senior leadership role, you will be responsible for developing, implementing, and managing our compliance programs to ensure adherence to applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards. You will work closely with the executive team, including the Founder, to align compliance objectives with our business strategy and drive a culture of compliance throughout the organization. The ideal candidate has extensive experience in compliance within financial services and a deep understanding of regulatory requirements, including those relevant to fintech.
15+ years of experience in compliance roles within the financial services or fintech industries
Proven track record of developing and implementing regulatory compliance programs
In-depth knowledge of relevant laws and regulations, including FCA guidelines and consumer protection laws
Exceptional leadership and management skills
Strong analytical and risk assessment capabilities
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels
Strategic mindset with the ability to align compliance initiatives with business goals
Relevant certifications (e.g., CAMS, CRCP) are highly desirable
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced startup environment
A competitive market salary
28 days holidays/ year incl. UK/ England public & bank holidays
Private Healthcare plan (if you want one)
Work with the Founder directly. Travel with him occasionally. Participation in the options plan once it goes live.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Chief Compliance Officer will be responsible for developing, implementing, and managing comprehensive compliance programs to ensure adherence to all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards. This role involves close collaboration with the executive team to align compliance objectives with the overall business strategy.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess over 15 years of experience in compliance roles within financial services or fintech, demonstrating a proven history of successfully developing and implementing regulatory compliance programs. Deep knowledge of relevant regulations, including FCA guidelines and consumer protection laws, is essential, alongside strong leadership and analytical capabilities.
linkedin_org_slogan: The Growth Engine for the Entire Compliance Lifecycle
linkedin_org_locations: Birger Jarlsgatan 27, Stockholm, Stockholm County 11145, SE
linkedin_org_description: Simplifying business onboarding and ensuring secure, compliant operations for seamless growth and trustworthy partnerships.
