title: Part-Time Leasing Consultant
description_text:
As a Part-Time Leasing Consultant, you will provide a positive community experience to residents and effectively market and lease apartments to prospects, while building and developing a professional foundation to launch your career.
What You’ll Do: 
Maintain a positive customer service relationship and provide a sense of community where people want to live.
Assist with planning and hosting of resident events
Participate in future hiring and training of new associates
Write up and file service requests from residents upon receipt
Contribute to the general upkeep and cleaning of office, common areas and model
Assist residents with the move-in and move-out processes
Assist in the performance of the turn process
Effectively market and lease apartments, selling the product and services of the property to prospects. 
Greet prospective residents and show model, market ready units and common areas
Assist prospective residents with completion of online application
Respond to internet leads, answer prospect calls and conduct property tours following the guidelines outlined in the training materials
Contribute to optimizing occupancy while maximizing leased rent
Utilize established sales and marketing strategies to increase property traffic, maintain closing ratios, and work to achieve and exceed budgeted occupancy percentages
Maintain thorough product knowledge of the property and competition
Perform various administrative tasks, including but not limited to:
Assist with market surveys, as needed
Collect deposit and/or fees associated with move-in
Accurately prepare and have a thorough knowledge of all lease-related paperwork
Maintain accurate prospect traffic and leasing data, and assist with other computer data entry as necessary
Inspect units on a quarterly basis as well as prior to move-in
Audit all lease and renewal files for key controls and bonus submission to the Property Manager
Perform other tasks and duties, as assigned by Property Manager
What You’ll Need: 
A desire to help others and impact your community
A strong customer focus mindset 
Ability to effectively solve problems and communicate information clearly and accurately
Ability to read and write English fluently
Ability to perform basic to intermediate math
Computer literacy required
What You’ll Get (Peak Perks):
Monthly leasing commissions available
401(k) Match
Housing Discount (varies by property)
Commitment to leadership training and growth opportunities
Wellness initiatives, healthy team competitions and reward programs through LiveWell Program
Who We Are
PeakMade Real Estate is an Atlanta-based company that specializes in the management and development of off-campus student housing, multi-family and build to rent communities across the US. Our working environment is fun, collaborative, forward-thinking and purposeful.
PeakMade Real Estate is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Leasing Consultant will be responsible for providing excellent customer service to current residents and effectively marketing and leasing available apartments to prospective tenants. Key duties include hosting events, assisting with move-in/move-out processes, and maintaining accurate leasing data.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a strong desire to help others, maintain a customer focus, and demonstrate the ability to solve problems and communicate clearly. Fluency in reading and writing English and basic to intermediate computer literacy are also required.
linkedin_org_slogan: PeakMade Real Estate is a diversified real estate services company offering multifamily & student property management.
linkedin_org_locations: 2970 Clairmont Road NE, Suite 310, Atlanta, GA 30329, US
linkedin_org_description: PeakMade Real Estate, based in Atlanta, GA, is a diversified real estate services company, offering property management, development, and consulting services for conventional multifamily and student apartments.  PeakMade Real Estate was built to execute on strategies consistent with the goals and long-term investment strategies of its partners. When something is PeakMade, it’s not only built upon expertise and experience, it’s built upon a foundation of community. For more information on PeakMade Real Estate, visit www.peakmade.com.

title: Financial Operations Specialist (Payroll and Billing)
description_text:
Financial Operations Specialist (Payroll & Billing)
Location: Remote
Company: Maxana
About Us
Maxana is a fast-growing technology consulting and staffing firm that supports enterprise clients with engineering talent. Behind every successful placement is strong operational execution — and that’s where you come in.
We’re hiring a detail-obsessed Financial Operations Specialist to own payroll accuracy, invoicing integrity, and financial back-office processes that keep our consultants paid and our clients billed correctly.
If you love clean numbers, organized systems, and catching mistakes before anyone else sees them — you’ll thrive here.
What You’ll Do
You’ll be the backbone of our financial operations.
Payroll & Timekeeping
Run and manage payroll in ADP 
Collect, validate, and reconcile consultant timesheets 
Audit hours, pay rates, and approvals for 100% accuracy 
Ensure on-time payments for contractors and employees 
Resolve payroll discrepancies quickly and professionally 
Billing & Invoicing
Generate accurate customer invoices in QuickBooks 
Match timesheets to contracts and billing terms 
Verify rates, hours, and client-specific requirements 
Track invoice status and assist with follow-ups 
Prevent revenue leakage and billing errors 
Operations & Reporting
Maintain organized financial records and documentation 
Reconcile payroll and invoicing data weekly/monthly 
Create reports for leadership (hours, revenue, margins, etc.) 
Improve processes and automate wherever possible 
Serve as the go-to person for payroll or billing questions 
Must-Have (non-negotiable)
Deep, hands-on ADP payroll experience 
Expert-level QuickBooks (invoicing, reporting, reconciliation) 
Strong proficiency in Excel
2–5+ years in payroll, accounting ops, or financial operations 
Extreme attention to detail (you naturally double-check everything) 
Comfortable working with numbers all day 
Highly organized and process-driven 
Able to work independently with minimal oversight 
Fully Remote Work 
Competitive Pay 
Working for an industry leading, tech forward company
Flexible Hours
ai_core_responsibilities: This role is responsible for owning payroll accuracy, including running and managing payroll in ADP, validating timesheets, and resolving discrepancies. Additionally, the specialist will generate accurate customer invoices in QuickBooks, matching timesheets to contracts and tracking invoice status.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess deep, hands-on experience with ADP payroll and expert-level proficiency in QuickBooks for invoicing and reporting, coupled with strong Excel skills. A minimum of 2 to 5+ years in payroll, accounting operations, or financial operations is required, emphasizing extreme attention to detail and the ability to work independently.
linkedin_org_slogan: Transformation that Transcends beyond Tradition
linkedin_org_locations: 10175 W Twain Ave, Las Vegas, Nevada 89147, US
linkedin_org_description:
Maxana is a digital transformation partner that understands your objectives and requirements, quickly assesses your current technology ecosystem, and delivers your FinTech Digital Initiatives.  Maxana combines best in class client delivery with a focus on transparency and engineering competency enabling outcomes that deliver the highest possible return on your FinTech investment dollars.

Professional Services and Staffing
Maxana’s Professional services and staffing enables our clients to achieve their Fin Tech goals and objectives. Maxana provides CTO level oversight with quality delivery with a customer first mindset. Our staffing and services run the gamut from Enterprise and solution architecture, best in class engineering and development teams, or discrete individual contributor resources to support your programs and projects.

Custom Integration Solutions
Maxana’s Custom Integration Solutions Teams maximize business value by rapidly designing and implementing best in class integrations. Our custom integration solutions securely extend the functionality of your applications with new and existing third-party SaaS, open and proprietary platforms, and enterprise systems quickly driving both accelerated revenue and amazing customer outcomes.

Payment Processing
Not every company will need the same kind of online payment software, which is why Maxana designs systems for our clients from the ground up. Bespoke software, off-the shelf solutions..whatever your company needs, Maxana we’ll deliver it in a cost-effective, rapidly deployable manner.

title: Project Management Internship - Summer 2026
description_text:
At Royal Electric Company we provide expert electrical design, construction, and service with builders, contractors and facility owners who value relationships, trust, expertise, and a commitment to each other towards achieving exceptional results.
We are able to sustain this through our linking values of expertise, collaboration, integrity, passion, and loyalty. These values support our core purpose; to build great relationships, one project at a time!
We are currently accepting applications for Interns for our San Francisco Project Office located at San Francisco International Airport. This opportunity will be starting Summer 2026, working with our Project Management teams.
There are several areas of the business the Intern will be exposed to at Royal; being provided with opportunities for hands on learning and invaluable experience that will serve them well in their careers.
Success in the position can be achieved through the following duties & responsibilities:
Project Engineer:
Assisting with coordinating crews and timelines
Assisting with writing RFIs and submittals
Daily reporting of project progress
Travel to jobsites as needed
Participate in safety meetings, i.e. Tailgate Talks, Stretch & Flex, etc.
Currently enrolled in a higher-level education program for Construction Management, Engineering, or related field of study
Proficient in all Microsoft Office Suite programs
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
Ability to communicate effectively and efficiently with all areas of business, internal and external customers, etc. 
Internships will look different based on a candidate’s direction, goals, and schooling.
Each role description is subject to change and may include other duties as they are assigned. 
Salary Range: 
$22-$27
Royal is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. Individuals seeking employment at Royal are considered without regards to age, ancestry, color, race, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, protected family care or medical leave status, religion, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.
We utilize E-Verify to confirm work authorization with both the Social Security Administration and the Department of Homeland Security. E-Verify Link: http://www.uscis.gov/e-verify/employees
Applicants must be authorized to work in the United States. Royal Electric is unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of employment Visa at this time. 
We promote a drug free workplace.

For 50 years Royal Electric Company had been deemed the "Best Subcontractor on the Project"​ by our customers.  We are a company of talented and energetic professionals who simply love to build great things. title: Operational Excellence Specialist
description_text:
About us
Founded in 2015 in Athens, Greece, Welcome redefines the way people travel by going above and beyond the commoditized transfer service and being the first company to deliver a complete, personalised, in-destination travel experience. From the moment a traveler arrives at a new destination, until their return journey home, Welcome accommodates all their travel needs, including transfers, sightseeing trips, and local information, in the easiest, friendliest, and most personalised way possible. Welcome's drivers are experts in the area and share their local know-how to make travellers feel at home wherever they are. The company has also introduced contactless rides, thorough cleaning protocols, and protective equipment to make every journey safe.
Being a travel tech startup, Welcome continues to grow and scale its operations and is quickly becoming a global category leader for in-destination travel services.
One of the highest-rated global transportation companies with a rating of 4.9/5 stars.
Expanded from 200 destinations last year to 350, achieving our ambitious 2024 growth target.
Over 4,000 travel partners including 2,500 hotels, numerous vacation rentals, and travel agents, adding 50+ new ones every month.
Over 2.5 million happy travellers every year.
⭐️ If you want to dive deeper into the awesomeness of Welcome's culture, click on to check our TikTok account.⭐️
The Team
We are a group of vibrant, diverse people who love travelling and never settle on quality. Each one of us didn’t join Welcome by chance and believes deeply in what Welcome is trying to achieve, so we work relentlessly to make that happen. We challenge common logic, focus on design, put simplicity and usability first, and create memorable experiences. We keep learning and exploring better ways to serve our community and grow personally and professionally in our respective fields. We stay humble along the way, with a “pay it forward” mentality, but with big and bold goals.
We're looking for an Operational Excellence Analyst to join our Operations team and help drive continuous improvement across the business. You'll work closely with the Ops Excellence Manager to identify inefficiencies, coordinate improvement initiatives, and build the systems that enable Welcome to scale.
This is a hands-on role that combines analytical thinking with execution. You'll dig into data to find opportunities, design better processes, track initiatives to completion, and work across teams to make improvements stick. If you love making things work better and have a knack for getting things done, this role is for you.
Responsibilities
Find opportunities: Analyse operational data to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities across the business
Design solutions: Document current processes, design improvements, and build business cases for change
Drive delivery: Track improvement initiatives, coordinate with owners, and ensure things get done on time
Build systems: Create dashboards, documentation, and playbooks that help the team operate more effectively
Work cross-functionally: Partner with Destination Ops, Driver Supply, Support, and other teams on improvement projects
Enable scale: Help automate manual processes and build the infrastructure for sustainable growth
2-4 years of experience in operations, business analysis, consulting, or similar roles
Analytical mindset - being comfortable with data, spreadsheets, and you can learn SQL
Process thinker - You naturally see how things could work better
Strong communicator -You can work with diverse stakeholders and get alignment
Ownership mentality. You take initiative and drive things to completion
Comfortable with ambiguity, thriving in fast-moving environments
Vibrant and fresh work environment
Flexible work-from-home policy
The tools you need to perform your daily tasks successfully
L&D personal budget
Private Insurance Plan
+4 extra PTO days annually
The unique opportunity to join “the next big thing” at ground level
ai_core_responsibilities: The Operational Excellence Specialist will analyze operational data to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities, design solutions, and track improvement initiatives to ensure timely completion. They will also create systems and documentation to help the team operate more effectively and enable sustainable growth.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 2-4 years of experience in operations, business analysis, or consulting, with an analytical mindset and strong communication skills. They should be comfortable with data and thrive in fast-moving environments.
linkedin_org_slogan: Welcome is here to redefine the way people travel in new destinations #yourfriendontheground

Barcelona, Catalunia 08001, ES

Travel, Explore, Discover, Experience

Welcome redefines the way people travel by offering the most personalized and wholesome travel experience from the moment they start planning their trip until the moment they return home. Welcome provides travelers with the warmest, most informative and friendliest transfer from and to the airport. 

Welcome helps you make the most of your travels by providing transfer services, essential products, pre-ordered tickets to top attractions, tips and tailor-made experiences led by locals.

title: 26-27 Director of After School Programming and Enrichment for Lower School
description_text:
POSITION DESCRIPTION
St. Catherine's School is an Episcopal day school in Richmond, Virginia, founded in 1890 enrolling roughly 950 girls age 3 through grade 12. Located on a 16-acre campus in the western part of the city, St. Catherine's core values include pursuing academic excellence, developing character, nurturing spiritual growth, celebrating individuals, and building community. 
St. Catherine's School provides a rigorous education that prepares girls with diverse perspectives for a lifetime of learning, leadership, and service in a global community.
POSITION OVERVIEW
The Director is responsible for the overall planning, staffing, and management of the After School Program serving St. Catherine’s students in grades JK-4. This program connects girls with a variety of enrichment classes and activities.
Supervisor: Head of Lower School and Early Childhood
Supervisory Responsibilities: Instructors, staff and vendors
Position Status: Full time, exempt, 10-month schedule
Hours of Work: Standard Work Week, Hours 10:30am - 6:30pm
ESSENTIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
Manage the program registration process
Develop, monitor, and maintain the program budget
Create, update, and manage marketing and informational materials for push pages, flyers, Papers for Parents, and the public SaintsNet page
Maintain and regularly update the SaintsNet web page
Ensure timely publication of current information regarding projects, classes, and special events
Oversee student activities content, including photos and highlights
Maintain accurate operating hours and essential program information
Update and manage faculty biographies
Prepare and distribute a monthly communication email to parents
Develop, implement, and maintain program policies and procedures, including safety and health guidelines and parent communication protocols
Create and maintain an annual enrichment schedule organized by trimester
Supervise instructors and staff; coordinate enrichment class teachers and external vendors
Provide administrative support, including sign-out procedures, attendance tracking, and enrichment class rosters
Research and develop age-appropriate daily activities for each student group
Identify and research opportunities to expand and enhance the program
Assist with program setup and cleanup as needed
Order and manage supplies necessary for students and staff
Coordinate with Assistant Head of Early Childhood to ensure all religiously-exempt childcare program requirements are met and maintained consistently (for example, Department of Social Services)
PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORK ENVIRONMENT
While performing the duties of this position, the employee is required to sit; stand; walk; use hands to manipulate, handle and feel objects, tools, or controls; reach with hands and arms; climb stairs; balance; stoop, kneel, crouch and crawl; talk and hear; engage in moderately strenuous physical activity both indoors and out; and smell. The employee may occasionally lift or move up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this position include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. 
TRAVEL
Travel is not a requirement for this role.
EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT
St. Catherine’s School does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, or national and ethnic origin in the administration of its hiring practices. St. Catherine’s School is committed to providing an inclusive educational environment. We actively engage students, families, faculty and staff from diverse backgrounds and experiences. Our School community respects the differences in ethnicity, race, religion, sexual orientation and socioeconomic status. Consistent with the School’s mission, St. Catherine’s fosters in our students, and experts from all others, respect for the dignity and wholeness of every person. For more information on St. Catherine’s Access, Equity and Belonging please follow this link https://www.st.catherines.org/about/access-equity-and-belonging
CONDITIONAL OFFERS
An offer of employment for this position is contingent upon the applicant’s right to work in the United States, authorization for and completion of successful background checks as may be requested by the school or required by law.
BENEFITS
St. Catherine’s School offers a highly competitive compensation package for this full-time position with benefits to include excellent health plan options, retirement benefits, exhaustive professional development and networking opportunities as well as state-of-the art facilities.
Note: This position description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work to be performed by employees within this classification. This position description should not be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties and responsibilities required or assigned to an employee in this position. Nothing contained in this position description restricts St. Catherine’s School’s right to assign or reassign duties and responsibilities to this position at any time.
REQUIREMENTS AND QUALIFICATIONS
Bachelor’s degree in Education or equivalent work experience required; Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in after-school programming, enrichment programs, or Lower School education, including leadership or supervisory experience
Strong understanding of child development, age-appropriate enrichment, and best practices for student safety and well-being
Proven ability to manage budgets, registration processes, and operational logistics
Experience supervising, mentoring, and evaluating faculty, staff, and enrichment instructors
Excellent organizational and project management skills with strong attention to detail
Strong written and verbal communication skills, including experience communicating effectively with parents and school leadership
Proficiency with educational technology platforms, websites, and content management systems (e.g., school portals, registration software)
Demonstrated ability to plan and execute engaging enrichment opportunities across academic, athletic, artistic, and social-emotional domains
Commitment to the school’s mission, values, and community culture
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment while maintaining a high level of professionalism
Current CPR/First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain upon hire)
ai_core_responsibilities: The Director is responsible for the comprehensive planning, staffing, and management of the After School Program for Lower School students (grades JK-4), which includes connecting girls with various enrichment classes and activities. Key duties involve managing registration, developing and monitoring the budget, creating marketing materials, and supervising instructors and staff.

title: Director of Product - Remote (Req. #750)
description_text:
Mindex is evolving into a provider of a complete K-12 software ecosystem. Reporting directly to the Chief Product Officer (CPO), the Director of Product serves as the vital link between high-level company goals and the day-to-day work of our product teams. You will ensure that our suite—SchoolTool, ClearTrack, MTSS Edge, Integrations Platform, and Advanced Analytics—functions as a unified, high-value solution for our customers.
As the Director of Product, you will lead a team of Product Owners while providing strategic direction and priority to our UX team. Your job is to facilitate a two-way flow of information: you will translate the CPO’s broad vision into clear roadmaps for each product, while also bringing deep insights from our customers and the market back to the CPO to help shape that future vision.
You are a hands-on collaborator who works across the company to make sure our products are easy to use, technically sound, and ready for the market.
Essential Functions:
Owns the roadmap for Mindex products. Partners with the CPO to turn the company vision into organized, well-communicated plans across all our products— SchoolTool, ClearTrack, MTSS Edge, Integrations Platform, and Advanced Analytics—ensuring they work together as one system. 
Serves as the main representative for product development. Builds and maintains enduring cross-functional relationships within Mindex and externally with our customers and partners. Drives meaningful collaboration and teamwork to understand buyer and customer needs, competitors and growth opportunities, and technical development, technical operations, and service considerations.
Builds, develops, and directs an industry-leading product team consisting of user experience and product management experts. Helps the teams to define their metrics and objectives and holds them accountable for success in the fulfillment of their responsibilities and following Mindex values.
Directs product management who collaborate with stakeholders to turn the product strategy into actionable near-term, problems-to-solve for their cross-functional agile software development teams in the form of epics, features, stories, and bugs, and provide continuous feedback and approval for release.
Directs user experience that deeply empathizes with SchoolTool users, their pain points and unmet needs, to identify and validate potential product roadmap opportunities that are coordinated with product owners and communicated to development teams via minimally sufficient designs (mocks, flow diagrams, etc.).
Collaborates with marketing, sales, and service delivery to ensure that major new feature launches have realistic and cohesive cross-functional plans that span how they will be marketed, sold, built, implemented, supported, and maintained; and that they are communicated clearly, executed with fidelity, and that any risks and issues are surfaced quickly and addressed as a team.
Education & Experience:
BS Degree in Computer Science, Engineering or related area preferred.
2-3 years of leadership experience, preferably director-level at a SaaS product company
5-7 years of management experience, SaaS product management, EdTech preferred.
K-12 Student Management Systems and New York State regulatory knowledge preferred.
Skills & Competencies:
Strong business acumen, strategic planning & execution skills, highly organized and accurate.
Strong interpersonal skills, able to work with stakeholders of all levels, great at presenting and public speaking.
Strong product management, critical thinking, analysis, and problem-solving skills.
Medical Insurance (with a free option!)
Dental Insurance
Vision Insurance
Company Paid Life and AD&D Insurance
Optional Additional Life Insurance
Company Paid Short-Term Disability Insurance
Company Paid Long-Term Disability Insurance
Medical and Dependent Care Flexible Savings Accounts (FSA)
Health Savings Accounts with Company Contributions
Optional Hospital Indemnity, Accident and Critical Illness Insurance as well as Legal Assistance
Our Perks:
Flexible Time Off
Eight Paid Holidays
Pre-tax and Roth 401(k) Options with Company Match
Investment in Professional Development including a license to Udemy training courses and leadership training
Employee Assistance Program
Adoption Assistance
Pet Insurance Discounts
Tickets to local sporting events
Team building events
Holiday and celebration parties
The band range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors considered in making compensation decisions, including, but not limited to, skill sets, education, experience, training, certifications, internal equity, and other business and organizational needs. It is not typical for an individual to be hired at, or near, the top of the range for their role, and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. The role has a base salary range of $140,0000 - $240,000 with additional variable compensation through commission.
Applicants must be authorized to work for ANY employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time.

title: PET/CT Technologist
description_text:
Join the fastest growing outpatient radiology practice in the Nation- SimonMed Imaging! Our commitment to excellence and improving patient care paired with the best in class technology allows us to be an industry leader in the constantly evolving health care environment. Secure your spot now and take advantage of a unique career opportunity to advance your skills while working alongside a dedicated team of board-certified subspecialty radiologists. We can’t wait to meet you!

As a Nuclear Medicine PET/CT Technologist at SimonMed, you will be performing diagnostic Nuclear Medicine, CT, and PET/CT exams.

You will be trained on the latest PET/CT procedures including but not limited to; Axumin, NETSPOT, Amyloid and Oncology Imaging. You will also be trained on the latest CT technology, including Dual Energy and iterative reconstruction. SimonMed is committed to becoming the Nation’s leading provider for low dose CT. You will gain experience in a variety of specialized exams including but not limited to; Coronary CTAs, Dual Energy Metal Suppression, and Kidney stone quantification.
\n

Essential Functions
Establishes and maintains a good rapport and professional relationship with fellow employees, other departments, and facility staff.
Works under general supervision.
Obtains patient history, explains standard procedures, and addresses patient concerns. 
Performs a variety of PET/CT procedures by following and verifying referring provider’s orders to provide images, data analysis and patient information for diagnostic interpretation or therapeutic procedures.
Performs Nuclear Medicine and/or PET/CT with a variety of technical procedures applying ionizing radiation for the purpose of detecting pathology. 
Performs procedures at a technical level not requiring constant supervision of technical detail.
Produces computerized tomographic scanner radiographs of specific areas as required by the departmental procedures.
Processes data and enhancing digital images using advanced computer technology.
Performs Nuclear Medicine and/or PET/CT procedures at a technical level not requiring constant supervision of technical detail.
Performs those duties directly involved with a variety of technical procedures applying ionizing radiation for the purpose of detecting pathology.
Regulates the equipment used to expose the x-ray film, develops and documents PACS imaging.
Operates equipment safely and maintains SimonMed standards while performing all types of procedures.
Evaluates images to determine the technical quality and calibration of instrumentation.
Provides diagnostic aid to physicians by conducting organ or body scans on patients. 
Administers radiopharmaceuticals and medications for patient imaging and therapeutic procedures
Administers and records isotope dosage in accordance to established departmental protocol. 
Observes patient during procedure and reports any abnormal activity. 
Familiar with standard concepts, practices and procedures. 
Relies on experience and judgment to plan and accomplish goals. 
Demonstrates competency in the performance of job related skills appropriate to his/her customer populations and departmental services while providing excellent customer service.
Utilizes and operates equipment and mechanical devices which provide for a functional, effective, and safe environment.
Adheres to policies, procedures, guidelines relative to departmental operations. 

Other Duties
Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of all activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time or without notice.

Benefits
Your health, happiness and future matters! At SimonMed Imaging, we offer medical, vision and dental insurance, 401(k) eligibility, paid holidays plus PTO, Sick Time, opportunity for growth, and much more!

Education
High School Diploma
Requires completion of a formal Radiologic Technology training program in AMA approved school.
Bachelor’s degree in a related field (Preferred)

License/Certification
BLS Certification or “Hands–On” CPR certification
Requires registration as a Certified Technologist Nuclear Medicine by the State
Requires registration as a Certified Radiologic Technologist by the State
Requires NMTCB and/or A.R.R.T.(N) certification
Requires registration as an RT and CT Technologist by the A.R.R.T.
SimonMed Imaging requires valid CPR certification

Experience
2 years of experience in radiology

Physical Demands
This position may require duties including lifting and carrying up to 40 pounds, sitting for prolonged periods of time, with frequent standing and walking. May be exposed to biohazards and chemicals as outlined in the MSDS Manual; and other hazards/unpleasant working conditions.

Dress Attire
Business Casual or scrubs dependent on department

\n
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Employment is contingent upon successful completion of drug and background screening. Some positions will require a favorable driving record.
ai_core_responsibilities: The technologist will perform diagnostic Nuclear Medicine, CT, and PET/CT exams, including administering radiopharmaceuticals and processing data using advanced computer technology. Essential functions involve obtaining patient history, explaining procedures, and ensuring the technical quality of images for diagnostic interpretation.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a High School Diploma and completion of a formal Radiologic Technology training program from an AMA-approved school, along with 2 years of experience in radiology. Required certifications include BLS/CPR, State registration as a Certified Technologist Nuclear Medicine and Radiologic Technologist, and NMTCB and/or A.R.R.T.(N) certification.

title: X-Ray Technologist
description_text:
Join the fastest growing outpatient radiology practice in the Nation- SimonMed Imaging! Our commitment to excellence and improving patient care paired with the best in class technology allows us to be an industry leader in the constantly evolving health care environment. Secure your spot now and take advantage of a unique career opportunity to advance your skills while working alongside a dedicated team of board-certified subspecialty radiologists. We can’t wait to meet you!
\n

Job Overview
We are seeking a skilled and compassionate X-ray Technologist to join our outpatient imaging team. The ideal candidate will perform diagnostic radiographic procedures with precision, accuracy, and attention to patient comfort. As part of a multidisciplinary team, the technologist will contribute to patient care by producing high-quality images that aid in diagnosis and treatment planning.

Key Responsibilities
Perform high-quality diagnostic X-ray exams as prescribed by physicians 
Prepare and position patients accurately to achieve optimal imaging results 
Ensure patient safety and comfort throughout the imaging process 
Operate and maintain X-ray equipment, ensuring proper calibration and functionality 
Review images for quality assurance and follow protocols for image storage 
Adhere to established radiation safety standards and infection control guidelines 
Document patient information accurately and complete reports as required 
Collaborate with other radiology and healthcare professionals to support optimal patient care 
Maintain knowledge of advancements in radiologic technology and imaging techniques

Qualifications
Certification: ARRT (American Registry of Radiologic Technologists) certification in 
Radiography required 
Licensure: Current state licensure as required by law 
Education: Associate's degree in Radiologic Technology or equivalent from an accredited program 
Experience: Minimum of 1-2 years of experience as an X-ray Technologist, preferably in an outpatient or clinical setting

Skills and Competencies
Strong knowledge of radiologic procedures, anatomy, and patient positioning 
Proficiency with imaging software and radiologic equipment 
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with a patient-centered approach 
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment and handle multiple priorities 
Detail-oriented with strong organizational skills 
Commitment to maintaining patient confidentiality and professionalism at all times

Preferred Qualifications
Experience in outpatient or ambulatory care setting 
BLS (Basic Life Support) certification 
Knowledge of additional imaging modalities (e.g., CT, MRI) is a plus

Benefits
Your health, happiness and future matters! At SimonMed Imaging, we offer medical, vision and dental insurance, 401(k) eligibility, paid holidays plus PTO, Sick Time, opportunity for growth, and much more!

\n
We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Employment is contingent upon successful completion of drug and background screening. Some positions will require a favorable driving record.

title: Customs Brokerage Supervisor
description_text:
CUSTOMS BROKERAGE SUPERVISOR


About the Organization
We are a logistics and trade solutions company with a team of over 300 specialists dedicated to fulfilling customer supply chain needs. Our focus includes customs brokerage, domestic and international transportation, trade compliance, education, and consulting.


Our business practices are guided daily by three core values: ENRICH purposefully, CARE personally, and DELIVER professionally. We are an employee-centric organization that prioritizes our people and our customers. This commitment has earned us the "Great Place To Work" certification for the past eleven years.


Essential Duties & Responsibilities

Supervise, train, and coach Entry-Writing/CHB staff.

Assign accounts and distribute workload across the team.

Measure performance of staff and address any emerging issues.

Support local branch Import Transportation teams with brokerage requirements.

Troubleshoot freight issues with US Customs and other government agencies.

Enhance department performance in collaboration with the Customs Brokerage Manager.

Complete Entry work and audit files for accuracy as needed.

Oversee billing processes and monitor dwell time for all files.

Improve procedures and streamline the Entry department workflow.

On-board new clients according to sales or client-specific SOPs.

Verify accuracy of US Customs information on all documentation.

Execute projects and provide support as assigned by management.




Desired Skills & Experience


Accreditation: Certified Customs Specialist (CCS) required; Licensed Customs Broker preferred.

Experience: Minimum of 5 years working with US Customs and other government agencies.

Regulatory Knowledge: Thorough understanding of US Customs rules and regulations.

Leadership: Prior experience in personnel supervision and/or training.



Employee Characteristics

Self-motivated and capable of effective prioritization.

Highly organized with the ability to multi-task and maintain attention to detail.

Problem-solver capable of resolving issues across all aspects of the position.

Adaptable to changing work environments.

Objective leader who can effectively supervise a diverse team.


\n

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: The supervisor will oversee Entry-Writing/CHB staff by assigning accounts, distributing workload, measuring performance, and addressing emerging issues. Key duties also involve supporting import transportation teams, troubleshooting freight issues with government agencies, and enhancing department performance.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a minimum of 5 years of experience working with US Customs and other government agencies, along with a thorough understanding of US Customs rules and regulations. A Certified Customs Specialist (CCS) accreditation is required, and a Licensed Customs Broker is preferred.

title: Head of Business Development, EU
description_text:
Help us use technology to make a big green dent in the universe!

Kraken powers some of the most innovative global developments in energy.

We’re a technology company focused on creating a smart, sustainable energy system. From optimising renewable generation, creating a more intelligent grid and enabling utilities to provide excellent customer experiences, our operating system for energy is transforming the industry around the world in a way that benefits everyone.

It’s a really exciting time in energy. Help us make a real impact on shaping a better, more sustainable future.

As we enter the stage of rapid commercialisation and customer account growth we are now looking for an exceptional person to increase our European new business presence and pipeline (utilities and asset owners). We envisage the ‘EU Head of Business Development’ taking on responsibility for generating, nurturing and closing deals across the region.

You will be empowered to help at every level of our organisation; working collaboratively with our senior leadership and management teams, and across our business development, delivery and technical teams to design and deliver customer oriented propositions that exponentially grow our client base and market share. 
\n

What you'll do
Develop and deliver new leads through your own pre-existing power market network and inbound and outbound channels
Develop and deliver excellent in person and remote pitches, which have clearly defined value proposition and show an in depth understanding our our solution, power markets and how we solves customer needs
Ability coordinate internal team members (and when required ‘self respond’ to formal tenders) such as RFI’s and RFP’s
Identify and develop valuable key stakeholder relationships with potential customers and in turn, influence decision making process
Work with all functions within Kraken to ensure that you are pitching the ‘latest and greatest’ our platform has to offer and utilise SME’s in the sales process to enhance market and technical value propositions
Work with management and executive teams to develop our customer proposition, ensuring value creation for the business and outstanding satisfaction for the customer
Prepare and present new business performance / forecast reports defining action plans to improve number of deals closing and annual recurring revenue
Take existing pricing models and tailor them based on your understanding of the prospects needs to create optimal commercial proposalsLead contract negotiations, through to close

What we're looking for
Experience as a SaaS sales lead in the energy industry
Experience working with energy related software solutions (preferably trading or ancillary service related)
Experience leading pitches / presentations to a very high standard
Experience of utilising the latest business development methodologies
Experience assessing product fit vs customer needs in the form of discovery and qualification with new business opportunities
Experience of developing market leading customer engagement propositions (RFI, RFP’s), plans and upskilling the business for successful delivery
Experience of being hands on in legal and pricing negotiations 
Experience using CRM to track deal progress
Proven success in delivering an outstanding prospect relationships
Proven success in closing deals and increasing annual recurring revenue
Ability to engender a high level of confidence from the senior leadership team
Knowledge of energy trading and balancing services Knowledge of energy storage and renewable asset generation
Excellent communication skills showing the ability to summarise complex problems and recommend concise plans of action
Effective risk management and negotiation skills - able to balance competing demands for resource to ensure best overall outcome
A proven ability to build relationships at every level of large organisations, especially the c suite.
Proficient in English, another EU language would be desirable

\n
Kraken is a certified Great Place to Work in France, Germany, Spain, Japan and Australia. In the UK we are one of the Best Workplaces on Glassdoor with a score of 4.5 and in Germany we rate 4.7 on Kununu as a Top Company. Check out our Welcome to the Jungle site (FR/EN) to learn more about our teams and culture.

Are you ready for a career with us? We want to ensure you have all the tools and environment you need to unleash your potential. If you have any specific accommodations or a unique preference, please contact us at inclusion@kraken.tech and we'll do what we can to customise your interview process for comfort and maximum magic!

Studies have shown that some groups of people, like women, are less likely to apply to a role unless they meet 100% of the job requirements. Whoever you are, if you like one of our jobs, we encourage you to apply as you might just be the candidate we hire. Across Kraken, we're looking for genuinely decent people who are honest and empathetic. Our people are our strongest asset and the unique skills and perspectives people bring to the team are the driving force of our success. As an equal opportunity employer, we do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute. We consider all applicants without regard to race, colour, religion, national origin, age, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, marital or veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status. U.S. based candidates can learn more about their EEO rights here.

Our (i) Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice and Artificial Intelligence (AI) Notice, (ii) Website Privacy Notice and (iii) Cookie Notice govern the collection and use of your personal data in connection with your application and use of our website. These policies explain how we handle your data and outline your rights under applicable laws, including, but not limited to, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA). Depending on your location, you may have the right to access, correct, or delete your information, object to processing, or withdraw consent. By applying, you acknowledge that you’ve read, understood and consent to these terms

title: Customer Care Consultant
description_text:

description_text:
About Coderio

Coderio designs and delivers scalable digital solutions for global companies. With a strong technical foundation and a product-oriented mindset, our teams lead complex software projects from architecture to execution. We value autonomy, clear communication, and technical excellence. We work closely with international teams and partners, building technology that creates real impact. 
🌍 More information: http://coderio.com

We are looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer (.Net/Angular) who excels in hybrid environments, is comfortable working within complex legacy systems, and can methodically modernize them without disrupting existing functionality.
If you enjoy high-stakes migrations, SQL-level performance tuning, and bridging on-premise Windows hosting with modern cloud-native architectures, this role is for you.

It is required to reside in Uruguay or Argentina.

What You Can Expect (Responsibilities)

Lead the migration of legacy ASP.NET MVC and Web API applications from .NET Framework 4.8 to .NET 8, ensuring stability, performance, and feature parity.
Drive frontend modernization by transitioning AngularJS applications to modern Angular (v20+) within a Microsoft-integrated ecosystem.
Manage database evolution from EF6 to EF Core, resolving migration blockers and optimizing SQL Server performance.
Operate and optimize Windows-based hosting environments, including IIS configuration, load balancing, and authentication model transitions.
Design and implement Azure PaaS solutions using Azure Functions, secure configuration management, and application monitoring.
Architect and maintain reliable background processing solutions using Windows-based services and job orchestration tools.

Requirements

Advanced Azure PaaS experience with Azure Functions, Blob Storage, App Configuration, Key Vault, and SignalR.

regions_derived: Autonomous Community of the Basque Country

description_text:
About Coderio

Coderio designs and delivers scalable digital solutions for global companies. With a strong technical foundation and a product-oriented mindset, our teams lead complex software projects from architecture to execution. We value autonomy, clear communication, and technical excellence. We work closely with international teams and partners, building technology that creates real impact.
🌍 More information: http://coderio.com

We are seeking a C#-centric Automation Engineer to play a key role in the modernization of mission-critical systems, supporting the migration from .NET Framework 4.8 to .NET 8 and from AngularJS to Angular 20. The role is focused on reducing regression risk by designing and owning a robust, scalable test automation framework across UI, APIs, and backend processes, with full responsibility for test quality and CI/CD integration in Azure DevOps.

It is required to reside in Uruguay or Argentina.

What You Can Expect (Responsibilities)

Design, maintain, and scale C#-based automation frameworks using Selenium or Playwright, applying clean architecture and SOLID principles.
Build and maintain API automation tests for legacy (.NET 4.8) and modern (.NET 8) services, validating business logic independently from the UI.
Own and manage the Quality Gate in Azure DevOps, integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines.
Configure and optimize pipelines to run smoke tests on PRs and full regression suites on nightly builds, ensuring reliability and fast feedback.
Validate backend and background processes through database queries and log analysis.
Actively detect, debug, and eliminate flaky tests, ensuring stable and trustworthy pipelines.
Handle complex asynchronous and dynamic behaviors using robust synchronization strategies.


Requirements

5+ years of strong C# development experience, with a developer-first mindset and ability to build complex, maintainable automation code.
Deep hands-on experience with test automation frameworks using Selenium or Playwright for .NET, including solid use of the Page Object Model.
Proven API automation experience, with strong understanding of REST principles, HTTP protocols, authentication, and JSON handling.
Experience integrating automated tests into Azure DevOps pipelines, including test execution, result publishing, and quality gates.
Solid SQL skills to prepare test data and validate backend transactions through database queries.
Reside in Uruguay or Argentina.

Nice to Have

Load Testing using k6 or JMeter.
Hangfire knowledge, including programmatic job status validation.
Docker / Containerization experience for spinning up test environments.

Benefits

100% remote
Long-term commitment with autonomy and impact
Strategic, high-visibility role within a modern engineering culture
Collaborative international team and strong technical leadership

title: Business Development Representative - Cold Outbound
description_text:
Open to:
Recent college graduates seeking a full-time, ground up opportunity with a high growth Saas Company
Current juniors and seniors interested in part-time or internship opportunities

About Beyond Menu
BeyondMenu helps 15,000 independent restaurants win with digital hospitality — SEO, online ordering, guest engagement, retention, and revenue-driving tools built specifically for owner-operators. We’re a leading revenue partner for independent restaurants in this space.

If you want to build real sales skill, compete at a high level, and be part of a performance-driven revenue org, this role is for you.

The Role
We’re hiring Business Development Representatives (BDRs) to drive new business through cold outbound — calling restaurant owners & decision makers, creating urgency, and booking high-quality meetings for Account Executives. 

This is a phone-first, high-volume prospecting role — ideal for competitive sellers who want to build elite sales fundamentals.

You will prospect, cold call, handle objections, control conversations, and win decision-maker attention — daily.If you want to build real sales skill, compete at a high level, and be part of a performance-driven revenue org, this role is for you.
\n

What You'll Do:
Cold call restaurant owners to generate qualified meetings for AEs
Run high-volume outbound prospecting (calls, follow-ups, tasks)
Position value quickly and confidently in fast-paced conversations
Handle objections and earn commitment from busy decision-makers
Maintain clean activity, notes, and outcomes in Salesforce
Hit daily execution standards for dials, talk time, connects, and meetings set
Continuously improve through call coaching, QA, and performance feedback

Who We're Looking For: 
You’re likely a strong fit if you:
Enjoy cold calling and live sales conversations
Want to build real sales skill (objection handling, control, closing for next steps)
Are comfortable with rejection, pressure, and accountability
Bring energy, urgency, and coachability
Track your numbers and take pride in performance
Want a clear path to AE or closing roles

Preferred Background
SDR / BDR experience OR high-drive entry-level sales talent
Experience in outbound, call-center, SMB, or high-velocity sales is a plus
Restaurant, hospitality, or local business exposure is helpful — not required

What We Offer: 
Competitive hourly + uncapped commission
Comprehensive training to ensure your success.
A supportive and collaborative team culture.
Clear execution standards
Structured daily call coaching and QA
Real - time daily scorecards and performance feedback
Fast track to Coaching or Account Executive roles for top performers

\n
ai_core_responsibilities: Business Development Representatives will drive new business by making cold outbound calls to restaurant owners and decision-makers to book high-quality meetings for Account Executives. This involves running high-volume prospecting activities, positioning value quickly, handling objections, and maintaining accurate records in Salesforce.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate enjoys cold calling, wants to build strong sales skills like objection handling and conversation control, and is comfortable with pressure and accountability. Preferred backgrounds include SDR/BDR experience or high-drive entry-level sales talent, with exposure to outbound or high-velocity sales being a plus.
linkedin_org_slogan: Our mission is to empower local restaurants to delight their diners through digital hospitality.
linkedin_org_locations: 115 Perimeter Center Pl, Suite 1025, Atlanta, Georgia 30346, US
6 S Rio Grande St, 2072, Salt Lake City, Utah 84101, US
linkedin_org_description: Beyond Menu is a leading web and app-based online food ordering service company. We partner with 15,000 local restaurants in 2,000+ cities across the nation. Our online and mobile ordering platforms connect hungry diners like you with your favorite restaurants.

title: Digital marketing Specialist
description_text:
Vuoi lavorare in un ambiente dinamico, internazionale e in continua espansione? Se cerchi un'azienda dove Competenza, Esclusività e Passione sono i valori fondamentali del successo, Engel & Völkers ti offre l’opportunità di fare la differenza. Unisciti a noi!

Da oltre 45 anni, Engel & Völkers è un’azienda globale leader nel settore dei servizi immobiliari residenziali e commerciali nel segmento premium. Con oltre 16.500 professionisti attivi in più di 1.000 sedi distribuite in 35 Paesi e 5 continenti, rappresentiamo un marchio di riferimento nel Real Estate internazionale.
In Italia, il nostro network include 8 Shops per il canale diretto e oltre 100 sedi di franchising, in costante crescita, grazie all’eccellenza del servizio offerto e alla selezione di proprietà esclusive che soddisfano le esigenze di una clientela sempre più sofisticata. Tutti i nostri collaboratori incarnano i valori fondamentali del nostro brand: competenza, esclusività e passione. Ogni giorno, ci impegniamo per offrire un servizio impeccabile, selezionando proprietà uniche e rispondendo alle esigenze di una clientela internazionale esigente.

Entrare a far parte del nostro team significa lavorare con standard di eccellenza e contribuire al successo di un marchio riconosciuto a livello mondiale.

Per l'espansione del nostro team marketing siamo alla ricerca di un/una:

DIGITAL MARKETING SPECIALIST (m/f/d) - per la sede di Roma

che si occuperà di:
\n

Campaign supervisione & media planning
Progettare la strategia di digital advertising su canali paid (Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn, programmatic/display) in linea con gli obiettivi di marketing e vendita dell’azienda.
Gestire end-to-end le campagne, includendo pianificazione, setup, segmentazione audience, A/B test e ottimizzazione continua di keyword, bid e budget.
Monitorare le performance con strumenti di analytics (es. GA4, piattaforme adv, dashboard) e produrre report periodici con insight e raccomandazioni per il management.
Collaborare con Content e Social per sviluppare creatività e copy efficaci, coerenti con il tono di voce e le linee guida di brand.
Gestire e ottimizzare il media budget, garantendo efficienza degli investimenti e continuo miglioramento di KPI.
Rimanere aggiornato sulle novità di piattaforma (Google, Meta, ecc.), sulle best practice di settore e sulle opportunità offerte da nuove soluzioni e formati adv.
Coordinarsi con il team centrale di Amburgo in lingua inglese per l’implementazione e l’allineamento delle campagne

SEO & sito web:
Eseguire e monitorare la strategia SEO Italia, in collaborazione con il team centrale di HQ e l’agenzia esterna, garantendo coerenza con gli obiettivi di business e marketing del brand.
Analizzare i trend dei diversi mercati e comprendere le logiche di posizionamento di motori di ricerca multipli
Identificare opportunità di crescita per aumentare la visibilità organica e migliorare le performance dei siti e-commerce del brand.
Condurre analisi approfondite e produrre report SEO regolari, individuando trend e proponendo ottimizzazioni mirate.
Collaborare con i team interni (Content, Front-End, IT) e con l’agenzia per integrare le best practice SEO nei progetti digitali.
Ottimizzare contenuti, struttura e performance tecnica del sito, seguendo principi di on-page e off-page SEO.
Gestire keyword research, analisi dei competitor e sviluppo di strategie di contenuti multilingue.

Email marketing & newsletter:
Gestire la strategia newsletter & emailing, assicurando coerenza con gli obiettivi di marketing e le strategie di engagement del brand.
Progettare e implementare cluster e segmentazioni avanzate del database, ottimizzando la personalizzazione dei messaggi per target specifici coordinadosi con il team Operation
Sviluppare contenuti e campagne email mirate, in linea con i diversi segmenti di audience e gli obiettivi di lead, retention e fidelizzazione.
Monitorare e analizzare le performance delle campagne (open rate, click-through rate, lead generation), identificando trend, insight e opportunità di miglioramento.
Implementare strategie di ottimizzazione continua, testando creatività, oggetti, copy, call-to-action e flussi automatizzati per massimizzare l’efficacia delle comunicazioni.

Cosa cerchiamo:
Esperienza di almeno 3-4 anni in ambito digital marketing, advertising e CRM/email marketing, preferibilmente in contesti strutturati o internazionali.
Consolidata esperienza nella gestione di campagne digital paid su Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn e programmatic/display, con capacità di ottimizzazione dei KPI 
Competenze solide in SEO: gestione strategica, ottimizzazione on-page e off-page, keyword research, analisi competitor, strategie di contenuto multilingue e strumenti di monitoraggio SEO.
Esperienza nella gestione di newsletter e campagne email, con segmentazione avanzata, personalizzazione dei contenuti, analisi delle performance e implementazione di strategie di ottimizzazione continua.
Ottima conoscenza degli strumenti di analytics e reporting (es. GA4, piattaforme adv, dashboard), con capacità di produrre insight strategici.
Capacità di lavorare in team cross-funzionali, collaborando con Content, Social, IT e agenzie esterne.
Approccio analitico e proattivo, con orientamento ai risultati e capacità di problem solving.
Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese, sia scritta che parlata, indispensabile per collaborare con team internazionali.

\n
€30,000 - €39,999 a year
\n
Candidati oggi stesso per entrare a far parte della nostra storia di successo.


People & Culture Team
EV MMC Italia Srl
Corso Vittorio Emanuele II - Roma
Via de' Toschi 4 - Bologna
Lungarno Guicciardini - Firenze

title: Executive Assistant & Project Lead
locations_derived: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
description_text:
About Naspers
For more than a century, Naspers has been reinventing itself, evolving from a South African media pioneer founded in 1915 into a global consumer internet group and one of the world’s most influential technology investors.

Through Prosus, Naspers’ international consumer internet arm, the group improves everyday life for billions of people across the globe through cutting-edge technology. As one of the world’s largest technology investors, Prosus builds, acquires and supports industry-leading businesses that empower individuals, strengthen communities and drive long-term growth. Naspers continues to play a defining role in South Africa’s technology ecosystem, growing iconic local businesses such as Takealot, Mr D Food, AutoTrader and Property24, alongside Media24, the country’s leading print and digital media company.

Naspers is listed on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and A2X, with an ADR listing on the London Stock Exchange. Prosus is primarily listed on Euronext Amsterdam with a secondary listing on the Johannesburg Stock Exchange. Learn more at www.naspers.com and www.prosus.com.

The Opportunity
The Executive Assistant will serve as a key support figure to the CEO of Naspers SA, taking on a blend of responsibilities, including executive assistant tasks and project management. This role has a local and international focus. The role will involve strategic relationship building with international stakeholders and helping to drive the CEO’s vision by managing projects and engagements in a highly dynamic, fast-paced, global environment. This role requires an individual with exceptional organizational, communication and relationship management skills.

What you’ll do
\n

Executive Support
Provide proactive, confidential, and professional executive assistance to the CEO.
Manage complex international calendars, appointments, and meeting schedules across multiple time zones.
Handle correspondence, emails, and calls efficiently - prioritizing issues and ensuring timely follow-up.
Maintain meticulous records of projects, meetings and international engagement schedules.
Liaise with senior executives, Board members, clients and partners on behalf of the CEO with professionalism and tact.
Act as the main point of contact for internal and external communications on behalf of the CEO, ensuring professionalism and diplomacy at all times. 
Improve workflows and guarantee smooth communication between the CEO’s office and other internal and external stakeholders.

Travel, Events & Logistics
Plan and manage extensive domestic and international travel arrangements, including flight bookings, visas, accommodation, ground transport and detailed itineraries.
Ensure smooth logistics and contingency planning for multi-destination travel, conferences, and events.
Prepare comprehensive travel packs, including schedules, contacts and meeting briefs.
Coordinate expense reporting and travel reconciliations promptly and accurately.

Meeting and Event Management
Organize and support Board meetings, leadership offsites and executive events.
Take minutes, track actions, and ensure timely follow-up on key deliverables.
Support event planning and logistics for high-profile internal and external engagements.

Briefings, Reports & Presentations
Prepare and coordinate high-quality, accurate briefing materials, reports and executive-level presentations for internal and external meetings.
Synthesize complex information into clear, action-oriented summaries and talking points aligned to the CEO’s objectives.
Ensure materials meet quality and confidentiality standards and are delivered on time.

Relationship Management
Develop relationships with executive assistants and personal assistants of senior leaders, government officials, policymakers and other key partners to ensure smooth coordination and accessible connections. 
Contact base development - Create and maintain a robust international contact database for government relations, public policy stakeholders, and critical business networks. 
Act as an effective representative of the CEO, exuding tact, professionalism and the ability to work across cultural nuances. 
Maintain the highest level of discretion and confidentiality in handling sensitive information.
Act as an ambassador for the CEO’s office, ensuring communications and interactions reflect the company’s values and professionalism.

Project and Operations Management
Act as the Project Manager for initiatives within the CEO officeTrack and manage critical projects and initiatives, ensuring timely execution, reporting, and status updates for the CEO. 
Anticipate and address potential issues or roadblocks in projects, providing solutions and recommendations.
Facilitate cross-functional communication between departments, enabling efficient execution of key priorities. 

What you’ll bring
Bachelor’s Degree or National Diploma in Business Administration or similar.
Proven experience (minimum 10 years) as an Executive Assistant supporting a C-suite executive in a corporate international environment.
Demonstrated project/programme management experience is advantageous.Proven experience working with international stakeholders; experience working with the EU government or related public policy sectors is an advantage.
Demonstrated experience managing complex travel and logistics.
Demonstrated track record of successfully building and managing relationships with external and governmental stakeholders.
Strong proficiency in Google Workspace and collaboration tools (Zoom, Slack, etc.).Confidently work across different cultures and time zones with tact and diplomacy.
Solid experience handling confidential information and managing senior-level communications.

Your strengths
Strong organization and project management skills.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Calm and composed under pressure.
Trustworthy and reliable, with impeccable integrity.
Proven ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion.
Flexible, resourceful, and able to adapt to changing priorities.
Professional, polished and confident with a proven track record in senior stakeholder engagement. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and handle multiple projects. Self-motivated with a focus on continuous improvement.

\n
Our Culture & Values
Our work is guided by a clear set of values: Innovation, Results, People, Impact and Entrepreneurship. These values shape how we think, how we build and how we lead. We believe progress happens when curious minds are empowered to take ownership, challenge assumptions and embrace change. We foster a collaborative, high-performance environment where diverse perspectives are welcomed and inclusion is not optional, it’s essential to our success.

Our commitment to building a diverse and inclusive workforce
We think about diversity and inclusion broadly and respect the dignity and human rights of individuals and communities wherever we operate in the world. Naspers is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. To this end, all positions will be filled in accordance with our Employment Equity plan. We also encourage candidates with disabilities to apply.

title: Veterinary Surgeon - Large Animal / Mixed
description_text:
Large Animal / Mixed Veterinary Surgeon 

Glenshane Veterinary Clinic – Northern Ireland 
Full-time or Part-time | Permanent 
Salary up to £65,000 DOE 
Relocation allowance + welcome bonus available 
Visa sponsorship considered 

A Proper Farm Vet Role – With Support, Variety and Balance 
Glenshane Veterinary Clinic is seeking an experienced Large Animal or Mixed Veterinary Surgeon to join our established, friendly team in Northern Ireland. This is an opportunity for a vet who genuinely enjoys farm work and wants to practice it well – with the time, tools and team support to do the job properly. 

While the role has a strong farm animal focus, there is flexibility for those who enjoy keeping some companion animal work in their week. Whether you’re looking for a predominantly farm role or a true mixed position, we’re open to shaping the job around the right person. 

This role is not suitable for new graduates. We’re looking for a vet who is confident working independently on farm, enjoys building long-term client relationships, and takes pride in practical, hands-on veterinary work. 

The Practice – Established, Well Equipped and Genuinely Supportive 
Glenshane Vets is a three-branch mixed practice based at the foot of the Sperrin Mountains, serving a loyal farming community with a strong mix of beef, sheep and dairy clients. Our clients range from large commercial units to smaller family-run farms, making the work varied, interesting and rewarding. 

You’ll be supported by an experienced team of vets, nurses and support staff who value teamwork, communication and a realistic approach to workload. We have invested in equipment that allows our vets to work efficiently and confidently, including EasyScan, cattle crush and stocks, small ruminant operating table, IDEXX lab station, digital X-ray, small animal ultrasound, and modern practice management systems. 

A practice vehicle is provided, and re have a relocation allowance available to help anyone move to the area. 

The Role – Hands-On, Varied and Community-Focused 
This is a client-facing farm vet role where you’ll be out on the road, managing routine and emergency farm work, herd health support and advisory services. You’ll have the autonomy to manage your own cases while always having experienced colleagues to lean on when needed. 

For vets who wish to maintain some companion animal involvement, the role can include small animal consults and procedures within the clinics. The balance between farm and small animal work can be tailored depending on your experience and interests. 

A full, valid UK driving licence is essential, as daily travel between farms and clinic sites is a core part of the role. 

Rota & Work–Life Balance 
The standard rota is designed to be fair and sustainable. You’ll work a 40-hour week, with 1 night on call per week and 1 weekend in 4, followed by time off in lieu. We are open to full-time or part-time arrangements, and we’re happy to have an open conversation about what works best for you. 


Who We’re Looking For 
This role will suit a vet who: 
*Has experience in farm or large animal practice 
*Is confident working independently on farm calls 
*Enjoys building strong relationships with farming clients 
*Holds a valid driving licence for the UK (Northern Ireland) 
*Is looking for a long-term role within a supportive, down-to-earth team 

Visa sponsorship can be considered for suitably experienced overseas candidates. This position is not suited to new graduate veterinary surgeons.

Because Our People Matter 
We recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our people, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. In addition to a competitive base salary, you will benefit from – 

Work-life balance 
*7 weeks annual leave 
*Additional holiday entitlement for your birthday 

Wellbeing 
*Enhanced family-friendly policies, including maternity/paternity/adoption/shared parental and surrogacy pay 
*Private Medical Insurance 
*Cycle to Work scheme 
*Initiatives focused on employee wellbeing, including discounted gym membership 
*Non-accidental injury support 

Development 
*£1250 CPD allowance with 40 hours paid CPD leave pro rata 
*Certificate support 
*Learning and development opportunities via the IVC Evidensia Academy 
*Access to Vetlexicon – our online encyclopaedia provider 
*All our Vets & Nurses can apply for research funding 
*Infection Prevention and Control Programme 
*Quality Improvement Programme 
Professional Memberships 
*VDS cover 
*RCVS fees 
*AVSPNI/NIVA 

Additional 
*Pawsome Benefits – reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with 100’s of retailers 
*Pension 5% Employee/3% Employer 
*Discounted staff pet care 
\n

\n
£0 - £65,000 a year
Salary up to £65,000 DOE
\n

40 hours paid CPD leave pro rata

title: Design Verification Engineer (EDI)
description_text:
For over four decades, Cirrus Logic has been propelled by the top engineers in mixed-signal processing. Our rockstar team thrives on solving complex challenges with innovative end-user solutions for the world's top consumer brands. Cirrus Logic is also known for its award-winning culture, built on a foundation of inclusion and fairness, meaningful community engagement, and delivering enjoyable employee experiences at every turn. But we couldn’t do it without our extraordinary workforce – and that’s where you come in. Join our team and help us continue to make Cirrus Logic an exceptional place to grow your career! 

Cirrus Logic has exciting opportunities for talented Design Verification Engineers at all levels, to join our Edinburgh and Newbury offices. You will participate in all aspects of digital verification for complete mixed signal IC developments, work on complex verification systems and contribute towards improvements in verification methodology within Cirrus Logic. This is a real chance for you to get heavily involved in cutting-edge projects working in a global organisation. In return, we offer you a great range of benefits including personal and professional development, a uniquely flat culture and much, much more!
\n

Responsibilities: 
Definition of IC verification plan linking product requirements through to detailed testcases
Leading teams of verification engineers to deliver thoroughly verified ICs
Create reliable and reusable testbench for complex subsystems and ICs
Participate in verification Specialist Groups and contribute to the digital verification methodology discussions
Supporting, and where necessary coaching, the verification team to follow, and improve, defined methodology practices
Hands-on project design/verification involvement

Required Skills and Qualifications 
BEng / BSc / MEng / MSc Degree or equivalent in Electronics/Computer Science or other related discipline.
Proven track record in delivering 1st time success with complex mixed signal IC’s.
Metric driven verification - verification planning, requirements extraction - Directed and constrained random verification - Functional and code coverage analysis
SystemVerilog - SVA (SystemVerilog Assertions)
Testbench design with verification frameworks like UVM/OVM, e, VMM
Debugging skills - RTL - Testbench, OOP - Gate level (including SDF)
Strong ability to interpret results and resolve problems
An innovative, creative, lateral thinking problem solver

Preferred Skills and Qualifications 
Formal verification and verification qualification techniques
Scripting experience with Ruby, sh/csh, TCL, Make, Perl
Power aware verification (using CPF/UPF)
Object orientated programming (OOP) - Use of OOP design patterns


\n
This position is based in either Edinburgh or Newbury, UK. 
This is a hybrid remote position and will follow a 2+ day in-office work schedule, with in-office days based on business needs and team preference. You must be based within commutable distance of the work location listed on the job posting, or willing to relocate prior to beginning employment with Cirrus Logic.

#LI-Hybrid
#LI-PD1
HOTT

Export control restrictions based upon applicable laws and regulations would prohibit candidates who are nationals of certain embargoed countries from working in this position without Cirrus Logic first obtaining an export license. Candidates for this role must be able to access technical data without a requirement for an export license. We are unable to sponsor or obtain export licenses for this role.

At Cirrus Logic, we believe that diversity drives innovation, and we are committed to encouraging an open and collaborative culture where different approaches, ideas, and points of view are respected and valued. We aim to promote a workplace where everyone can contribute irrespective of race, colour, national origin, religion or belief, gender or gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, pregnancy status, or disability.
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves participating in all aspects of digital verification for complete mixed-signal IC developments, working on complex verification systems, and contributing to improvements in verification methodology. Responsibilities include defining the IC verification plan, leading verification teams, and creating reliable and reusable testbenches for complex subsystems and ICs.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a BEng/BSc/MEng/MSc Degree or equivalent in Electronics/Computer Science or a related discipline, along with a proven track record in successfully delivering complex mixed-signal ICs. Essential technical skills include expertise in metric-driven verification, SystemVerilog/SVA, UVM/OVM frameworks, and strong debugging capabilities across RTL and gate level.
