title: Veterinary Receptionist (Part-Time)
description_text:
We are hiring!

Here at Abington Park, we are looking for a friendly and approachable Veterinary Receptionist to join our fantastic team!
As the first point of contact, our receptionists play a vital role in ensuring that our clients and their pets have a welcoming and positive experience. We are looking for someone with excellent communication skills, a warm and compassionate demeanour, and the ability to juggle multiple tasks efficiently in a busy environment. 

Main responsibilities of the role include but are not limited to; 
*Liaising with the clients and the clinical staff to provide a high level of customer care.
*Meeting and greeting clients and pets.
*Booking appointments and diary management.
*Managing telephone and email enquiries.
*Taking payment for services and products.
*Managing cash and end-of-day banking.
*Maintaining a clean and safe space for our clients and their pets.

About the rota

This is a part-time permanent role working 25 hours per week. We are fully flexible within opening times, including Saturday on rota with time in lieu during the week. 

Want to know more about us?

We are situated in picturesque Northamptonshire, offering a wide range of sporting, recreational and cultural activities for all, with great links to London and Birmingham. We have a great team of 13 Veterinary Surgeons (majority of them are certificate holders), 18 Registered Veterinary Nurses, 4 Student Veterinary Nurses, 4 Kennel Assistants and a Lab Technician. 

In addition to a competitive salary, you will benefit from:
*6.6 Weeks annual leave FTE
*Additional annual leave for your birthday which will be added to your holiday allowance
*Enhanced family-friendly policies, including maternity/ paternity/ adoption/ shared parental and surrogacy pay 
*Cycle to work scheme 
*Initiatives focused on employee wellbeing 
*Non-accidental injury support 
*Learning and development opportunities via the IVC Evidensia Academy 
*Pawsome Benefits - reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with 100's of retailers 
*Pension scheme 
*Discounted staff pet care
\n

\n
£0 - £0 a year
Up to £16,356 per annum
\n

Reward gateway with discounts and cashback

Responsibilities also include managing appointments, handling communications via phone and email, processing payments, and maintaining the cleanliness and safety of the premises.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate must possess excellent communication skills and a warm, compassionate demeanor suitable for interacting with clients and their pets. Efficiency in juggling multiple tasks within a busy environment is essential for managing bookings, inquiries, and financial transactions.

title: Veterinary Receptionist
description_text:
Do you like working with people? And love animals? We are currently looking for a full-time receptionist to join our team.

You will be part of a fast-paced and friendly team, responsible for being the first point of contact to our clients. You will be booking appointments and dealing with a variety of enquiries either face-to-face, over the phone or via email. Maintaining client records and taking payments when required whilst ensuring that our clients receive the excellent service we are known for.

A practice you are proud to be a part of

We have a wonderful ethos at Grove Lodge, creating a safe and open culture for all our team. We have 17 Vets and 26 Nurses including SVN’s and further supported by VCA’s and an Administrative Support team. As a team of encouraging, supportive, and exceptionally knowledgeable individuals we pride ourselves in offering the best care to our clients and their pets. 

More about you 

We're looking for someone with great customer service skills, who can show empathy and handle emotional situations. Experience in a customer-facing role, both in person and over the phone, is a plus. You should be passionate about helping people and animals, have solid IT skills, and be able to record data accurately. Attention to detail and being a good team player are important. If you know a bit about the veterinary industry, that's a bonus, but not a must!
This exciting position will require you to work at different sites, therefore a car is essential for this role. You could be asked to cover any one of our seven sites which are situated in Brighton, Portslade, Southwick, Lancing, Broadwater, Tarring and Durrington.

Work pattern 

You will work 40 hours per week. Monday to Friday, 9 am to 6 pm with occasional flexibility, including covering Saturday mornings. You may, from time to time, be required to work shifts across our various sites, depending on the needs of the business.

Feel valued and rewarded 

We recognise that our people are fundamental to the success of our business. Investing in our people, premises and processes is at the heart of what we do. 

In addition to a competitive salary, you will benefit from:

*6.6 weeks annual leave 
*Additional annual leave for your birthday which will be added to your holiday allowance
*Enhanced family-friendly policies, including maternity/ paternity/ adoption/ shared parental and surrogacy pay 
*Cycle to work scheme 
*Initiatives focused on employee wellbeing 
*Non-accidental injury support 
*Learning and development opportunities via the IVC Evidensia Academy 
*Pawsome Benefits - reward gateway giving discounts and cashback with 100's of retailers 
*Pension scheme 
*Discounted staff pet care
\n

\n
£0 - £0 a year
Up to £25,994.40 DOE
\n
ai_benefits: Additional annual leave for your birthday
ai_core_responsibilities: The receptionist will serve as the primary point of contact for clients, managing tasks such as booking appointments, handling various inquiries via multiple channels, maintaining client records, and processing payments. They must ensure clients receive excellent service consistent with the practice's standards.
ai_requirements_summary: The ideal candidate needs strong customer service skills, empathy for handling emotional situations, and experience in a customer-facing role, preferably both in person and over the phone. Essential requirements include solid IT skills, accurate data recording, attention to detail, being a good team player, and possessing a car for travel between the seven required sites.

title: Media Planning Associate
description_text:
WHO WE ARE 

At Basis, we empower agencies and brands with cutting-edge software that automates digital media operations. Our all-in-one platform supports seamless planning, reporting, and financial reconciliation across direct, programmatic, search, and social media.

We’re not just building advanced technology; we’re also building a culture where passionate, motivated individuals come together to drive meaningful change. Our commitment to inclusivity and growth ensures our employees thrive both professionally and personally.

With our headquarters in Chicago, we offer flexible work options across the U.S. and Canada, including remote, hybrid, or on-site at headquarters.

ABOUT THE TEAM
The Strategic Media Services (SMS) team provides strategic partnership and integrated media solutions to our diverse suite of clients. We deliver Raving Fan Service by helping clients challenge their media objectives and realize their business ambitions. Powered by best-in-class research and planning tools, SMS works in partnership with our sales team to tailor our tech enabled services approach to drive better outcomes and media campaigns.

WAYS YOU'LL CONTRIBUTE
This position supports the media planning process and plays an important role in driving client satisfaction. You'll help interpret campaign performance data, media briefs, and industry insights to guide strategic thinking, while contributing to execution and communication across internal and client-facing workstreams. You'll contribute by:
\n

Developing a foundational understanding of how digital channels work together to deliver an integrated media plan including: programmatic display, video, CTV, audio, paid social, and paid search.
Supporting media proposal creation for assigned accounts through PowerPoint slides or Word deliverables, while shadowing Integrated Media Planners to learn the process for more complex accounts.
Building familiarity with media metrics and assisting in the development of performance measurement plans. 
Managing internal workflows and timelines for assigned accounts, ensuring that tasks stay on track and materials are delivered on time.
Acting as a primary point of contact for day-to-day needs on assigned accounts, while partnering with Integrated Media Planners on more complex engagements.
Staying current on the digital media landscape and the latest Basis Tech capabilities to help identify opportunities and support account growth.
Gaining visibility into internal processes across planning, buying, reporting, and delivery--serving as a key connector between internal teams and client needs.

WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TABLE
Strategic thinking and proactive client service skills
Proven communication skills, particularly in a client-facing manner
Strong issue resolution and problem-solving capabilities
Ability to work well in a cross functional team environment
Passion for learning and staying up to date on media research and systems
Intermediate MS Excel and PowerPoint skills
Commitment to creating inclusive, respectful environments where all voices are valued and supported.
Thoughtful approach to collaboration, design, and decision-making that prioritizes equity, access, and continuous learning.

BONUS POINTS
6+ months of experience in digital media planning and/or buying, with programmatic, search and/or social experience being a plus
Hands on DSP buying experience
Previous holistic media planning experience
Experience in digital reporting dashboards such as Datorama
Experience buying Paid Search and/or Paid Social ads
Basis Certified

\n
$51,227 - $70,694 a year
This salary range reflects a typical compensation range for the role and level in most U.S. locations. Final compensation offered will depend on your location, experience, and skills. Total compensation may also include bonuses, equity, and competitive benefits.
\n
Integrity in Hiring
At Basis, integrity is at the core of how we hire. To protect our candidates and our company, we verify identity, education, and employment history for all hires. Some roles may also require onsite or in-person interviews as part of the process. Any misrepresentation of identity, experience, or skills may result in removal from the hiring process or termination of employment. We value transparency and authenticity and expect the same from every applicant.

Anything Else?
Don’t have every skill listed? No problem! We know experience can be built in many ways. If you have relevant skills that aren't reflected in your resume, we encourage you to share them in an optional cover letter. 

Life with Basis 
We’re committed to our people’s growth and well-being because our success is tied to theirs. That’s why we’ve earned recognition as a top workplace, including: Ad Age, #1 Best Places to Work 2025, WorkLife's #1 Most Committed to Work Life Balance, and the Chicago Tribune's Top Workplaces in Chicago. We offer competitive perks, including a flexible work week, 401k/RRSP matching, mental health support, paid sabbaticals, generous parental leave, flexible work options, and more.

Basis is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate all team members regardless of gender identity, sexual orientation, race or cultural background, religion, disability, age, and beyond.

title: Sr. Recruiter
description_text:
The Senior Recruiter is a high-ownership, execution-driven individual contributor role designed for an experienced recruiter who thrives in a PE-backed environment and takes pride in building disciplined, high-quality hiring outcomes. 

You’ll own full-cycle recruiting for roles primarily in the United States, with occasional hiring support in LatAm and EMEA – personally driving sourcing, screening, and candidate engagement while applying strong judgment to identify talent aligned with business needs and performance standards. 

PetDesk follows Who: The A Method for Hiring, a four-step hiring framework designed to consistently hire A Players. This role will serve as an expert practitioner of the Who method—ensuring it is applied rigorously across searches and helping hiring managers understand how to use the framework effectively.

This role requires comfort operating through the ebbs and flows of hiring demand. You’ll proactively build pipelines during slower periods, refine processes, and continuously raise the bar—ensuring PetDesk’s recruiting engine remains strong and disciplined at all times. 
\n

Apply if you’re excited to:
Own full-cycle recruiting for PetDesk roles, from intake through offer and close, with accountability for quality, speed, and candidate experience
Apply and champion the Who hiring framework, ensuring consistent use of scorecards, structured interviews, reference discipline, and A-Player standards across searches
Serve as a trusted advisor to hiring managers, guiding role definition, interview strategy, and providing clear, constructive challenges when needed
Build and manage strong candidate pipelines using LinkedIn Recruiter and proactive sourcing strategies aligned to the company’s ideal employee profile
Evaluate high volumes of applicants with sound judgment, identifying candidates who meet A-Player criteria and business requirements
Optimize and maintain the ATS (Lever), ensuring clean workflows, accurate data, effective reporting, and a strong experience for candidates and hiring managers
Continuously improve recruiting operations, leveraging AI, automation, documentation, and process enhancements to increase efficiency, quality, and consistency

4–6 years of full-cycle recruiting experience, ideally within a private-equity-backed or high-growth environment
Demonstrated success hiring high-performing talent, with strong judgment around role fit and long-term performance
Experience applying structured hiring methodologies (experience with Who: The A Method for Hiring is a strong plus)
Strong expertise using LinkedIn Recruiter (or Recruiter Lite) and proactive sourcing strategies
Proven ability to manage and evaluate large applicant volumes without compromising quality or fairness
Comfort operating in environments with shifting priorities and variable hiring demand
Great attention to detail, documentation, data integrity, and operational rigor
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a polished, professional presence and respect for employer brand standards

\n
$94,000 - $110,000 a year
\n
PetDesk offers an ecosystem of top-tier client engagement solutions for the pet healthcare and services communities, enhancing communication, service quality, and client retention. With its cutting-edge solutions, we support and serve more than 10,000 veterinary clinics and over 400 grooming facilities, and serve over 20 million pet parents worldwide.

Our recruiting process is rooted in “Who: The A Method of Hiring” and consists of an average 2-week hiring timeline. This standardized interview process allows candidates to answer the same questions, experience the same process, and ensure a fair performance review from multiple perspectives.

Please, no external recruiters—candidate profiles submitted from external recruiting agencies will not be considered.

title: Prematch Trader - Peru
description_text:
Oddin.gg is the global leader in B2B esports betting solutions.
We deliver an end to end ecosystem to bookmakers across the globe, helping them attract a new generation of bettors, drive betting volumes 24/7, and improve sportsbook profitability.

We work with purely esports titles such as Dota2, LoL, CS2, as well as esports analogues of traditional sports like EA FC 24 (soccer), NBA 2K (basketball), Cricket 22 and more. Based on the data we have available, we create and continuously improve mathematical models that help us predict match odds.

With a team of 300 plus people across Europe, Asia, and LATAM, Oddin.gg is growing fast and is now looking for a Prematch Trader to strengthen our trading operations.

Your position on the board
As a Prematch Trader, you will support our Prematch trading team by operating esports events for clients worldwide through our proprietary trading platform. You will play a key role in ensuring accuracy, consistency, and performance of prematch odds while working closely with a highly collaborative international team.

This role requires strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and a genuine passion for esports.
\n

Our idea of you
Track and analyze esports matches across assigned titles
Monitor and manage esports prematch lines
Create and adjust odds and actively participate in odds modelling
Apply deep esports knowledge to prematch events and market decisions
Continuously educate yourself on esports titles, teams, and competitive environments
Test new markets and discuss findings and improvements with the team
Contribute to the development of new trading projects within esports
Research and assess valuable information related to players, teams, and competitions

What do we expect from you
Strong knowledge of one or two major esports titles such as Dota 2, LoL, CS GO, Valorant, R6, or CoD
Ability to analyze markets and data and draw sound conclusions
Confident decision making based on multiple information sources
Proactive mindset with daily information seeking habits
High level of reliability and accountability
Time flexibility to support global operations
Daily communication in English with internal teams and clients

You will impress us if you have:
Previous experience in the betting industry, ideally esports betting
Experience in a similar trading or analytical role

\n
Haven’t We Caught You Yet? 
- A hands-on role in an esports analytics company, operating in a global environment.
- Full training and specialization provided, no specific degree required. If you’re passionate and skilled in esports, we’ll teach you the rest.
- Clear professional growth paths and opportunities to advance across multiple departments.
- Daily exposure to international clients, partners, and projects within the esports ecosystem.
- A dynamic, young, and collaborative team culture that values initiative and learning.
- Competitive salary and quarterly performance-based bonuses.
- Office located in San Isidro with hybrid work flexibility available based on performance and role maturity.
- Transparent, merit-based feedback culture and career development support.

This involves tracking and analyzing matches, monitoring lines, creating and adjusting odds, and actively participating in odds modeling.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess strong knowledge of major esports titles like Dota 2, LoL, CS GO, or Valorant, along with the ability to analyze markets and data to draw sound conclusions. The role requires confident decision-making, a proactive mindset, high reliability, time flexibility for global operations, and daily communication in English.

linkedin_org_description:
Oddin.gg is the number one B2B choice for esports betting, enabling the growth of its partners by providing an engaging esports betting experience through its end-to-end esports betting ecosystem. Oddin.gg’s clients benefit from its multi-award winning esports odds feed, risk management, iFrame solution, widgets, 24/7 content, live esports data, and marketing services.

The company maximizes its partners’ profitability by combining automated infrastructure and machine learning algorithms with advanced data science. Oddin.gg powers solutions for Betsson, Betway, Yolo Group, Stake, OpenBet, Vibra Gaming, Betby, and many more sportsbook and platform providers on a global scale.

Oddin.gg is licensed in New Jersey, Colorado, West Virginia, Ohio, Arizona, US, and Ontario, Canada, and is in the process of acquiring multiple more licenses in North America.

Our mindset

Born from a deep understanding of esports and a burning desire to build something meaningful, Oddin.gg was founded in 2018 with a singular goal: to transform our experience and adventurous spirit into something bold, exciting, and lasting. Since day one, we've been driven by curiosity, ambition, and a relentless pursuit of excellence.

We're not just building a company; we're creating an environment where talented people come together, prioritize delivery, get things done, and take full ownership. Here, focus, accountability, and teamwork aren't just buzzwords—they're the very pillars of how we operate. We champion those who take initiative, care deeply about their impact, and know how to enjoy the process. Because when our people thrive, Oddin.gg truly soars.

Oddin.gg is growing—and fast. In just a few years, we've exploded from 50 to nearly 300 team members, and we're not hitting the brakes. We're launching new verticals, evolving our processes, and unlocking fresh opportunities for our people to learn, experiment, and shine.

title: Formulation Development Technician
description_text:
Company Overview 

At Revive RX, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare by delivering exceptional, personalized wellness solutions through our state-of-the-art compounding pharmacy services. Headquartered in Houston, TX, we specialize in wellness optimization, hormone therapy, IV hydration, and performance-based medicine. Our team of passionate professionals is committed to advancing patient outcomes through innovation, collaboration, and excellence in everything we do. Join us as we continue to grow and shape the future of health and wellness.


Formulation Development Technician

Revive RX
Houston, TX (On-Site)
Full-Time


The Opportunity
We are seeking a talented Formulation Development Technician to join our newly established Research & Development division. This is a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of pharmaceutical science and innovation—transforming drug concepts into viable, patient-ready formulations.
As a Formulation Development Technician, you will operate in a dedicated research lab environment, separate from daily compounding operations. You'll be the critical bridge between ideation and implementation, conducting hands-on formulation development, analyzing results, and collaborating with our sterile and non-sterile production teams to bring new products to life.
This role is ideal for a self-directed pharmaceutical professional who thrives in a research-focused environment and is passionate about advancing the science of compounding pharmacy.

What You'll Do
Formulation Research & Development
Design, test, and refine pharmaceutical formulations in a controlled laboratory setting
Conduct hands-on compounding to evaluate formulation viability, stability, and efficacy
Serve as the bridge between conceptual drug development and operational production
Data Analysis & Documentation
Prepare comprehensive reports analyzing formulation successes and failures
Document research findings, methodologies, and recommendations with precision
Maintain detailed records in compliance with pharmaceutical R&D standards
Present findings to stakeholders clearly and effectively
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Submit validated formulations to sterile and non-sterile compounding teams for operational implementation
Coordinate lab usage and scheduling with production teams
Partner with leadership to prioritize development projects aligned with business objectives
Laboratory Management
Manage significant R&D documentation and regulatory paperwork
Maintain organized laboratory records and research files
Ensure compliance with safety protocols and quality standards


Required Qualifications
Hands-on compounding experience in a pharmacy or laboratory setting
Proficiency with Microsoft Word and Excel for data management and reporting
Proven experience presenting findings and data to teams or stakeholders
Self-starter with exceptional time management and the ability to work independently
Strong communication and leadership skills to collaborate across departments
Ability to execute formulation protocols with minimal supervision
Preferred Qualifications
Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT) credential
Experience with PK (pharmacokinetics) software or similar analytical tools
Sterile compounding certification (e.g., USP 797)
Background in pharmaceutical research, formulation science, or product development
Experience in a compounding pharmacy or pharmaceutical manufacturing environment

Why Join Revive RX?
Innovation-Driven Culture: Be part of a forward-thinking organization at the forefront of personalized medicine
New R&D Division: Ground-floor opportunity to help build and shape our research capabilities
Professional Growth: Develop expertise in formulation science within a rapidly growing company
Impactful Work: Your formulations will directly improve patient outcomes and expand our product offerings
Collaborative Environment: Work alongside passionate professionals committed to healthcare excellence

Work Environment & Schedule
Location: Houston, TX – On-site position in our dedicated research laboratory
Schedule: Standard business hours with flexibility to accommodate lab availability
Environment: Research lab setting separate from daily compounding operations, allowing for focused development work


About the Role
This is more than a lab technician position—it's an opportunity to be a pharmaceutical innovator. You'll have the autonomy to problem-solve, experiment, and contribute to the next generation of compounding pharmacy products. If you're passionate about pharmaceutical science, thrive in independent research environments, and want to see your work make a tangible difference in patient care, we want to hear from you.

Ready to transform pharmaceutical concepts into patient solutions?
Revive RX is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace.
\n

\n
Why Join Revive RX?
· Work with a passionate team dedicated to transforming lives through wellness innovation.
· Shape the future of a growing and mission-driven company.
· Competitive compensation, benefits, and a collaborative work environment.
· Opportunities for leadership, creativity, and long-term impact.

This role involves preparing comprehensive reports, maintaining detailed records, and submitting validated formulations to sterile and non-sterile compounding teams.

A self-starter mentality with strong time management and independent work capability is essential for success in this research-focused role.

title: Cyber Engineering and Automation Manager (Remote, Full-Time) [HRPP]
description_text:
About Smart Working

At Smart Working, we believe your job should not only look right on paper but also feel right every day. This isn’t just another remote opportunity - it’s about finding where you truly belong, no matter where you are. From day one, you’re welcomed into a genuine community that values your growth and well-being.

Our mission is simple: to break down geographic barriers and connect skilled professionals with outstanding global teams and products for full-time, long-term roles. We help you discover meaningful work with teams that invest in your success, where you’re empowered to grow personally and professionally.
Join one of the highest-rated workplaces on Glassdoor and experience what it means to thrive in a truly remote-first world.

About the role

As a Cyber Engineering and Automation Manager, this is a high-impact, greenfield leadership role for a senior cybersecurity professional with strong hands-on engineering capability. The role sits at the heart of Security Operations, with responsibility for defining and implementing how central SOC services collaborate with supporting functions across the organisation.

You will shape the end-to-end operating model for security operations, with a strong focus on automation, tooling, and scalable delivery across cyber threat intelligence and incident response. A key early priority will be onboarding and stabilising a new Managed Security Services Provider (MSSP), followed by building the foundations for stronger internal security capabilities over time.
\n

Responsibilities
Define and implement the operating model for collaboration between the central SOC and supporting security functions
Design and deliver automation solutions to streamline SOC activities such as alert triage, incident response, and reporting
Lead the onboarding and transition of a new MSSP, including: Defining operational procedures and service delivery models, Establishing KPIs and SLAs, Acting as a key point of escalation and partnership
Own the optimisation and ongoing operation of SOC platforms, including SIEM tooling
Oversee BAU maintenance and operational health of security platforms (e.g. Splunk, cloud security tooling)
Integrate security tools such as SIEM, SOAR, firewalls, and related platforms to improve data flow and response coordination
Create and maintain automated incident response playbooks for common threat scenarios
Develop and maintain SOC knowledge bases and documentation repositories
Enhance SOC workflows to reduce manual effort and improve response efficiency
Monitor performance of automation tooling and resolve operational issues
Collaborate closely with SOC analysts, engineers, and wider security teams to identify automation opportunities
Build trusted relationships with stakeholders across operating companies to align SOC services with business risk and priorities
Contribute to longer-term strategy and business cases for strengthening in-house SOC capabilities

Requirements
Strong experience designing and delivering SOC automation solutions
Hands-on expertise with automation and orchestration tooling (e.g. SOAR platforms, Ansible, Phantom)
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python, PowerShell, or Bash
Deep understanding of SOC processes, including incident response, threat detection, and triage
Practical experience working with SIEM platforms, particularly Splunk
Experience integrating and automating security tools across complex environments
Proven ability to develop automated workflows and response playbooks
Strong knowledge of security frameworks such as MITRE ATT&CK and NIST
Experience with log management and event correlation automation
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong communication and collaboration skills, able to work across technical and non-technical teams

Nice to have
Experience operating in large, distributed, or regulated enterprise environments
Exposure to cloud security operations across multiple cloud accounts
Experience contributing to SOC transformation or greenfield security programmes
Background working with MSSPs and transitioning services in-house over time

Benefits
Fixed Shifts: 12:00 PM - 9:30 PM IST (Summer) | 1:00 PM - 10:30 PM IST (Winter)
No Weekend Work: Real work-life balance, not just words
Day 1 Benefits: Laptop and full medical insurance provided
Support That Matters:Mentorship, community, and forums where ideas are shared
True Belonging: A long-term career where your contributions are valued

\n
At Smart Working, you’ll never be just another remote hire.

Be a Smart Worker - valued, empowered, and part of a culture that celebrates integrity, excellence, and ambition.

If that sounds like your kind of place, we’d love to hear your story.

title: Customer Experience Admin
description_text:
Window Nation is growing fast! Join a high energy team where your ideas and contributions will make a lasting impact on our future. 


One Goal, One Passion – Growth is Everything at Window Nation
At Window Nation, we don’t just replace windows and doors – we help transform homes into spaces people love. With nearly 20 years of craftsmanship, innovation, and competitively priced solutions, we’ve enhanced the comfort, safety, and value of homes for customers nationwide. In doing so, we’ve become a trusted leader in home improvement, striving to be the provider of choice for all window and door replacements.
As we continue to grow and expand into new markets in 2026, we’re looking for passionate, driven team members who thrive in a high-growth environment. Join a company where your expertise is valued, your contributions matter, and your work makes a lasting impact – on customers and the future of home improvement. 



The Customer Experience Admin serves as the first point of contact and trusted liaison for Window Nation's customers, past and present. In this role, you'll connect directly with homeowners to ensure their questions and concerns are resolved quickly and professionally. In partnership with our Customer Excellence Team, you'll help deliver smooth, timely, and positive experiences at every touchpoint. This role calls for someone who is empathetic, detail-oriented, and genuinely passionate about providing exceptional service and communication. 

This position will work out of our company headquarters in Fulton, MD. Candidates in Maple Lawn, Columbia, Scaggsville, Laurel, Clarksville, Silver Spring, College Park, Baltimore, Glen Burnie, Bowie, Odenton, Crofton, and surrounding areas are encouraged to apply!
\n

Core Role Responsibilities
Handles post-installation customer communications, triaging inbound phone calls, chat, and emails to drive a one-call resolution environment. 
Review and action email correspondence appropriately ensuring all communication from customers is being addressed swiftly to assist both the customer and company, leading to faster resolutions and an elevated level of customer satisfaction.
Liaise between Window Nation's post-installation customers and support teams.
Enter data and upload documents accurately into systems related to customer interactions to ensure that all details are accurately documented to resolve customer needs. 
Operate with empathy and courtesy toward consumers and colleagues in all interactions to serve the goals of the organization and provide outstanding customer experience.

Basic Qualifications
High school Diploma, GED, or equivalent required
1+ years of customer service experience to include call centers, customer care or help desks


Preferred Qualifications
Experience working with a CRM system or other customer tracking software
Experience working with Microsoft Suite products
Ability to organize multiple activities with minimal supervision
Demonstrated success in deadline-driven environment
Customer-centric mindset
Strong attention to detail
Ability to communicate with customers with a high level of empathy and understanding, at times deescalating when needed

\n
$20 - $20 an hour
Pay starting at $20.00 per hour
\n
#LI-NB1

What We Offer:
- Competitive pay* and performance-based bonus opportunities 
- Full benefits package including medical, dental, vision, life, and 401(k) retirement options 
- Paid time off 
- Growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding company 
- A supportive team culture where your contributions matter and craftsmanship is celebrated 

*Window Nation provides competitive compensation and benefits. The compensation range offered for this position will be based on relevant skills, experience, qualifications, and location, and will be disclosed during the interview process. 

Why Window Nation? 
At Window Nation, we know that when our people grow, our company grows. We’re committed to helping you build a rewarding career while being part of a team that values hard work, reliability, and collaboration. 
Window Nation provides competitive compensation and benefits. 

Window Nation cultivates a culture of inclusion that respects our employees' individual strengths, views, and experiences. We believe our differences make us a better team – one that makes good decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results. 

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, sex, national origin, religion, age, disability, genetic information, status as a military veteran or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. 

At this time, Window Nation is unable to provide sponsorship for employment visas now or in the future. Candidates must be authorized to work in the United States on a permanent basis without the need for current or future visa sponsorship.

linkedin_org_description:
Founded in 2000, Vohra Wound Physicians is a physician-led, wound care specialty practice with 300+ physicians serving post-acute facilities nationwide. Vohra provides bedside clinical care to hundreds of thousands of patients annually at nearly 3,000 skilled nursing facilities across 28 states and patients healing at home via telemedicine. 

Supported by proprietary technologies and extensive, ongoing physician training, Vohra delivers results, including a 21-day improvement in healing time and an 88% reduction in wound-related hospitalizations. 

Our mission is to improve patient outcomes and reduce hospitalizations while reducing costs for facilities and home health partners. 

What Makes Vohra Great for Physicians: 
Work-Life Balance: enjoy autonomy and flexibility with no nights, call, or weekends. 
Clinical Satisfaction: focus on helping patients heal without the administrative burden of paperwork. 
Compensation: earn an average of $300,000 annually for full-time roles plus full W2 benefits. 
Lifelong Learning: join a specialized wound care training program followed by a one-year fellowship, the first in the country specializing in post-acute wound care. 
Support: benefit from malpractice coverage, legal and administrative support, and technical support like our EHR, which is loved for its efficiency & usability.

Today, we are proud to be recognized as a leader in the wound care specialty, an innovator in healthcare technology, and a pioneer in telemedicine.

title: Process Engineer - #2875.08
description_text:
What We Offer:
Our excellent salary and benefits package includes medical, dental, vision, life insurance, short and long-term disability coverage, education reimbursement, 401(k), performance bonuses, and an employee stock program. Employee Resource Groups and Programs offered include the Young Professionals Group, Women at Wade Trim, Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, Professional Development, Leadership Development, Rotation Program, Mentor Program, Sustainability Program, and Wellness Program.

Position Description:

We are looking for a Process Engineer to join our Water Team to improve infrastructure in our Pittsburgh office. The candidate must be a licensed PE with a bachelor's degree in civil or environmental engineering and five to ten years of related experience. The candidate should have experience working with process engineering knowledge and application to water and wastewater treatment facilities. The candidate must also be self-motivated, work well with others, and have excellent writing, organizational, and communication skills. A flexible hybrid-remote work schedule is available after 30 days of employment.
\n

Typical responsibilities include: 
Provide technical planning, biological process and mechanical design and construction support on various water and wastewater treatment and resource recovery projects. projects that may include combined sewer overflow, collection system, pump station, stormwater, hydrologic and hydraulic models and/or other water and wastewater projects
Prepare engineering calculations, run process models (i.e. Excel spreadsheet, Biowin, GPS-X, etc.) and support analysis of alternatives. Utilize effective technical writing skills to prepare technical memoranda to document alternative analyses, basis of design, business case evaluations, P&ID reports etc.
Prepare design reports and construction plans for treatment plants and pump stations using local and state standards
Prepare/assist with specifications and permit submittals
Assist with/review scope, budget, and schedule of new project tasks
Monitor task budget and progress
Document design changes and communications
Conduct on-site inspections and technical guidance for start-up as needed
Prepare project status and other reports
Assist in the preparation of proposals
Attend client, public, project and staff meetings including client progress, plan review, pre-construction, etc.
Possess strong written and verbal communication skills
Maintain excellent client relations
Maintain a safe working environment
Participate in the Florida Water Environment Association, or similar organization as a committee member.
Publish and present papers at state and national conferences. Serve as an active member in the Wade Trim Water and Wastewater Treatment Practice

Education:
Bachelor's degree in Civil or Environmental Engineering

Skills/Experience:
5-10 years of related experience required
Licensed Professional Engineer required
Experience working with large-scale piping, valve, pumping systems, and treatment technologies
Demonstrated knowledge of water and wastewater treatment facilities is a plus
Experience developing detailed design plans for treatment projects is a plus
Expertise in more than one area of conventional activated sludge wastewater treatment liquid and solid processes including fundamentals of nitrification and denitrification systems, chemical or biological phosphorous removal, secondary and tertiary treatment including high rate disinfection systems, solids handling and biosolids treatment is a plus
Experience in assisting with sales efforts for large scale projects is a plus
Strong written and verbal skills

\n
About Wade Trim:

Wade Trim is committed to maximizing the value of infrastructure investments. We’ve been solving complex engineering challenges for a century. We customize our work approach to fit each project using a collaborative, friendly style to deliver solutions our clients can stand behind.

Our supportive culture recognizes and strives to fulfill collective client, company, and individual needs. Mentoring and building skills of Wade Trim staff is a priority. Frequent interaction among staff is encouraged, company leadership is easily accessible, and opportunities are provided for staff to help shape the firm’s future through strategic planning. Work/life balance is supported through a flexible, hybrid work schedule that brings team members together in the office at least three days a week and connects them virtually when working from home.

To solve our clients’ toughest challenges, we’ve devoted ourselves to delivering innovative solutions. Our Office of Applied Technology (OAT) is dedicated to seeking new technologies or ways to apply existing technologies to enhance value to our clients and positively impact communities. All staff are encouraged to share ideas and suggestions for innovative technologies or processes to adopt. This open-minded approach enables us to advance technology, foster innovation, and stay ahead of our clients’ needs.

Wade Trim’s success is shared by the employees that make it happen. Since our beginning, our firm has been 100% employee owned. This cultivates an ownership mindset that benefits our work approach, collaborative culture, and ability to deliver client solutions. We believe employee ownership drives the sustainability and growth of our firm and provides all our employees with opportunities for financial success.

If you are looking for a challenging and rewarding career in a friendly environment, please submit your resume by visiting the Careers section of our website at http://www.wadetrim.com/careers.

Wade Trim does not accept unsolicited resumes, candidate profiles, or CVs from third-party recruiters or employment agencies. Any submission made without a valid, signed agreement and an approved engagement request from Wade Trim’s People Services Team will be considered the property of Wade Trim. Wade Trim reserves the right to pursue and hire any candidate submitted through unsolicited means without any financial obligation to the recruiter or agency. A valid agreement can only be signed by the Director of People Services. Resumes or candidate profiles submitted at the request of a Wade Trim employee who is not authorized by the People Services team do not constitute a valid engagement. Recruiters and agencies must have a current, written agreement authorized by the Director of People Services to be considered an approved vendor.

Wade Trim is an Affirmative Action/Equal Opportunity Employer.

HISTORY AND FUTURE GROUNDED IN INFRASTRUCTURE
Wade Trim is proud of our municipal infrastructure roots and continues to build on this heritage. We are trusted professionals delivering America’s infrastructure solutions. This is our culture, our brand and what our clients depend on. Our disciplines include civil engineering, planning, surveying, landscape architecture and environmental science services.

Our staff’s ability to deliver solutions is supported through a collaborative work environment that focuses on problem solving. Wade Trim’s 19 offices and more than 600 staff members create a network of resources that is shared to meet specific infrastructure challenges. For instance, a traffic engineer in our Taylor office may work on a project with water resources engineers in our Cincinnati office. Mentoring between employees is important. Senior staff often work with younger employees to increase their knowledge and develop strong working relationships with clients. Diversity of expertise among employees also makes our project teams strong and flexible. In addition, we are committed to strengthening diversity in our industry by working with and mentoring minority-owned, women-owned and small businesses on projects.

Wade Trim is consistently ranked in the top 500 design firms, top 200 environmental firms and continues to be nationally recognized for innovative solutions. We are on a quest to grow, using our expertise to provide infrastructure solutions in new areas.

title: Professional Services, Senior Engineer - NiCE CXone
locations_derived: Bogota, Capital District, RAP (Especial) Central, Colombia
description_text:
At TTEC Digital, we coach clients to ensure their employees feel valued, and fully supported, because an amazing customer experience is an employee first process. Our vision is the same, a place where employees know they can thrive.

The NICE CXone Professional Services Senior Engineer is responsible for the overall development, testing and delivery of the NICE CXone software platform. This is a hands-on, highly technical role for resources skilled in both business and technical consulting experience. You will be tasked to implement the NICE CXone software. Technical experience with various Contact Center technologies including NICE CXone, IVR/ACD, VoIP, Email, SMS, and Chat systems will be required. Key responsibilities include all tiers of the technology, including (but not limited to) design and development of our products and features, unit testing, code reviews, resolving defects encountered during the QA cycle, supporting the production environment, and assisting other developers in a team environment utilizing agile or waterfall development processes.


\n

What You'll Be Doing
Recognized as an external and internal thought leader within CX transformation, Process Automation Digital Transformation, and Omnichannel technologies. 
Exercises wide latitude in determining objectives and approaches to engagements.
Influences client leadership to establish overall business relationship and engagement strategy. Is primarily client-focused; however, does have some responsibility for internal functional matters and partner engagements.
Works on issues that impact design/selling success, future concepts, and product offerings. 
Ability to mentor Software Engineers
Work as a member of the TTEC Digital project team to enhance and improve software written in one or more of the following languages: CXone Studio, C#, ASP.NET
Design and present projects to improve current process and overall platform.
Code and test projects.
Deliver high-quality software on time.
Attend meetings and training as required.
Work with system analysis and project management for the successful completion of a project.


What You Bring to the Role
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, IT, or engineering preferred.
5 or more years’ experience in NICE CXone and UC environments.
3 or more years of software development, to include design, custom and common coding (JavaScript, Python, Java, C++), and testing of real time systems.
Experience with enterprise level CCaaS architecture for complex business needs.
Network infrastructure experience.
Experience creating and presenting to executive level stakeholders (technical and non-technical). 
Team Player
Desire to work in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent customer-facing presentation skills with the ability to develop new solution pitches, value propositions, and demo experiences.
Existing knowledge and/or industry certifications for NICE CXone and process automation technologies
Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) software management and domain experience.
NICE CXone and CXone Studio Experience
1-3 years of ASP/.NET experience (C#, VS.Net, XML, Web Services, etc.)
Ability to work US daytime hours

title: Technology Operations Excellence Director
description_text:
About SugarCRM

From the very beginning, SugarCRM had a unique vision: to offer a different kind of customer relationship management (CRM) software. We pioneered a solution that easily adapts to customer needs, and now, more than two decades later, we’re on a mission to help sales teams reach their highest potential. 
Our diverse team around the world shares a passion for helping customers succeed. Together, we’re building a culture that values personal and professional growth—and we’re proud to be recognized as a Great Place to Work. We care about work/life balance and flexibility for our employees, and we're proud of how we show up for our customers every day. 

If you're looking to level up your career and help businesses grow better and faster, you’re in the right place. Learn more about SugarCRM careers and how you can be part of our journey.

Where You Fit In: The Technology Operations Excellence Director sits within the CTO organization and is responsible for driving financial and operational discipline across our technology functions. This role partners closely with Engineering, Cloud Operations, Quality Assurance, and Release & Reliability leaders to improve cost transparency, operational insight, and decision-making—without owning infrastructure, service delivery, or enterprise IT. 
\n

Impact You Will Make in the Role
Lead the FinOps framework for cloud spend, providing visibility into usage, trends, and optimization opportunities across AWS and related services. 
Partner with Cloud Operations and Engineering leaders to identify and drive cost optimization initiatives while preserving performance, reliability, and scalability. 
Establish consistent operational metrics, dashboards, and reporting to improve transparency and decision-making within the CTO organization. 
Provide data-driven insights to support capacity planning, prioritization, and investment tradeoffs across engineering and cloud functions. 
Enable operational excellence by identifying process inefficiencies and supporting continuous improvement initiatives. 
Collaborate with Finance and FP&A by supplying accurate inputs related to cloud and technology operations; FP&A retains ownership of company-wide OpEx reporting and budgeting. 
Communicate complex technical and financial information clearly to technology leadership and executives. 

What You Will Bring 
8+ years of experience in technology operations, FinOps, cloud financial management, or operational excellence roles. 
Strong hands-on experience with AWS and cloud cost management practices. 
Proven ability to partner effectively with senior technical leaders without direct ownership of teams or infrastructure. 
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to derive actionable insights from complex technical and financial data. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience presenting to executive audiences. 
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic, and evolving technology environment. 
Collaborative, structured, and detail-oriented approach to driving operational discipline. 

Preferred Qualifications:
FinOps Certified Practitioner or equivalent experience. 
Experience supporting globally distributed engineering or cloud operations teams. 
Familiarity with DevOps, cloud architecture concepts, and modern delivery pipelines (from an enablement perspective). 
Background in building executive dashboards and operational reporting frameworks. 

\n
$136,000 - $150,000 a year
Expected salary range, depending on experience.
\n
We understand that no candidate is perfectly qualified for any job. Experience comes in different forms; many skills are transferable; and passion goes a long way. Even more important than your resume is a clear demonstration of dedication, impact, and the ability to thrive in a fluid and collaborative environment. We want you to learn new things in this role, and we encourage you to apply if your experience is close to what we’re looking for. We also know that diversity of background and thought makes for better problem solving and more creative thinking, which is why we're dedicated to adding new perspectives to the team.
Benefits and Perks:
Beyond a stellar work environment, friendly people, and inspiring work, we have some sweet benefits and perks:
· Excellent healthcare package for you and your family 
· Savings and Investment – 401(k) match
· Unlimited Paid Time Off
· Paid Parental Leave
· Online Legal Services (Rocket Lawyer)
· Financial Planning Services (Origin)
· Discounted Pet Insurance (Embrace Pet Insurance)
· Corporate Benefit Program (Working Advantage). This benefit offers you exclusive travel and entertainment offers and special discounts that are not available to the general public
· Health and Wellness Reimbursement Program 
· Travel Discounts
· Educational Resources - Career & Personal Development Program
· Employee Referral Bonus Program
· We are a merit-based company - many opportunities to learn, excel and grow your career!

If you require a reasonable accommodation to search for a job opening or submit an application, please call +1 (877) 842-7276 with your request and contact information.

Our company uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit www.dhs.gov/E-Verify.

title: Head of Paid Social
description_text:
Make Your Mark as a Head of Paid Social at Storio group

About the role:
As the Head of Paid Social at Storio, you will lead our vision for paid social and affiliate marketing across all our brands. You will guide a talented international team across Amsterdam, Barcelona, and London to drive growth and reach our customers in meaningful ways. This is a leadership role where you will build a high-performance culture and ensure our social strategy helps people celebrate their best memories.
\n

How you embrace curiosity daily:
Create and lead the paid social strategy for all group brands and websites.
Build strong partnerships with major networks like Meta, TikTok, and Pinterest through strategic business plans.
Partner with our Creative team to brief and develop assets that lead to campaign excellence.
Look for ways to use automation and new marketing technology to improve how we work.
Mentor your team to help every individual grow their skills and reach their goals.
Work closely with Commercial and Analytics teams to find new opportunities for growth.
Stay ahead of platform shifts and new features to keep our brands competitive.

How you make your mark:
Scale our ad spend efficiently to expand our revenue and customer base.
Strengthen the role of Paid Social within our media mix by developing a vision and proving the incremental value of these activities.
Foster a culture of shared insights across the company to drive innovation.
Use advanced targeting and data to connect Storio with new audiences.
Take ownership of forecasting and ensure all campaigns hit their performance.

What you bring to the team:
Significant experience leading paid social marketing campaigns in an eCommerce setting. 
A proven track record of managing multi-million-euro budgets across different regions.
Strong leadership skills with experience managing and developing marketing professionals.
Deep knowledge of the customer journey from brand awareness to conversion.
Excellent analytical skills and a habit of making decisions based on data.
Strong communication skills to build relationships with internal and external partners.
Experience with tools like Smartly and web analytics platforms such as Adobe Analytics.

What sets you apart:
You have experience testing incrementality and understand complex attribution topics.
You are comfortable working with OKR frameworks and managing multiple brands at once.
You hold a degree in marketing, business, or a related field.

\n
About us
At Storio Group, we help people hold onto life's moments. We make personalised photo products that turn fleeting memories into things you can keep, share, and re-live.

Every person at Storio Group helps create our products and shape our company. You will see the impact of your work daily. We invite you to make your mark on our business, products, and customers' lives.

We act with heart by putting people first and valuing diverse perspectives. We give our best and aim for high standards in all we do. We own our work, taking initiative to find solutions. We embrace curiosity, always learning and trying new things. We find the joy in our work and create a positive environment.

Equal Opportunities & Right to Work
Storio Group is an equal opportunity employer, celebrating diversity and fostering an inclusive environment. If you require reasonable adjustments during interviews please contact our HR team. 

Applicants must also have the legal right to work in the position's country without requiring sponsorship.

title: Ultrasound Technologist - Full-time - Texarkana TX
description_text:
Ultrasound Technologist - Full-time - Texarkana TX

Build a Career That Moves With You. At Roshal Health, your skills don’t just fill a role—they make a difference.
Roshal Health was built for clinical professionals who want more than a fixed schedule and a single facility. We connect skilled technologists with on-demand opportunities across leading hospitals and health systems—giving you the freedom to choose when, where, and how you work. Whether you prefer set schedules, supplemental shifts, or expanding your experience across multiple settings by joining our travel team, Roshal offers flexibility without sacrificing stability, support, or pay. Backed by responsive leadership, streamlined onboarding, and a technology-driven approach, you’ll spend less time navigating logistics and more time doing what you do best: delivering exceptional patient care. 
Ultrasound Technologist Requirements
· Minimum 1 year of ultrasound imaging experience
· Current RDMS certification 
· Professionalism and strong interpersonal communication skills
· Self-motivated with the ability to work independently
· Credentialing documentation as required by Roshal, clients, and state regulations
· Up-to-date vaccinations and successful completion of required health screenings (including drug and background checks)
· Current BLS/CPR certification
· Must be able to pass all necessary health requirements for hospital employment, including drug screening and background check.
Responsibilities
· Perform a wide range of ultrasound imaging procedures, including general, OB and vascular studies primarily in an ER environment
· Possess effective communication skills and excellent patient care skills
· Functions proficiently with all technical equipment in diagnostic modality.
· Conduct and document High-Level Disinfection (HLD) procedures with precision
Physical Requirements
· Ability to lift 50–100 lbs., including patients, portable equipment, and positioning tools
· Physical agility to bend, twist, and reposition as needed
· Endurance for prolonged standing, walking, and multiple back-to-back procedures
Benefits
· * Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance (available to Full-time & Part-time W2 employees)
· 401(k) retirement plan

title: VP Sales, Americas
description_text:
Who is Sonar?

Sonar helps prevent code quality and code security issues from reaching production, amplifies developers' productivity in concert with AI assistants, and improves the developer experience with streamlined workflows. Sonar analyzes all code, regardless of who writes it — your internal team, genAI, or third parties — resulting in more secure, reliable, and maintainable applications. Rooted in the open source community, Sonar’s solutions support over 30 programming languages, frameworks, and infrastructure technologies. Today, Sonar is used by +7M developers and 400K organizations worldwide, including the DoD, Microsoft, NASA, MasterCard, Siemens, and T-Mobile.

We believe in developing great products that are supported by great internal teams and a strong culture. We are highly committed to and obsessed with the company, users, each other, and our open source community. We have high standards and hold each other accountable for acting with positivity, dedication, thoughtfulness, empathy, and passion daily. 

We are deliberate with our decisions with high clarity of intention. At the same time, we feel extreme urgency and move forward quickly. 

And lastly, we are highly effective and operationally efficient. We operate collectively as One Team to accomplish our goals.

At Sonar, CODE is more than just an acronym – it's a mindset that defines daily operations. 

Why You Should Apply: 

At Sonar, we’re a group of brilliant, motivated, and driven professionals working hard to help supercharge developers to build better, faster. Sonar helps to continuously improve code quality and code security while reducing developer toil. This means that developers can focus on doing more of what they love and less of what they don’t. Our solutions don’t just solve symptoms of problems – we help fix issues at the source – for all code, whether it's developer-written, AI-generated, or from third parties.

We have a dynamic culture with employees worldwide and hub offices in the USA, Switzerland, the UK, Singapore, and Germany. Team members should be able to come to work every day, work on a product they are proud of, love what they do, and feel energized by their peers. With our roots deep in the open source community, we’re all about the mission: supercharge developers to build better, faster.

The Impact You Will Have:

Sonar is seeking a seasoned and transformational Vice President, Sales, Americas to serve as a key member of the senior management team who can capture the company’s biggest growth opportunity in the Americas region. The Vice President, Americas has the significant responsibility of developing and executing a multi-faceted sales strategy to gain market share as well as build the highest levels of customer satisfaction and loyalty. 
\n

What You Will Do Daily
Scale and maintain a world-class Americas sales organization that sustains efficient and high long-term growth.
Create and drive new strategic go-to-market plans to meet company growth and market share goals through new logo acquisition and current customer expansion. 
Assess the current sales methodologies and team structure to propose/implement necessary adjustments in order to optimize performance and build the infrastructure and incentives to facilitate further aggressive growth.
Maintain strong reporting and accountability against numbers, driving to exceed quotas, while developing and managing motivating incentive tools such as spiffs and stack rankings. 
Act as a strategic thought leader and advisor to the CRO and broader Executive Team; a true business leader and general manager.
Develop short and long-term market strategy and forecasts through market analysis and interpretation of data and communicate sales activities of execution and sales productivity with the senior management teams.
Leverage and develop strong customer relationships, consistently collecting and providing feedback on customer needs, market trends, and opportunities for continuous process, product, and solution improvement. 
Build massive sales capacity and coverage, and develop a data-driven, metrics-centric sales operations function.
Work closely with functions and other executives across the organization to establish and develop a strong and collaborative partnership to ensure continued success.
Recruit, inspire, lead, mentor, and instill operational rigor into the sales organization, to capitalize on the market opportunity for Sonar.
Cultivate loyalty and strong followership within the Austin-based sales team by motivating and energizing the broader Americas Sales organization. Drive success through a hands-on, in-person presence three days a week in the local Austin office.
Inspire the team by clearly communicating Sonar's mission and vision, fostering alignment and engagement across both the sales force and the wider organization.

The Experience You Will Need
Go-to-Market Complexity – Responsibility for a complex, platform/multi-product sales motion across field, inside and channel sales selling into a multi-segment (SMB to Enterprise) customer base. Experience across both net new and account management and can drive engagement cross-functionally within the region.

High Growth at Scale – Track record of leading an organization through rapid growth to $1B and beyond. Develops processes and strategies that enable the organization to scale with the business.

Modern SaaS – Experience within a cloud-native SaaS business, and track record of managing a predictable and repeatable recurring revenue business model. 

Operational Rigor – Experience implementing complex strategy and operational improvements. Takes a data driven and efficiency-oriented approach to sales operations. Measurable success in improving sales enablement, hiring practices, productivity, sales strategy and programs.

\n
We Value Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion:

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures.

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the precise results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

We do not currently support visa candidates in the UK.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Vice President, Sales, Americas will develop and execute a multi-faceted sales strategy to gain market share and build customer satisfaction. They will scale and maintain a world-class sales organization while driving new strategic go-to-market plans.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have experience in managing complex, multi-product sales motions and a track record of leading organizations through rapid growth. A background in cloud-native SaaS businesses and operational improvements is also essential.
linkedin_org_slogan: Trusted by 7M devs, Sonar is committed to enabling developers and organizations to build better code for better software

linkedin_org_description:
Sonar is the trust and verification layer for AI code, and the industry standard for automated code review for 17+ years. 

Sonar delivers deterministic, repeatable, and actionable code verification at scale by integrating code quality and code security into a single platform. The company analyzes more than 750 billion lines of code daily to ensure software is secure, reliable, and maintainable. 

Sonar is rooted in the open source community and is trusted by 7M+ developers globally, including teams at Snowflake, Booking.com, Deutsche Bank, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company. 

To learn more about Sonar, please visit: www.sonar.com

title: Ultrasound Technologist - PRN - Indianapolis IN
description_text:
Ultrasound Technologist - PRN - Indianapolis IN

Build a Career That Moves With You. At Roshal Health, your skills don’t just fill a role—they make a difference.
Roshal Health was built for clinical professionals who want more than a fixed schedule and a single facility. We connect skilled technologists with on-demand opportunities across leading hospitals and health systems—giving you the freedom to choose when, where, and how you work. Whether you prefer set schedules, supplemental shifts, or expanding your experience across multiple settings by joining our travel team, Roshal offers flexibility without sacrificing stability, support, or pay. Backed by responsive leadership, streamlined onboarding, and a technology-driven approach, you’ll spend less time navigating logistics and more time doing what you do best: delivering exceptional patient care.
Ultrasound Technologist Requirements
· Minimum 1 year of experience post externship
· Current RDMS certification 
· Professionalism and strong interpersonal communication skills
· Self-motivated with the ability to work independently
· Credentialing documentation as required by Roshal, clients, and state regulations
· Up-to-date vaccinations and successful completion of required health screenings (including drug and background checks)
· Current BLS/CPR certification
· Must be able to pass all necessary health requirements for hospital employment, including drug screening and background check.
Responsibilities
· Perform a wide range of ultrasound imaging procedures, including general, OB and vascular studies primarily in an ER environment
· Possess effective communication skills and excellent patient care skills
· Functions proficiently with all technical equipment in diagnostic modality.
· Conduct and document High-Level Disinfection (HLD) procedures with precision
Physical Requirements
· Ability to lift 50–100 lbs., including patients, portable equipment, and positioning tools
· Physical agility to bend, twist, and reposition as needed
· Endurance for prolonged standing, walking, and multiple back-to-back procedures
Benefits
· * Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance (available to Full-time & Part-time W2 employees)
· 401(k) retirement plan
· Emphasis on work/life balance
· Optional leadership and management career track
Roshal Imaging Services LLC is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action employer. All qualified individuals will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, age, disability, protected veteran status, marital status, or another protected status under federal, state, or local laws.

title: CT / Xray Technologist - PRN - Tacoma
description_text:
CT / X-Ray Technologist - PRN - Tacoma, WA
Apply to join the Roshal Health Team! 

Roshal Health partners with health systems, hospitals, and healthcare companies to provide on-demand diagnostic services performed by expert healthcare professionals who care. We offer competitive pay and a flexible schedule you control. 

Want to supplement your income with extra shifts while allowing you to keep your current full-time job? That’s exactly what you get as a PRN with Roshal Health! 

Simply provide your schedule of availability and augment your current full-time income via PRN work with Roshal. 
We provide opportunities across multiple healthcare settings.
Job Requirements:
-RT (R),(CT), (ARRT), and BLS certifications.
-Must have the ability to function as a CT Technologist in performing examinations of patients of all ages
-Demonstrates competency in PACS process and performs daily QA process.
-Effective communication skills and excellent patient care skills required. 
-Functions proficiently with all technical equipment in diagnostic modality.
-Properly uses radiation protection and shielding material to ensure compliance with federal and state guidelines. 
-Must be up to date on all vaccinations as required for hospital employment and pass any needed health screenings, including drug screening and background check. 

Job Responsibilities:
-Knowledge of proper use and maintenance of CT equipment. 
-CT examinations and procedures that require the use of contrast agents.
-Ability to manage patients during complex CT procedure exams.
-Able to follow oral and written instructions and communicate effectively with patients, visitors, and employees within the organization.
-Scans in and labels documents appropriately, resolves QA issues promptly, and ensures images are released to radiologists promptly.
-Follows all radiation protection and dose reporting policies and processes. 
-Assist other technologists when necessary to maintain productivity. 
-Assist radiologist with imaging procedures, starting IVs, and injecting contrast, if applicable.

Physical Effort:
·Must be capable of lifting 50-100 lbs, including patient lifting, positioning aids, portable ultrasound machines, and assisting with minor patient movements. Requires physical flexibility to bend, reach, twist, and adjust equipment or patients as needed.
·Requires prolonged standing or walking while performing scans, moving between patients, and positioning equipment. Physical endurance is essential to complete multiple consecutive scans.

Roshal’s CT Technologists are responsible for coordinating and completing daily procedures, including prioritizing the sequence of operations, completing the actual exam, and following through with all the necessary paperwork with minimal supervision, requiring independent judgment.
Roshal Imaging Services LLC is an Equal Opportunity / Affirmative Action employer. All qualified individuals will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, age, disability, protected veteran status, marital status, or another protected status under federal, state, or local laws.
\n

\n

They must follow through with all necessary paperwork with minimal supervision, requiring independent judgment.

Effective communication skills and excellent patient care skills are required, along with proficiency in technical equipment and compliance with radiation protection guidelines.
